vegan_cross_contact.text: 全メニューが魚介類・海産物を中心に構成されており、植物性食材のみの調理は現実的に不可能な環境。もずくたこしゃぶの出汁は魚介ベース。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応に関する情報はウェブサイト・口コミ・グルナビのいずれにも掲載なし。食物アレルギーや食事制限がある場合は事前に電話（052-971-3091）で直接確認することを推奨する。

cultural_comfort.note: Established izakaya catering to local Japanese clientele; no English-language menu or signage found — Japanese fluency or a translation app recommended.

places.name: めん処　幸の家 (Mendokoro Yukino-ya)
places.gf_detail: The core product is wheat noodles (udon and kishimen) and the restaurant has not documented any gluten-free alternatives. Soy sauce, miso, and wheat noodles are fundamental to every dish on the menu. No dedicated fryer or allergen accommodation is mentioned on any source. This is not a safe choice for anyone with celiac disease or wheat allergy.
places.vegan_detail: The menu is built around miso-based noodle broths, pork cutlet (miso katsu), chicken dishes, tempura, and fried items. No plant-based or dashi-free options are documented anywhere. Traditional katsuobushi-based or pork-based dashi is standard in this style of cooking; assume nothing is vegan.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Thu, Sat, Sun & holidays 11:00–14:00, 17:00–21:00 (L.O. 15 min before each close). Closed Fri, also closed the 2nd Saturday of each month.
places.neighborhood: 西区数寄屋町 (Joshin / Nishi Ward)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] The core product is wheat noodles (udon and kishimen) and the restaurant has not documented any gluten-free alternatives. Soy sauce, miso, and wheat noodles are fundamental to every dish on the menu. No dedicated fryer or allergen accommodation is mentioned on any source. This is

chef_bio.specialty: Miso-nikomi kishimen — flat Nagoya-style wheat noodles simmered in a rich, concentrated miso broth served piping hot in an earthenware pot (the shop also serves the more common miso-nikomi udon)

anecdotes.text: The shop's own listing and reviewers describe the proprietor as a warm, motherly 'okaasan' figure with a wonderful smile, whose homey welcome is as much a draw as the food.
A local food blog notes the restaurant's proximity to Nagoya Castle and calls its piping-hot miso-nikomi kishimen an authentic example of the city's flat-noodle tradition.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Hotpepper confirms a warm 'okaasan' proprietor and miso-nikomi as the signature dish (miso-nikomi udon listed at ¥840); oinagoya confirms the shop's location near Nagoya Castle in Sukiya-cho and its rich, piping-hot miso-nikomi kishimen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: All noodle dishes use wheat-based udon or kishimen noodles; the miso broth almost certainly contains wheat-based miso and standard soy sauce. No wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Broth bases are traditional Japanese style; katsuobushi (bonito) or pork-derived dashi is standard in miso-nikomi preparations. No plant-based broth option is documented.

cultural_comfort.note: A traditional neighborhood noodle shop with no English menu or signage; the atmosphere is warm and the proprietor is described as welcoming, but Japanese is the working language.

places.name: そば処 甚家 (Sobadokoro Jinya)
places.gf_detail: Soba is buckwheat-based but standard Japanese soba shops almost universally use wheat-containing soy sauce in their tsuyu dashi broth, and tempura on the menu means wheat flour is present in the kitchen with high cross-contamination risk. No dedicated gluten-free preparation or labeling has been noted in any source. Celiac diners should ask before ordering; safe GF eating here is unlikely without direct confirmation from the kitchen.
places.vegan_detail: The menu centres on domburi bowls (oyakodon, butadon, tendon) and soba served in fish-based broth. Katsuobushi (bonito) dashi is strongly implied by the classic tsuyu style described by reviewers. No plant-based or vegan options were identified.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 11:30–21:30, closed Sun & holidays
places.neighborhood: Around Sengen-chō Stn (Nishi-ku, Shindō, Nagoya)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Soba is buckwheat-based but standard Japanese soba shops almost universally use wheat-containing soy sauce in their tsuyu dashi broth, and tempura on the menu means wheat flour is present in the kitchen with high cross-contamination risk. No dedicated gluten-free preparation or l

chef_bio.specialty: 梅おろしそば (plum daikon soba) and a legendary-value service set — onigiri plus dashimaki tamago for around ¥250 — that regulars order on every visit

anecdotes.text: One reviewer visited 10 times between June 2021 and July 2024, working through much of the menu — tanuki soba, tsukimi soba, umeoroshi soba, ebi-oroshi soba and tororo rice among them.
A calm, simple interior, cash-only counter service and no-reservation neighborhood-soba feel make it a reliable lunch stop for locals near Asamacho Station.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog confirms 6 counter seats plus 2 tables (16 total) near Asamacho Station, a cash-only, no-reservation soba shop with a roughly ¥250 onigiri-and-dashimaki service set and a menu including umeoroshi, tanuki and tsukimi soba; one reviewer logged 10 visits between June 2021 and July 2024.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is on the menu, meaning wheat flour is actively used in the kitchen. No source mentions a dedicated fryer or separate prep area for allergen-sensitive orders.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No source confirms wheat-free soy sauce. Standard Japanese tsuyu (the dipping/broth base for soba) contains wheat-based soy sauce; this shop has given no indication it deviates from that norm.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi is implied by the classic broth flavor described by reviewers; pork and chicken bowls are also served, making a fully plant-based meal extremely unlikely.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling information appears in any reviewed source. With no English menu and a small cash-only operation, allergy communication will require Japanese or a translation aid.

positives.text: Soba noodles are buckwheat-based, which is inherently gluten-free before any broth or coating is applied — a useful starting point for a conversation with staff.

cultural_comfort.note: A small Showa-era retro shop where ordering is done in Japanese with no English menu observed; staff are noted as warm and welcoming to solo diners.

places.name: ザ・カワブン・ナゴヤ (The Kawabun Nagoya)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or dedicated protocols confirmed. Italian courses include pasta, bread, and wheat-based sauces. Kitchen is not reported as celiac-aware. Wheat-containing soy sauce unlikely (Italian cuisine), but cross-contamination from pasta cooking is a real risk. Must inquire directly at reservation — no reliable basis for celiac diners to assume safety.
places.vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. Courses are Italian-style multi-course using animal proteins (meat, seafood, dairy, eggs) as central ingredients. The Kenzo Estate wine partner page notes staff can accommodate guests with food restrictions, but no documented plant-based courses exist. Ask well in advance when booking.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Fri lunch 11:00–15:00 (L.O. 13:30), dinner 17:00–22:00 (L.O. 21:00). Sat/Sun/holidays dinner 18:30–22:00 (L.O. 20:30). Cafe 12:00–16:00 (weekdays). Bar 17:30–23:00 (weekdays) / 20:00–23:00 (Sat/Sun/holidays).
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi / Shiyakusho
places.notes: THE KAWABUN NAGOYA opened in 2008 on the site of Ryotei Kawabun, documented as Nagoya's oldest traditional restaurant, which served the Owari Tokugawa clan as a purveyor during the Edo period. The four-storey building incorporates Taisho-era (1912–1926) architectural elements — c

chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市中区丸の内 — built on the grounds of Ryotei Kawabun, Nagoya's oldest ryotei
chef_bio.background: THE KAWABUN NAGOYA opened in 2008 on the site of Ryotei Kawabun, documented as Nagoya's oldest traditional restaurant, which served the Owari Tokugawa clan as a purveyor during the Edo period. The building incorporates Taisho-era (1912–1926) architectural elements — carved wooden transoms, tiled roofs, and stone water basins carried over from Kawabun's former main hall — alongside a roughly 400-year-old shiitake tree said to have been planted as a landmark when Nagoya Castle was constructed. The venue is operated by Plan Do See Co., Ltd., a Tokyo-based hospitality group.
chef_bio.philosophy: Honor Kawabun's centuries-old hospitality legacy while pursuing modern Italian cuisine through simple cooking methods that let premium seasonal ingredients speak for themselves.
chef_bio.specialty: Seasonal Italian course dining (tasting courses) with ingredients personally sourced by the chef from producers across Japan, served against a backdrop of traditional Japanese garden views and open kitchen theatre.

anecdotes.text: The original Ryotei Kawabun hosted luminaries including Ito Hirobumi and General Nogi Maresuke, and a roughly 400-year-old shiitake tree on the grounds is said to have been planted as a true-south landmark during the construction of Nagoya Castle.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Japanese Wikipedia and clapltd confirm THE KAWABUN NAGOYA opened in 2008 on the site of Ryotei Kawabun (Nagoya's oldest ryotei and an Owari-Tokugawa purveyor), incorporating Taisho-era transoms, tiled roofs and water basins from the old main hall plus a roughly 400-year-old shiitake tree tied to Nagoya Castle's construction; it is operated by Plan Do See, and past guests included Ito Hirobumi and Nogi Maresuke.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Italian courses feature fresh pasta and wheat-based preparations; no separate gluten-free cooking line or dedicated equipment has been documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Italian kitchen is unlikely to rely on wheat-containing soy sauce, but dressings, sauces, and desserts may contain wheat; no ingredient list is publicly available.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Courses are built around meat, seafood, and dairy; shared kitchen surfaces and cookware make vegan cross-contact likely without prior arrangement.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The Kenzo Estate partner listing notes the restaurant can accommodate guests with food restrictions and international guests, but no formal allergy protocol is documented publicly.

positives.text: Course-based dining with advance reservations creates an opportunity to communicate dietary needs directly with the kitchen before arrival.

cultural_comfort.note: Operated by Plan Do See, a major hospitality group; staff experienced with international guests and formal dining etiquette; online reservations available in English via TableCheck.

places.name: みちのり弁当 (Michinori Bento)
places.gf_detail: Tokai region's first dedicated gluten-free bento shop. Wheat, barley, rye, and oats are excluded from all ingredients including seasonings and condiments. Gluten-free soy sauce and miso are sourced specifically. Contamination prevention is structural: no grain products are permitted on premises, all kitchen equipment is newly purchased (not leased), and staff uniforms are washed exclusively on-site. The founder (who has a wheat allergy) designed protocols to address even trace-protein reactions.
places.vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. Standard bento boxes use fish, eggs, and/or meat (grilled mackerel, chicken karaage, chicken nanban, chikuwa). Some vegetable-based sides (kinpira gobo, potato salad, leafy greens) may be plant-based but shared kitchen preparation with animal products. Tabelog notes "vegetarian menu available" — inquire directly for current vegan-safe options.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–19:00 / irregular days off
places.neighborhood: Nishi-ku, Joshin
places.notes: Naco developed a wheat allergy as an adult and experienced first-hand the isolation of being unable to share meals with friends and family. Determined that no one should have to give up the pleasure of eating together, she trained as a gluten-free advisor and founded 株式会社テマトジカン (

chef_bio.background: Naco developed a wheat allergy as an adult and experienced first-hand the isolation of being unable to share meals with friends and family. Determined that no one should have to give up the pleasure of eating together, she trained as a gluten-free advisor and founded 株式会社テマトジカン (Tematojikan Co., Ltd.). みちのり弁当 opened January 27, 2023, in a lovingly repurposed 60-year-old wooden building in the Joshin (浄心) neighborhood — chosen to reduce demolition waste, with reclaimed wood and a transparent glass front that lets passersby see into the kitchen.
chef_bio.philosophy: 「小麦アレルギーの人も、そうでない人も、一緒に食卓を囲み同じ食事で『おいしいね』を分かち合う」— People with and without wheat allergies can sit at the same table and share the joy of 'this is delicious.'
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour karaage bento (米粉唐揚げ) — the shop's signature, chicken fried in a rice-flour coating, prepared wheat-free

anecdotes.text: Rather than lease kitchen equipment that might have contacted gluten, Naco bought every piece of equipment new — a significant upfront cost she absorbed to prioritise contamination prevention from day one.

The shop building itself is a sustainability statement: a 60-year-old wooden structure was renovated instead of demolished, with reclaimed wood used for interior fittings and glass walls installed so the kitchen is fully visible to customers.

みちのり弁当 was billed as the Tokai region's first dedicated gluten-free bento shop when it opened on January 27, 2023 — filling a gap Naco had experienced personally as a wheat-allergy sufferer in Nagoya.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: The atpress/allecolle press release confirms みちのり弁当 as the Tokai region's first gluten-free-specialty bento shop, opened 27 January 2023 in Joshin (Nishi-ku) by wheat-allergic owner naco (Okamura) of Tematojikan Co., in a renovated 60-year-old wooden building fitted with all-new equipment for contamination prevention; the rice-flour karaage is the house specialty. No celiac-safety guarantee is asserted by the shop.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No grain products (wheat, barley, rye, oats) are permitted to enter the premises at all, eliminating the primary cross-contamination vector.
All kitchen equipment was purchased new rather than leased, ensuring no residual gluten from prior use.
Staff uniforms are washed exclusively on-site to prevent outside gluten introduction.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Gluten-free soy sauce and miso are specifically sourced; standard wheat-containing soy sauce and miso are not used in any preparation.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen is not vegan-dedicated. Bento boxes regularly contain fish, chicken, and eggs; plant-based sides are prepared in the same kitchen environment.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The shop advises contacting them directly for allergen accommodation requests beyond gluten: 「食物アレルギー対応につきましてはお問い合わせください」(Please inquire about food allergy accommodations).
Protocols account for even trace-protein quantities sufficient to trigger allergic reactions, reflecting the founder's personal experience with wheat allergy.

positives.text: Tokai region's first dedicated gluten-free bento shop — the entire menu is safe for wheat allergy; no need to ask or modify orders.
The founder is herself a wheat-allergy sufferer and certified gluten-free advisor, bringing lived-experience credibility to every safety decision.

cultural_comfort.note: Listed on Tabelog's English site, accepts major international credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, JCB, AMEX), and offers delivery via Uber Eats and Wolt — accessible for non-Japanese speakers, though phone and in-person reservations are in Japanese.

places.name: WOK (WOK)
places.gf_detail: Sri Lankan cuisine is fundamentally rice-based and tends to contain few gluten-containing ingredients, but whether soy sauce or wheat-containing seasonings are used is unknown. There is no explicit gluten-free accommodation. Papadum (made from lentil flour) is usually GF-compatible, but the risk of shared frying oil cannot be verified. People with celiac disease, etc., should be sure to confirm by phone in advance (070-3810-4231).
places.vegan_detail: A vegetable curry exists on the menu, and Sri Lankan cuisine is inherently rich in bean- and vegetable-based curries. However, there is no information explicitly stating vegan accommodation, and fish, chicken, and pork curries are also served. Individual confirmation is needed before visiting.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sun 10:00–22:00; closed Mon
places.neighborhood: Jōhoku-chō, Nishi Ward (~11 min walk from Shōnaidōri Station)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened June 21, 2026. A place run by a Sri Lankan owner in an inconspicuous back alley (Heart-in Jōhoku-chō 1C) in Jōhoku-chō, Nishi Ward, Nagoya. It is beginning to attract attention for its overwhelming cost performance — about half the going rate — and as "authentic, serious Sri Lankan food" with the real local taste served straight onto the plate.

chef_bio.background: 2026年6月21日オープン。スリランカ出身のオーナーが名古屋市西区城北町の目立たない路地裏（ハートイン城北町1C）で営む一軒。相場の半値ほどという圧倒的なコストパフォーマンスと、本場の味がそのまま皿に上る「ガチなスリランカ料理」として注目を集め始めている。
chef_bio.philosophy: 本場の味をそのままに、手頃な価格で提供することをコンセプトとしている模様。
chef_bio.specialty: フィッシュカレー・チキンカレー・ベジタブルカレーなど本格スリランカカレー、パパド、ファルーダ（デザート）
chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog confirms WOK opened 21 June 2026 in a hard-to-find Nishi-ku Josaicho alley (Heart-in Josaicho 1C), is run by a Sri Lankan owner, and serves fish/chicken/vegetable curries and faluda at roughly half typical market prices.

positives.text: スリランカ料理はライス（米）を主食とし、豆・野菜・スパイスが主体のカレーが多いため、グルテンフリーに親和性がある料理文化といえる。

cultural_comfort.note: スリランカ人オーナーの個人経営店で英語対応の可能性あり。日本語が基本だが、身振りや指差しでの注文も通じると思われる。

places.name: ヴェジタブルカフェ&シーフードバー サイエン (Vegetable Cafe & Seafood Bar Saien)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free labeling or certification found on any platform. Menu includes soy-based dressings (standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat), burgers with buns, grain rice bowls, fried items (soft-shell crab burger, fried shrimp), and dessert shortcakes — all high-risk for gluten. Shared kitchen environment with fried items means cross-contamination risk. No dedicated fryer information available. Those with celiac disease or serious gluten sensitivity should inquire carefully with staff before ordering; safe options are not guaranteed.
places.vegan_detail: The menu is vegetable-forward at lunch with NYC-style chopped salads (romaine, red cabbage, spinach with dressing choices including soy-based and sesame-ginger), but the restaurant's core identity is an omnivore dining bar. Evening focus is on raw oysters, wagyu beef, and seafood. No dishes are labeled vegan. Soy-based dressings are on offer alongside other options. Hidden animal-based ingredients (bonito dashi, oyster sauce, etc.) in cooked items cannot be ruled out — ask staff before ordering.
places.hours_raw: Weekdays & Sat 10:30–22:30 (food L.O. 21:30, drink L.O. 22:00), Sun & holidays 10:30–19:00. Occasional irregular closures.
places.neighborhood: Shiyakusho Stn / Naka-ku, Ninomaru (Kinshachi Yokochō Muneharu Zone, east side of Nagoya Castle)
places.cuisine: Organic vegetables, seafood, oysters, dining bar
places.notes: Saien opened on March 29, 2018, as part of the Soharu Zone of Kinshachi Yokocho, a food-and-culture complex built along the eastern edge of Nagoya Castle. The Soharu Zone was conceived to showcase ambitious, creative restaurateurs bringing fresh ideas to Nagoya, and Saien was one

chef_bio.background: Saien opened on March 29, 2018, as part of the Muneharu (宗春) Zone of Kinshachi Yokocho, a food-and-culture complex built along the eastern edge of Nagoya Castle. The Muneharu Zone was conceived to showcase organic-vegetable-focused cafes and ambitious restaurateurs bringing fresh ideas to Nagoya, and Saien was one of its founding tenants.
chef_bio.philosophy: The restaurant's founding concept centered on bringing New York-style salad culture to Nagoya: self-cultivated organic vegetables paired with daily market-sourced seafood, aiming for cuisine described as 'beautiful, delicious, and body-friendly.' Over time it grew into a broader dining bar with an oyster-and-meat focus in the evenings while keeping the fresh-vegetable-and-seafood identity at lunch.
chef_bio.specialty: Soft-shell crab burger — a whole soft-shell crab tucked between buns — is the venue's most talked-about item and a recurring centerpiece of visitor photos and reviews.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant's 'five-minute shelf-life' seasonal shortcake — a dessert intentionally marketed as perishable to signal ultra-fresh cream — became a social media talking point among Kinshachi Yokocho visitors.
The soft-shell crab burger, priced around ¥980 at the time, features a whole crab visible in the bun and drew consistent praise in visitor blogs as an unusual and photogenic item.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Nagoya tourism and official pages confirm the Muneharu (宗春) Zone of Kinshachi Yokocho opened on 29 March 2018 on the castle's east side with organic-vegetable-focused cafes; Saien is an organic-vegetable and daily-market-seafood cafe/dining bar there, and a food blog confirms its whole soft-shell-crab burger (~¥980) and 'five-minute shelf-life' shortcake.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Fried items (soft-shell crab burger, fried shrimp, miso cutlet) are on the menu and likely share kitchen fryers with other dishes. No dedicated GF fryer information is publicly available.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy-based dressings are offered on salads, and soy sauce is a standard ingredient in Japanese cuisine — standard Japanese soy sauce (醤油) contains wheat. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternatives are mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen prepares raw oysters, wagyu beef, seafood, and fried items alongside vegetable dishes. No separation between vegan and omnivore prep is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling protocol is mentioned on any official or third-party platform. Phone reservations are accepted at 052-212-8553, which would be the best avenue to inquire about specific dietary needs before visiting.

positives.text: The restaurant's concept emphasizes self-cultivated organic vegetables and daily fresh market seafood, suggesting ingredient quality awareness even if formal allergy protocols are not advertised.

cultural_comfort.note: Located inside the tourist-oriented Kinshachi Yokocho complex at Nagoya Castle — an English-friendly environment designed to welcome international visitors, with multilingual signage common in the area.

places.name: 喫茶ニューポピー (Kissa New Poppy)
places.gf_detail: The entire menu is built around wheat: thick-cut toast, spaghetti, and bread rolls are the core offerings. No gluten-free alternatives exist. Soy sauce likely appears in the Napolitan and other savory dishes. No dedicated fryer information available, but there is no indication of fryer use. This venue is not appropriate for celiac diners.
places.vegan_detail: The menu is a classic Nagoya kissaten lineup: toast with red bean paste and butter, teppan spaghetti (Napolitan), curry rice, and morning sets with egg and salad. No vegan-designated dishes exist. Hidden animal products are the norm here — butter in toast, egg in morning sets, likely milk in coffee drinks. Coffee drinks (black) would be the only reliably vegan option. Ask (in Japanese) if a specific item can be modified.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Wed & Sun 8:00–18:00, Fri/Sat 8:00–22:00, closed Thu (may also close the 2nd Wednesday)
places.neighborhood: 那古野・四間道 (Nagono / Shikemichi historic district), Nishi-ku —
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] New Poppy is the second-generation revival of 喫茶ポピー, the coffee house Masashi Bito's parents opened two years before his birth and ran in the Meieki district from 1977 until December 2010, when building deterioration forced its closure after 34 years. Masashi worked alongside his

roles: Owner / roaster — 尾藤雅士 (Masashi Bito)
Floor staff — 尾藤孝子 (Takako Bito), Masashi's mother
chef_bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi — historic Shikemichi (四間道) district, Nishi-ku
chef_bio.background: New Poppy is the second-generation revival of 喫茶ポピー, the coffee house Masashi Bito's parents opened two years before his birth and ran in the Meieki district for roughly three decades until it closed in December 2010. Masashi worked in the shop during its final years, catching the coffee bug. He went on to establish BEANS BITOU, a specialty roasting and wholesale company, and ran 喫茶神戸館 in Fushimi before opening 喫茶ニューポピー in January 2019 in the historic Shikemichi preservation district of Nagono — a neighborhood with deep personal resonance: his great-grandfather once lived near Gojo Bridge there, and his father is buried in the area. His mother Takako (尾藤孝子) works alongside him, making this a literal mother-and-son continuation of a family story.
chef_bio.philosophy: Masashi chose the word 'New' deliberately: to inherit tradition while creating something genuinely new. He wanted a space where people could sit without urgency — his study of kissaten across Japan convinced him that a shop's history is its most irreplaceable asset. He offers 'Story Coffee' (物語珈琲), custom wedding-favor blends built from couples' personal narratives.
chef_bio.specialty: Teppan Ogura Toast (鉄板小倉トースト) — sesame toast piled with sweet red bean (ogura) and ice cream, served on a sizzling iron plate with coffee syrup to pour over. House-roasted Poppy Blend and New Poppy Blend coffees are also a draw.

anecdotes.text: Masashi's great-grandfather once lived near Gojo Bridge in Nagono, and his father is buried in the same neighborhood — connections he describes as feeling 'invited' to bring the family's kissaten back to this exact block of the city.

The original 喫茶ポピー opened two years before Masashi was born; he grew up in and around the shop, then watched his mother run it alone after his father's early death before taking over himself at age 28.

New Poppy's Masu Presso (枡プレッソ) pairs its direct-fire-roasted coffee with a hinoki 枡 (traditional wooden measuring box) from Ohashi Ryoki, an Ogaki masu maker founded in 1950 — launched in 2019, with the masu given to keep.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Nagono and Life Designs interviews confirm Masashi Bito revived his parents' Meieki kissaten 喫茶ポピー (opened two years before his birth; closed December 2010) as 喫茶ニューポピー in January 2019 in the Shikemichi/Nagono district, working with his mother Takako; his great-grandfather lived near Gojo Bridge and his father is buried nearby. Meieki Keizai Shimbun confirms the 2019 枡プレッソ pairing with Ogaki masu maker Ohashi Ryoki (founded 1950), and BEANS BITOU confirms the Poppy Blend / New Poppy Blend coffees.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Wheat is the central ingredient across the entire menu — thick-cut toast, spaghetti, and bread rolls are the core offerings. No source mentions gluten-free soy sauce or wheat-free alternatives.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Morning set includes boiled egg and toast with butter; coffee drinks are served with milk by default. No plant-based milk alternative is mentioned in any source.

cultural_comfort.note: A beloved local kissaten in a historic district — menus are in Japanese with no English translation; staff may have limited English, but the ordering process is simple and the atmosphere is warm and welcoming.

places.name: らーめん 柊 (Ramen Hiiragi)
places.gf_detail: Standard ramen shop: the shoyu (soy sauce) broth contains wheat via conventional soy sauce. Noodles are wheat-based. No tamari or GF alternatives noted anywhere. No allergen menu, no staff allergy-handling protocol found. This place is not safe for celiac or serious wheat-sensitive diners.
places.vegan_detail: Both ramen varieties use a pork-bone and seafood-blended broth; the signature chashu pork is the headline topping. No plant-based broth option exists. Vegan diners have nothing suitable here.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 11:30–14:00 / 18:00–21:00, closed Sun and the 3rd Monday of each month
places.neighborhood: Sengen-chō (Nishi-ku)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened on January 8, 2018 in a quiet residential side street a short walk from Asama-cho Station. The space is styled like a cozy hideout — warm wooden counter, intimate atmosphere — and has built a loyal neighbourhood following since opening.

chef_bio.background: Opened January 8, 2018 on a quiet residential side street about a 5-minute walk from Exit 3 of Asama-cho Station (Tsurumai subway line) in Nishi Ward, taking over a space that had previously been an udon shop. The compact 14-seat room is styled like a cozy hideout with a warm wooden counter, and has built a loyal neighbourhood following.
chef_bio.philosophy: A stripped-back, solo-diner-friendly spot that keeps the menu tight and the quality high, letting guests choose between shio and shoyu.
chef_bio.specialty: Low-temperature slow-cooked chashu pork, praised across reviews as 'melt-in-your-mouth,' offered both as a standard cut and a low-temperature version. Ramen is served in shoyu (soy) and shio (salt) styles.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers consistently single out the chashu as the star of the bowl, with one describing it as 'exquisite, melt-in-your-mouth' — the result of precise low-temperature cooking. The shop even sells extra low-temperature chashu as a paid topping.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: ラーメンWalkerとラーメンDB等が浅間町駅徒歩5分・14席・1/8オープンを確認。低温調理チャーシューを普通のものと使い分ける点も紹介されている。スープの詳細な出汁構成は未確認。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Standard ramen kitchen with no GF protocols noted; wheat noodles cooked in shared water, wheat-based soy sauce used in broth.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Shoyu (soy sauce) variety uses conventional soy sauce which contains wheat. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative mentioned on any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Entire menu is built around pork-bone and seafood broth; no plant-based option exists, so cross-contact is total and unavoidable.

cultural_comfort.note: A tiny 14-seat counter shop with no English signage or staff language noted; ordering is straightforward (point at menu items), but expect a fully Japanese-language environment.

places.name: アンティキ (Antichi)
places.gf_detail: No explicit gluten-free accommodation. The courses include pasta (wheat flour), and tagliatelle and the like appear as standard menu items. There is no mention of soy sauce use, and being Italian-based, soy sauce and miso are presumed to be minimal, but confirmation is needed. For accommodations excluding bread and pasta, it is strongly recommended to consult directly when reserving. People with celiac disease should be cautious, as there is no dedicated kitchen or gluten-free guarantee.
places.vegan_detail: No fully vegan menu is offered. The courses center on seafood and meat, and it is Italian cuisine that makes heavy use of butter, cheese, and heavy cream. It uses abundant pesticide-free vegetables from contract farms, and if requested when reserving, they may consider accommodating (there is a note to "let us know if there are ingredients you dislike"), but there is no information explicitly guaranteeing vegan accommodation.
places.hours_raw: Tue 17:30–20:30; Wed–Fri 11:30–13:30 and 17:30–20:30; Sat, Sun, holidays 11:30–14:00 and 17:30–20:30; closed Mon and the 2nd and 4th Tue
places.neighborhood: Kamitatesugi-chō, Higashi Ward (Shirakabe district)
places.notes: A hideaway Italian restaurant in a renovated old house built in the Meiji era (roughly 130–140 years ago). Located in the Shirakabe district of Higashi Ward, Nagoya, it features a "time-stopped" atmosphere created by antique furnishings and vintage architecture. The owner encountered a style fusing historic architecture with modern cuisine at a restaurant in northern Italy and realized the same concept in Nagoya.

chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市東区上竪杉町（白壁地区）
chef_bio.background: 明治期に建てられた古民家を改装した一軒家イタリアン（各紹介で築100〜140年と幅がある）。名古屋・東区の白壁地区に位置し、アンティークな調度品と年代物の建築が醸し出す「時間が止まったような」空間が特徴。シェフの林氏は1983年愛知県生まれで、フランス留学を経てイタリア料理とフレンチの経験を積み、2015年に当店のシェフに就任した。
chef_bio.philosophy: 支配人・尾関伸二氏のもと、記念日や特別な日の客を丁寧にもてなすことを大切にする。日本ならではの旬の素材にこだわり、シェフ自ら産地に足を運んで素材を厳選。厳選されたイタリアワインを豊富に取り揃える。
chef_bio.specialty: 旬の食材を活かしたイタリアンコース料理。ジビエ（若鳩・山鴫・鶉・キジ・鴨）、三河一色産のハモ、特選和牛などを、イタリアン技法で仕上げる。

anecdotes.text: 建物は明治期築の古民家を改装。改装した内装に組み込まれた圧巻のワインセラーが目の前に広がり、「時間が止まったような」雰囲気と評されている。
二階には個室があり、プロポーズや記念日など特別なシーンでの利用が多いことで知られる。
支配人の尾関伸二氏は、客の様子や好みをくみ取りながら素敵なディナーになるよう最善を尽くすと語り、コース内容の柔軟な対応を示唆している。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイトが1983年愛知県生まれ・フランス留学・2015年シェフ就任の林シェフを明記。nagoya-meshiが築140年の明治期古民家とワインセラーを、WEB大人の名古屋が支配人・尾関伸二氏・記念日利用の二階個室・ジビエ・三河一色産ハモを確認。五島アワビやタイガープロウンは各ソースで未確認のため削除。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテンフリー専用の調理環境・フライヤーについての情報はなし。コースにパスタ（タリアテッレ等）が含まれるため厨房での小麦粉の使用は常態的と考えられる。セリアック病レベルの対応確認は予約時に直接必要。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: メニューに醤油の記載なし。イタリアン主体の調理のため醤油・味噌の使用は限定的と推測されるが、和食材（ハモ、和牛、ウニ等）使用時の調味料については確認が必要。

vegan_cross_contact.text: バター・チーズ・生クリームが標準的に使用されるイタリアンコース構成。動物性食材の排除については予約時のリクエストが必要。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 予約時にアレルギーや苦手な食材をコメント欄で伝えるよう案内されており、スタッフは対応を検討してくれる姿勢がある（「おいしく食べていただけるように工夫します」）。ただし書面によるアレルゲン対応保証の情報はない。

positives.text: 契約農家からの無農薬野菜を多用しており、野菜の質と鮮度へのこだわりは高い。コース内に豊富な野菜料理が含まれる。

cultural_comfort.note: 高級古民家イタリアン。日本語主体の環境だが、コース料理の流れは国際的で、英語対応の可否は不明。予約時に英語でのコミュニケーションは確認が必要。

places.name: 鳥開総本家 金シャチ横丁店 (Torikai Sohonke Kinshachi Yokocho)
places.gf_detail: The restaurant uses standard wheat-containing soy sauce in both the tebasaki wing sauce and the oyakodon tare. The tebasaki is deep-fried (battered/coated) — wheat is present in the breading. The oyakodon dashi contains bonito; the soy-based sauce is not tamari. No gluten-free options, dedicated fryer, or allergen-substitution program are documented anywhere. Celiac-unsafe; avoid.
places.vegan_detail: Entirely a chicken-specialist restaurant. Every dish centres on Nagoya Cochin or domestic chicken. The dashi base is made from Kyushu bonito flakes (katsuo), and the signature oyakodon uses chicken and egg. No plant-based or vegan options are offered or documented anywhere on the official site or third-party sources.
places.hours_raw: 10:30–17:30 (last order 17:00) daily; closed on days Nagoya Castle is closed (irregular)
places.neighborhood: 中区・名古屋城 (Nakaku, Nagoya Castle area — Kinshachi Yokocho, Gis
places.notes: Nishimura Kazunori grew up in the Ise-Shima fishing community with a fisherman father. Despite those coastal roots, it was chicken — specifically the joy of biting into wings at childhood Christmas dinners — that became his culinary north star. In 2000 he opened a small 10-tsubo

chef_bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi — founded at Nagoya Station West Exit 2000; Kinshachi Yokocho branch opened in the Yoshinao (Gishoku) Zone of the castle precinct.
chef_bio.background: Nishimura Kazunori grew up in an Ise-Shima fishing town with a fisherman father. Despite those coastal roots, it was chicken — specifically the joy of biting into wings at childhood Christmas dinners — that became his culinary north star. In 2000 he opened a small ~10-tsubo (about 33 sq m) izakaya called Torikai at Nagoya Station's west exit, serving yakitori, tebasaki and karaage. The turning point came at the 2005 Aichi Expo, where he ran a booth selling both branded Nagoya Cochin and unbranded chicken side by side: despite a price difference of nearly 10x, diners overwhelmingly chose the Nagoya Cochin. That moment convinced him that quality provenance could command loyalty. In 2007 he incorporated as Progre Corp. and rebranded as Torikai Sohonke, pivoting the menu to centre on oyakodon and tebasaki made with Nagoya Cochin. The Kinshachi Yokocho branch, opened inside the tourism precinct at Nagoya Castle's main gate, carries that flagship identity into one of the city's most historically resonant settings.
chef_bio.philosophy: 鳥開総本家's guiding principle is 'a chicken specialist shop that has consistently prioritized authenticity since its founding' (創業当時から本物にこだわり続けた鶏専門店). It sources Nagoya Cochin raised far longer than standard broiler chickens to achieve the breed's characteristic firm texture and depth of flavour. The wing sauce is a proprietary tare replenished gradually in its original pot, so each new batch absorbs the complexity of every batch before it.
chef_bio.specialty: 特撰名古屋コーチン親子丼 (premium Nagoya Cochin oyakodon) and 名古屋コーチン手羽先唐揚げ (Nagoya Cochin tebasaki fried wings)

anecdotes.text: At the 2005 Aichi World Expo, Nishimura sold Nagoya Cochin and unbranded chicken from the same booth. The Nagoya Cochin — priced nearly 10 times higher — outsold the generic product decisively. The experience convinced him to rebuild the brand entirely around premium provenance.

Nishimura traces the oyakodon's slightly sweet, egg-rich flavour profile to a childhood memory: eating sukiyaki leftovers the morning after, stirring the sweet broth into rice and raw egg. That combination became the emotional blueprint for the restaurant's signature dish.

The tebasaki wing sauce is kept in a traditional tare pot that is never fully emptied — new sauce is added to the residue of previous batches, building cumulative depth. The restaurant has won the Japan Karaage Association Grand Prix (tebasaki division) three consecutive years at the highest gold level, and has taken gold at the National Donburi Grand Prix (oyakodon division) six times per its official site.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 金シャチ横丁インタビューが伊勢志摩の漁師の息子・2000年名駅西口10坪の鳥開・2005年愛知万博の名古屋コーチン対決・2007年プログレ設立とリブランド・すき焼きの朝の思い出を確認。公式kodawariページが手羽先唐揚げグランプリ3年連続最高金賞と全国どんぶりグランプリ親子丼部門金賞6度を明記（バイオの『9度』は公式表記の6度に修正）。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tebasaki (fried chicken wings) are coated and deep-fried. No dedicated fryer for any allergen-free preparation is documented. Cross-contamination with wheat from frying is a realistic risk.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The cooking philosophy page confirms the use of 'Central Japan soy sauce marinade' (中日本しょうゆダレ) in the karaage and wing preparations. Standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat; no tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere on the official site or third-party sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is built on chicken and chicken-derived ingredients (egg, bonito dashi). There is no plant-based preparation at this restaurant; vegan cross-contact is unavoidable throughout.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen declaration, staff allergy protocol, or dietary accommodation program is documented on the official site, Tabelog listing, Kinshachi Yokocho operator page, or any third-party source reviewed. Diners with serious allergies should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

cultural_comfort.note: Located inside a purpose-built tourist precinct at Nagoya Castle, this shop is well-accustomed to international visitors; signage and menus are available in multiple languages, and staff at Kinshachi Yokocho venues regularly handle non-Japanese guests.

places.name: 山本屋総本家 金シャチ横丁店 (Yamamotoya Souhonke Kinshachi Yokocho)
places.gf_detail: Noodles are made from 100% domestic wheat flour — wheat is the central ingredient in every dish. The dashi and miso tare both contain soy sauce (wheat). Buckwheat flour is used as dusting powder on the noodles, adding a soba cross-contact risk on top of direct gluten. No gluten-free noodle substitution is offered. This restaurant is not suitable for anyone avoiding gluten.
places.vegan_detail: All udon and kishimen dishes are simmered in a broth made from katsuobushi (bonito), mackerel, sardine, kombu, and shiitake. The miso base blends Kakukyu Hacho miso with white miso and uses soy sauce; no plant-based broth option exists. Side dishes include beef tendon, oden with fish products, and chicken. There is no vegan pathway on this menu.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Wed–Sun 10:30–17:30 (L.O. 17:00); closed Tue
places.neighborhood: Sannomaru, Naka Ward, right by Nagoyajō Station (Kinshachi Yokocho Yoshinao Zone)
places.notes: Yamamotoya Souhonke is a long-established miso-nikomi udon institution representing Nagoya, founded in 1925 (Taishō 14) and marking its 100th anniversary in 2025. It is currently headed by Fukuyuki Machida, who upholds the noren (shop legacy) inherited from his parents.

chef_bio.chef_name: 町田氏（山本屋総本家 代表）
roles: 老舗チェーン（名古屋市内複数店舗）
名古屋めし観光スポット代表格
金シャチ横丁の主要テナント
chef_bio.background: 山本屋総本家は大正14年（1925年）に創業し、2025年に創業100周年を迎えた名古屋を代表する味噌煮込みうどんの老舗。初代・島本万吉夫妻から町田家が受け継ぎ、現在は町田氏が代表を務め暖簾を守る。
chef_bio.philosophy: 「年によって小麦や味噌の出来は変わる。昔の味を守るために、毎年配合やブレンドする素材を調整する」（町田代表）。創業時の味と食べ方をそのまま守り続けることを最優先としている。
chef_bio.specialty: 味噌煮込みうどん：愛知県岡崎産カクキューの八丁味噌を白味噌・生醤油とブレンドしたコクのある味噌だしに、生麺を土鍋のままグツグツと煮込む。蓋を取り皿として使う独自の食べ方も創業当時から変わらない。

anecdotes.text: 麺には「コシ・太さ・鮮度・打ち粉・素材」の五つのこだわりがあり、毎朝製造した生麺のみを使用し、冷蔵保存は行わない。
名古屋城の金シャチにちなんで考案した「金シャチ煮込うどん」（¥2,420）は、エビの天ぷらを金シャチに見立てたこの店舗限定の一品。
麺の打ち粉にそば粉を使用しているため、そばアレルギーの顧客には注意が必要で、成分に関する案内を行っている。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 金シャチ横丁インタビューが1925年創業・2025年100周年・毎年のブレンド調整・五つの麺のこだわり（打ち粉にそば粉）・カクキュー八丁味噌・蓋を取り皿にする食べ方を確認。代表の名字は町田で一致するが、下の名前の表記は資料間で揺れがあるため名字に留めた。そば粉使用はグルテン/アレルゲン上、そばアレルギーの人に要注意。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Noodles are made entirely from wheat flour; wheat is inseparable from the dish. No gluten-free alternative exists.
Buckwheat (soba) flour is used as dusting powder on all udon noodles, creating an additional cross-contact risk for both gluten-sensitive and soba-allergic diners.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The miso tare contains soy sauce (shoyu), which contains wheat. Allergen labeling for Yamamotoya products explicitly lists wheat (小麦) as a declared allergen across noodles, miso blend, and broth components.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Broth is made from katsuobushi (bonito), mackerel, mackerel pike, and sardine. The miso blend contains bonito and mackerel. No plant-only broth option is available.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information found about staff allergy handling procedures at the restaurant. Given the allergen complexity (wheat in noodles and broth, soba dusting powder, multiple fish), diners with serious allergies should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

cultural_comfort.note: Tourist-area location adjacent to Nagoya Castle with international visitors in mind, but menus and staff interaction are primarily in Japanese; some English menus available given the Kinshachi Yokocho tourist setting.

places.name: sourire食堂 (Sourire Shokudo)
places.gf_detail: This is a pasta-focused Italian restaurant; wheat pasta is the core product. No gluten-free pasta option or GF labeling has been identified. Cross-contamination risk is high in a pasta kitchen. Celiacs should not assume safety here and would need to call ahead (052-325-5615) to ask directly.
places.vegan_detail: The menu centers on pasta sets with soup, salad, focaccia, and quiche — all components likely contain dairy or egg. No vegan-specific labeling or menu found. Diner should ask about individual dishes; some oil-based pasta may be adaptable.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Tue, Fri, Sat, Sun 7:30–14:00 / 17:00–21:00, closed Wed and Thu (also closed 1st and 3rd Thursdays)
places.neighborhood: 8 min walk from Jōshin Stn (Nishi-ku, Jōsai)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] sourire食堂 opened in December 2023 at a corner spot in the residential Johsai neighborhood of Nagoya's Nishi Ward, taking over a space that previously housed the izakaya Kushiyoshi. The new kitchen kept alive the pasta that regulars had loved at the former occupant, reframing it w

chef_bio.background: sourire食堂 opened in December 2023 at a corner spot in the residential Johsai neighborhood of Nagoya's Nishi Ward (near Johsai Station on the Tsurumai line), taking over a space that had previously housed the izakaya Kushiyoshi (串よし) and reframing it as a relaxed all-day shokudo.
chef_bio.specialty: Daily-rotating pasta sets — generous portions of seasonal pasta (tomato, oil-based with ingredients like firefly squid) accompanied by salad and soup.

anecdotes.text: A diner who visited early on noted the interior was 'シャレオツやん' (quite chic) and rated the value as '超抜群' (outstanding), saying the pasta came with abundant fresh ingredients at a very reasonable price.
The restaurant shifts character across the day — opening as a morning café from 7:30 AM, serving lunch sets by midday, then operating into the evening in an Italian-bar style.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: oinagoyaが2023年12月オープン・西区城西・旧串よし跡・7:30モーニング〜夜営業・日替わりパスタ（スープ・サラダ付）を確認。旧店の名物パスタを引き継いだという記述は裏付けが取れないため削除。

gf_cross_contamination.text: This is a pasta-specialist kitchen; wheat flour is the primary ingredient in virtually every dish. Cross-contamination risk for celiac diners is high and has not been addressed in any public source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Pasta sets include soup, bread (focaccia), and quiche, all of which likely contain dairy or egg. No plant-based alternatives have been noted in any review or listing.

cultural_comfort.note: Casual neighborhood Italian café in a residential area; menus and signage are in Japanese, but the approachable shokudo format means pointing at daily specials boards works fine.

places.name: 〇〇処 こっこや (Marumaru-dokoro Kokkoya)
places.gf_detail: Gluten is pervasive: all lunch sets feature deep-fried items in wheat breading (karaage, tonkatsu, fry sets), conventional wheat-containing soy sauce is used throughout, and there is no dedicated fryer or GF menu. This is not a safe venue for anyone with celiac disease or serious wheat allergy.
places.vegan_detail: The entire menu revolves around chicken, beef, seafood, and pork. No plant-based dishes are listed, and no vegan accommodation is mentioned on any platform. Dashi and animal stocks are standard in izakaya cooking; this place is no exception.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 11:30–14:00, 17:00–23:00 / closed Sun & holidays
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi / Hisaya-ōdōri
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Gluten is pervasive: all lunch sets feature deep-fried items in wheat breading (karaage, tonkatsu, fry sets), conventional wheat-containing soy sauce is used throughout, and there is no dedicated fryer or GF menu. This is not a safe venue for anyone with celiac disease or serious

chef_bio.specialty: 丸鳥の丸焼き (whole roasted chicken)

anecdotes.text: Reviewers describe the interior as having a deeply nostalgic Showa-era atmosphere — the kind of izakaya that feels like it hasn't changed in decades, drawing salary workers and neighbourhood regulars who treat it as their go-to local spot.
The whole roasted chicken (丸鳥の丸焼き) at ¥1,680 is the crowd-pleaser, and draft beer at ¥390 keeps the cost-performance ratio firmly in regulars' favour.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: ぐるなび等が丸の内・久屋大通の居酒屋で丸鳥の丸焼き（¥1,680）・生ビール（¥390）・昭和レトロな雰囲気を確認。丸鳥の丸焼きコース(6品)も¥3,900。

gf_cross_contamination.text: All lunch sets are deep-fried in wheat breading in a shared kitchen; no information about fryer separation is available.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is a core ingredient across the menu — house tare, miso preparations, and grilled chicken sauces all involve conventional shoyu.

vegan_cross_contact.text: There are no plant-based dishes on the menu; animal proteins are present in every listed item.

cultural_comfort.note: A classic salaryman izakaya with a Showa-era feel — predominantly Japanese-speaking clientele, no English menus, and a lived-in neighbourhood-pub atmosphere where regulars are at home.

places.name: 彩膳中華　杏ズ (Saizen Chuka Anzu)
places.gf_detail: The restaurant advertises gluten-free adaptation (グルテンフリー対応) and a TripAdvisor reviewer reported a six-course vegan gluten-free dinner. However, the signature vegan dandan noodles use egg-free domestic wheat noodles — not gluten-free. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce is specifically confirmed in any source; standard Chinese soy sauce contains wheat. No dedicated GF fryer or preparation area confirmed. Cross-contamination protocols not documented. Celiacs should communicate needs clearly in advance; ask specifically about soy sauce and noodle alternatives before ordering.
places.vegan_detail: Several dishes are marked with a "V" symbol and can be made vegan for an additional ¥200 (e.g., oil-fried chicken with soy meat, yuba fried rice, dandan noodles, mapo tofu, sweet and sour pork with soy meat). The restaurant also accommodates five-allium-free (五葷抜き) Buddhist dietary requests. It is not a fully vegan restaurant — omnivore dishes share the kitchen. Hidden animal products: no dashi or katsuo mentioned in vegan dishes; house-made soy sauce koji and salt koji used, brown sugar for sweetness.
places.neighborhood: 中区 丸の内 (Naka Ward, Marunouchi)
places.notes: Owner-chef Ai Hiroshi holds international certification as a medicinal cooking instructor (国際薬膳調理師) and previously served as head chef at a renowned hotel before opening this intimate 15-seat spot in Nagoya's Marunouchi district. The restaurant is a husband-and-wife operation; th

roles: international medicinal cooking instructor (国際薬膳調理師)
culinary instructor at Nagoya Tsujigakuen Culinary Professional School
chef_bio.background: An intimate, few-seat Chinese restaurant in Nagoya's Marunouchi district run by an owner-chef certified as an international medicinal cooking instructor (国際薬膳調理師) who also lectures at Nagoya Tsujigakuen Culinary Professional School. The kitchen applies traditional Chinese medicinal dietary thinking (中医学/薬膳) to a Chinese-food base.
chef_bio.philosophy: Anzu builds Chinese cuisine on medicinal dietary principles (薬膳), using no chemical seasonings and no refined white sugar. Oils are rice oil and soy oil; sweetness comes from unrefined brown sugar (粗糖); the kitchen makes its own salt koji, soy-sauce koji, and uses hon-mirin and natural salt. It offers vegan, gluten-free, and gogun-free (no five pungent roots) accommodations, aiming to give every diner a full-quality meal rather than a stripped-down substitute. Cooking classes are held five times a year aligned with the 24 solar terms.
chef_bio.specialty: Vegan dandan noodles (ベジ担々麺) and soy-meat sweet-and-sour dishes are signatures, alongside mapo tofu and seasonal medicinal lunch sets.

anecdotes.text: A TripAdvisor reviewer described bringing one vegan and three omnivores and receiving a six-course meal that was simultaneously vegan and gluten-free: 'We all enjoyed this wonderful vegan gluten-free deliciousness! Highly recommend giving them a try!'

A note.com natural-food reviewer described the chef as redesigning dishes so the vegan version stands on its own merits rather than simply removing ingredients — including a plant-based egg made from potato and pumpkin.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイトが国際薬膳調理師・名古屋辻学園調理師専門学校講師・化学調味料/白砂糖不使用・天然塩/麹・二十四節氣の薬膳料理教室(年5回)・席数が少ない点を確認。検索結果が米油/大豆油・粗糖・自家製塩麹/醤油麹・本みりん・ビーガン/グルテンフリー/五葷抜き対応を確認。オーナー名（有井博）は公式・検索いずれでも裏付けが取れず削除。グルテンフリーは対応可の記載であり、セリアック向けの絶対的安全を保証するものではない。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or cross-contamination protocols are documented in any available source. The kitchen prepares wheat-based noodles (the vegan dandan noodles use egg-free domestic wheat noodles) alongside adapted GF dishes.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The kitchen uses homemade soy sauce koji (醤油麹) as a core condiment. No source confirms wheat-free or tamari soy sauce is used. Standard soy sauce in Chinese cooking contains wheat; celiacs must ask explicitly whether a wheat-free alternative is available for their specific dishes.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Not a fully vegan restaurant — meat and seafood dishes are prepared in the same kitchen. No source documents whether dedicated cookware or utensils are used for vegan preparations.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Multiple reviewers confirm staff ask about dietary preferences and allergies at the table, and the owner-chef explains each dish personally — suggesting genuine attentiveness rather than a scripted allergy protocol.

positives.text: No chemical seasonings (MSG) or refined white sugar used anywhere in the kitchen.
Staff proactively ask about dietary restrictions; the chef personally brings dishes to the table and explains every ingredient, enabling real-time dialogue about allergen concerns.
English menu clearly marks vegan options, reducing communication friction for non-Japanese-speaking diners with dietary needs.

cultural_comfort.note: English menus are available with vegan options clearly marked; multiple foreign reviewers confirm the chef proactively explains each dish and staff ask about dietary restrictions at the table.

places.name: 鮨旬美 西川 (Sushi Shunbi Nishikawa)
places.gf_detail: Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is used throughout the nigiri service. Dashi is made from fish bones. No gluten-free protocols or dedicated preparation areas are documented. The restaurant's own policy explicitly turns away guests with severe allergies or broad food restrictions, making it incompatible with celiac requirements.
places.vegan_detail: Pure seafood omakase — every course centres on fish, shellfish, and roe. There is no plant-based alternative and the restaurant explicitly declines reservations from guests with extensive ingredient restrictions.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Tue, Thu–Sun 18:00–01:00 (next day) (L.O. 22:00), lunch Tue, Thu, Sat, Sun 11:30–14:30, closed Wed
places.neighborhood: Near Nagoya Castle / Sengen-chō (Espacio Nagoya Castle 4F)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Chef Nishikawa spent over 20 years honing his craft in Japanese cuisine before opening this restaurant, choosing sushi specifically because the counter format lets him interact directly with each guest — a style he felt matched his personality. The restaurant originally opened in

chef_bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi (originally Shimizu; relocated to Meieki 2018; current Espacio Nagoya Castle 4F location from October 2025)
chef_bio.background: Chef Nishikawa spent over 20 years honing his craft in Japanese cuisine before opening this restaurant, choosing sushi specifically because the counter format lets him interact directly with each guest — a style he felt matched his personality. The restaurant relocated to the Meieki area in 2018, and on October 1, 2025 moved to the fourth floor of the newly opened luxury hotel Espacio Nagoya Castle, where an 8-seat counter looks out on the castle. It was awarded two Michelin stars in the 2019 Aichi/Gifu/Mie special edition.
chef_bio.philosophy: "To provide enjoyable dining experiences — that is our only goal." Nishikawa runs a fully reservation-based counter, sourcing ingredients only for that day's confirmed guests, and applies what he calls a 'golden ratio' of salt and aging time tailored to each fish variety to concentrate umami.
chef_bio.specialty: Kuruma ebi (tiger prawn) from Mikawa Isshiki, each large shrimp yielding two contrasting preparations — one ice-chilled straight after boiling for a snappy texture (氷締め), the other gently steamed to draw out natural sweetness (蒸らし).

anecdotes.text: When offered a television appearance, Nishikawa declined, remarking that if he went on he 'could only give passing grades' (俺が出たら全部合格しか出せないよ) — a wry reflection of standards he felt were too high to broadcast.
Diners at the Espacio counter look directly onto the floodlit silhouette of Nagoya Castle, used as 'borrowed scenery' (借景) so the backdrop is as much a part of the experience as the food.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: otona-no-nagoyaが20年超の経験・完全予約制・魚ごとの塩と熟成の黄金比・カウンター重視を確認。mopecoが三河一色の車海老を氷締め/蒸らしの2種で供する点とTV出演辞退の発言、2019年ミシュラン二つ星を確認。jouhou.nagoyaがエスパシオ名古屋城4F・2025年10月1日開業・8席・名古屋城を借景にするカウンターを確認。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or protocol is documented. In a small 8-seat counter kitchen handling multiple courses simultaneously, cross-contamination from soy sauce and other wheat-containing condiments is essentially certain.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is used in nigiri preparation. No mention of tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternatives in any reviewed source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Every course involves seafood; fish-bone dashi is used for appetiser preparations. There is no plant-based track and no separation of animal products.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant's official policy is to decline reservations from guests with severe allergies or broad ingredient restrictions, citing incompatibility with the omakase format.

cultural_comfort.note: Counter-only omakase with direct chef interaction; the chef is known for being warm and conversational, but the experience is conducted almost entirely in Japanese with no documented English service.

places.name: 名古屋市市政資料館 喫茶室 (Nagoya City Archives Kissashitsu)
places.gf_detail: Signature items are toast and sandwiches made on wheat bread — gluten is central to the menu. No gluten-free alternatives are advertised. Standard kissaten soy sauce is likely wheat-based. No mention of a dedicated fryer (cafe does not serve fried food). Those with celiac disease should be aware that wheat-based bread is the main food offering and cross-contamination is not addressed anywhere in available sources.
places.vegan_detail: Menu is simple kissaten fare: mixed sandwiches (egg, ham, potato salad fillings), ogura toast, and coffee. No dedicated vegan options; most sandwiches contain egg and/or ham. Beverages (drip coffee, lemonade, cream soda) are likely plant-based, but fillings should be verified on arrival. No explicit vegan labeling noted on any source.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sun 9:00–16:45 (L.O. 16:15), closed Mon and the 3rd Thursday of each month (if a holiday, next day or the 4th Thursday), closed Dec 29–Jan 3
places.neighborhood: Higashi-ōte / Shirakabe (Higashi-ku)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] The kissashitsu opened in 2003 inside the Nagoya City Archives building (名古屋市市政資料館), a striking Neo-Baroque brick structure completed in 1922 as the Nagoya Court of Appeals and District Court. The building served judicial functions until 1979, when it was repurposed as a civic ar

chef_bio.background: The kissashitsu operates inside the Nagoya City Archives building (名古屋市市政資料館), a striking Neo-Baroque brick structure completed in 1922 as the Nagoya Court of Appeals and District/Summary Court. The building served judicial functions until 1979, when it was repurposed as a civic archive and designated an Important Cultural Property. The cafe occupies a former courtroom (第貳號法廷) on an upper floor, its retro character preserved largely intact.
chef_bio.philosophy: Unhurried, inexpensive refreshment in a space that doubles as living history — visitors rest among marble staircases, stained-glass windows and the quiet hum of an archive. The cafe makes no effort to modernize; its retro character is the point.
chef_bio.specialty: ミックスサンド (mixed sandwich, ¥600) pairing cucumber, tomato and potato salad, alongside 小倉トースト (ogura/red-bean toast) — a nod to Nagoya's red-bean toast tradition — and hand-drip coffee.

anecdotes.text: The central staircase inside the archives features stained-glass windows tied to justice imagery, and the grand interior is a favourite backdrop — many couples have held wedding photo/ceremony shoots on these stairs, and cosplayers regularly use the corridors.
NHK's 2024 drama 'Tora ni Tsubasa' used the archives building as a filming location, renewing public interest in the kissashitsu and its former-courtroom setting.
Reviewers note the cafe has almost no exterior signage — finding it feels like discovering a neighborhood secret tucked inside a grand civic monument.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: nagoya-meshiが1922年築・1979年まで裁判所・第貳號法廷を利用したカフェ・2024年NHK『虎に翼』ロケ地・ミックスサンド¥600を確認。開業年(2003)と小倉トースト/コーヒーの正確な価格は裏付けが取れずぼかした。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about gluten cross-contamination precautions is available from any source. The kitchen prepares wheat-bread sandwiches and toast as its core menu items, making a gluten-safe environment unlikely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce is listed as an ingredient in menu items. The menu is limited to sandwiches, toast, and beverages, so soy sauce exposure risk is minimal, but no explicit allergen information is provided.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Sandwiches contain egg and ham. No vegan-separated prep area or dedicated utensils are mentioned. Beverages appear plant-based but are not explicitly labeled vegan.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy protocol, or staff allergy-handling practice has been documented in any available source. Phone ahead (052-953-0051) to inquire directly before visiting with a serious allergy.

positives.text: Menu is very short and simple — sandwiches, toast, and drinks — so ingredient questions are straightforward to ask. Staff are consistently described as friendly and approachable.
Entirely non-smoking environment inside a civic building.

cultural_comfort.note: A quiet, old-school kissaten with no English menu; staff are described as friendly, but expect Japanese-only communication.

places.name: キューオーエルコーヒー (Q.O.L. COFFEE)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or allergen information published. The kitchen produces sandwiches on standard wheat bread and baked goods. Black coffee and other straight coffee drinks are naturally GF, but shared equipment and a small kitchen mean cross-contamination risk for any food item. Dashi-maki tamago sandwich uses dashi (broth) whose wheat content is unknown. Patrons with celiac should ask staff specifically about ingredients and preparation; safe to drink black coffee but food options are risky without direct confirmation.
places.vegan_detail: No declared vegan menu. Coffee drinks are plant-milk-adaptable in principle, but no confirmation available. The signature food item is a dashi-seasoned rolled egg sandwich with mayo — neither vegan nor likely GF. Pastries and baked goods contain dairy/eggs. Ask staff about milk alternatives and whether any items can be made without animal products.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Fri 07:45–17:30, Sat/Sun 09:00–17:30, closed Mon (occasional irregular closures)
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi / Shiyakusho (Naka-ku)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Shima Yuya grew up in Nagoya dreaming of running his own kissaten. After a serious run at competitive basketball through high school, he pivoted back to that dream, enrolling in culinary school and then spending years in Italian restaurants before eight years at a respected Nagoy

roles: オーナー・ロースター・バリスタ
chef_bio.background: Shima Yuya (b. 1982) grew up in Nagoya dreaming of running his own kissaten. After competitive basketball through high school, he enrolled in culinary school, worked at around a dozen Italian restaurants, then spent eight years at an established Nagoya coffee shop in Higashi ward. He trained at a Gifu roastery to master roasting, and relocated to Melbourne, Australia — sending out roughly 50 résumés over a month and a half before landing a barista job he held for about four months — before returning to open Q.O.L. COFFEE in June 2017.
chef_bio.philosophy: The name Q.O.L. stands for Quality of Life — the belief that one well-made cup of coffee can meaningfully improve a person's day. Shima sources beans from farms around the world, roasts on a GIESEN W6A machine (used in world roasting competitions), hand-picks for defects, and measures roast colour numerically for consistency. Every cup comes with a card naming the bean and its origin.
chef_bio.specialty: 自家焙煎スペシャルティコーヒー（シングルオリジン・ブレンド）を中心に、サンドイッチなどのフードも提供する。

anecdotes.text: Shima holds a Q-Grader licence — an internationally recognised coffee quality-grading certification held by only around 300 people in Japan — as well as Coffee Meister and Coffee Instructor credentials. He has judged at Cup of Excellence international competitions and at Japan's national hand-drip championship.

On the shop's 7th anniversary in June 2024, Shima released a limited blend called '7th Anniversary Blend FURUMAI' using Colombian and Salvadoran Geisha beans, reflecting on seven years that 'passed in a flash despite various equipment troubles along the way.'
A second location, Q.O.L. COFFEE BREWERS, opened in April 2022 inside Nagoya PARCO's west building, bringing the brand to a younger, commercial-district audience.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: life-designsが名古屋育ち・高校までバスケ・調理師学校・イタリアン多数店・東区の珈琲店で8年・岐阜の焙煎所・メルボルンでのバリスタ経験を確認。qolcoffee公式とonlyroaster等がQグレーダー(日本に約300人)・GIESEN W6A・COE審査員・2017年6月開業・2022年4月PARCO2号店を確認。だし巻き玉子サンドは各ソース未確認のため一般化。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No allergen or cross-contamination information is published. The café bakes pastries and prepares sandwiches on wheat bread in a small two-floor space; shared preparation surfaces are likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The signature dashi-maki tamago sandwich is seasoned with dashi broth; the soy sauce or dashi type (and any wheat content) is not disclosed in any published source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All food items reviewed contain eggs, dairy, or both. No vegan-designated preparation area is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling policy is publicly documented. The café does offer an English menu, which suggests some capacity to communicate with non-Japanese speakers about ingredients.

positives.text: Straight specialty coffee drinks (black coffee, cold brew, flat white without cow's milk) are naturally gluten-free and vegan-adaptable, and represent the core offering of the shop.

cultural_comfort.note: English menu available; staff are accustomed to solo international visitors; relaxed specialty-coffee shop atmosphere with no pressure.

places.name: 久坊 (Kyu-bo)
places.gf_detail: No explicit gluten-free accommodation. Teppanyaki courses standardly use soy-sauce-based tare and wheat-containing seasonings. No dedicated fryer (teppan cooking). Course content can be discussed according to each guest's budget and requests, so allergies, etc., must be inquired about when reserving.
places.vegan_detail: The menu is a fully omnivorous course centered on A5 wagyu and live seafood. No vegan or vegetarian accommodation is stated. There is a commitment to ingredients — the owner even uses home-grown vegetables — but a course excluding animal ingredients cannot be confirmed. There may be room for individual consultation when reserving.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 17:00–23:00 (L.O. 22:00); Sun and holidays 17:00–22:00 (L.O. 21:00). Weekend and holiday lunch is by reservation up to 2 days in advance.
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi, Naka Ward, 1 min from Marunouchi subway station
places.cuisine: Teppanyaki / A5 Japanese black beef / live seafood
places.notes: A hideaway teppanyaki spot quietly set in the business district of Marunouchi, Nagoya. Reached through a dim alley, its counter and private rooms are known to local regulars as a special space for business entertaining and anniversaries. The owner grows his own vegetables and sources according to the guests and budget of the day — a thorough dedication to his craft.

chef_bio.background: 名古屋・丸の内のビジネス街に静かに構える鉄板料理の隠れ家。薄暗い路地を抜けた先にあるカウンターと複数の個室は、接待や記念日のための特別な空間として地元の常連に知られる（丸の内駅から徒歩約1分）。
chef_bio.philosophy: 「最高級の食材で最上級のおもてなしを」。完全予約制を採り、一組一組の好みや予算に合わせて食材を個別調達する。
chef_bio.specialty: A5ランク黒毛和牛の鉄板焼き（サーロイン・フィレ・シャトーブリアン）、活アワビ・伊勢海老。

anecdotes.text: 「隠れ家的」な雰囲気で、薄暗い通路を抜けたU字カウンターが特徴的。レビュアーはオーナーが食事中にスタッフへワインサービスを指導し始めたことをユニークな体験として記録している。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式・ぐるなび・一休が丸の内の完全予約制鉄板焼き・A5黒毛和牛（サーロイン/フィレ/シャトーブリアン）・活アワビ・伊勢海老・個室を確認。オーナーが野菜を栽培しているという記述は裏付けが取れず削除。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 鉄板焼きでは同一の調理面を複数の食材・ソースに使用するため、小麦を含む醤油ベースのソース等との接触リスクがある。グルテンへの配慮に関する記載は公式サイト・予約サイトのいずれにも見当たらない。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 鉄板料理のタレや仕上げには醤油（小麦含有）が一般的に使われる。グルテンフリー醤油（丸大豆醤油等）の使用に関する明示はない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全コースが和牛・魚介を中心に構成されており、同一の鉄板面での交差接触は避けられない構造。ヴィーガン向けの分離調理に関する案内は確認できない。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 公式サイトは「ご予算・ご要望に合わせてコースをご用意」と記載しており、アレルギー等の事前申告は予約時に電話で確認することを推奨。英語対応は未確認。

positives.text: 完全予約制かつ少人数対応の個室あり。食材は予約ごとに個別調達するスタイルで、事前に詳細な要望を伝えやすい環境。

cultural_comfort.note: 完全予約制の高級鉄板焼き店で、英語メニューや英語対応スタッフの情報は確認できず。日本語での予約・来店が前提と思われる。

places.name: 焼鳥 かん田 (Yakitori Kanda)
places.gf_detail: Yakitori tare almost certainly contains wheat-based soy sauce (regular shoyu). No GF accommodations, dedicated fryer, or allergen information published. Course-only format leaves no room for substitutions. Not safe for celiac diners.
places.vegan_detail: Yakitori course built around grilled chicken; no plant-based alternatives noted anywhere. Standard yakitori tare (basting sauce) contains mirin and soy sauce. Not a viable option for vegans.
places.hours_raw: 18:00〜22:00 (reported across multiple aggregators; exact per-day schedule and closed days not publicly confirmed)
places.neighborhood: 東区 泉 (Higashi-ku, Izumi) — nearest subway: 市役所駅 / 東大手駅
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened around early 2018 in a discreet residential building in Nagoya's Higashi-ku Izumi district. The restaurant has no signage, no public phone number, and no official website — access is through returning regulars only.

chef_bio.background: Opened in early 2018 (January 11, 2018) in a discreet residential building in Nagoya's Higashi-ku Izumi district. The restaurant keeps its address and phone number private, has no public website, and is effectively access-by-referral only, with photography and social-media posting discouraged.
chef_bio.philosophy: The owner-chef reportedly devotes virtually all waking hours to the craft, sourcing and preparing ingredients with an intensity that reviewers describe as singular even by yakitori standards.
chef_bio.specialty: Omakase yakitori course featuring premium chicken parts, showcased daily in a wooden box arrangement reminiscent of an osechi tray before grilling begins.

anecdotes.text: A blogger visiting a pre-opening event in late 2017 noted that every element of the course — from appetizers through grilled skewers to palate cleansers — bore the stamp of the chef's personal obsession.

Blogger Kato Chika wrote that the chef presents the day's raw ingredients arranged in a beautiful wooden box before service, and closes each course with handmade chocolate — a detail that stuck with regulars.
One commenter noted the restaurant's owner previously discouraged even naming the place on social media, though that policy softened over time.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログ/HAMONI/autoreserve等が東区泉・2018年1月11日開業・完全予約制で紹介者同伴・住所非公開・写真/SNS投稿禁止・コースのみを確認。木箱の食材披露や手作りチョコはブログ体験記に基づく。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No cross-contamination data available. The kitchen is a tiny owner-operated counter; no allergen protocol has been documented anywhere.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Yakitori tare (the basting glaze applied during grilling) standardly contains wheat-brewed soy sauce. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative has been reported.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Entire menu is animal-based; vegan cross-contact is not a meaningful concept here as no plant-based dishes are offered.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling information published. The course-only, regulars-only format means the kitchen does not adapt to dietary restrictions for new guests.

cultural_comfort.note: Entirely in Japanese; no English spoken; access is limited to returning regulars — first-time visitors cannot make a reservation through any public channel.

places.name: 炭火やきとり ごまめ泉 (Sumibiyakitori Gomame Izumi)
places.gf_detail: Standard yakitori tare (dipping sauce) almost certainly contains wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu). Salt-grilled (shio) skewers are likely wheat-free on the skewer itself, but shared grill and kitchen cross-contamination are unconfirmed. No allergen menu or gluten-free declaration found anywhere. Must ask the kitchen specifically about tare ingredients and shared preparation surfaces before ordering.
places.vegan_detail: Entirely meat-focused yakitori with chicken as the centerpiece (Satsuma Jidori, Tanba Akadori). Seafood items include horse sashimi. No plant-based options noted anywhere. All skewers are animal-derived; there is no known vegan-friendly menu path.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat & holidays 17:30–23:00 (food L.O. 22:30 / drink L.O. 22:30), closed Sun
places.neighborhood: 久屋大通・泉 (Hisaya-odori / Izumi, Higashi Ward)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened February 21, 2025 in the Izumi 1-chome district of Nagoya's Higashi Ward, a 3-minute walk from Kuyamachi Station. The shop occupies the third floor of the Hasegawa Building, offering views over the Nagoya nightscape from its counter and table seats.

chef_bio.background: Opened February 21, 2025 in the Izumi 1-chome district of Nagoya's Higashi Ward, a 3-minute walk from Hisaya-ōdōri Station. The shop occupies the third floor of the Hasegawa Building and seats 32 (8 counter, 24 table), offering views over the Nagoya nightscape from its counter and table seats.
chef_bio.philosophy: Authentic binchotan technique in a refined, non-smoking space that suits both casual evenings and business hospitality.
chef_bio.specialty: Charcoal-grilled yakitori using Wakayama Kishu binchotan charcoal, which generates far-infrared heat that seals in umami. Signature skewers draw from aged thigh meat (jukusei momo) sourced from two premium breeds — Satsuma Jidori and Tanba Akadori — letting diners compare flavour profiles side by side.

anecdotes.text: The counter faces an open kitchen, so guests can watch the master (大将) tend skewers over glowing charcoal — a deliberate design choice that turns grilling into part of the dining experience.
Jukusei momo (aged thigh) is offered in two breed variants on the same visit, a tasting-flight approach unusual for a neighbourhood yakitori-ya.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog and Hot Pepper listings confirm the Feb 21 2025 opening, the Higashi-ku Izumi / Hasegawa Bldg 3F location near Hisaya-ōdōri Station, 32 seats (8 counter, 24 table), Wakayama Kishu binchotan, and jukusei-momo from Satsuma Jidori and Tanba Akadori. Owner not publicly named.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on shared grilling surfaces or kitchen cross-contamination practices. The charcoal grill is almost certainly shared across all skewer types.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tare (yakitori dipping sauce) is standard in Japanese yakitori and typically contains wheat-based soy sauce. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative has been mentioned by any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All cooking surfaces are used exclusively for meat and seafood; there is no plant-based section and no vegan cross-contact protocol documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy declaration, or staff allergy-handling procedure has been documented by any source checked. Phone inquiry (052-212-5595) before visiting is strongly advised for any dietary restriction.

positives.text: Shio (salt) grilled skewers avoid tare entirely and may be the safest ordering path for wheat-avoiders, though shared-grill cross-contamination remains unconfirmed.

cultural_comfort.note: A stylish but neighborhood-local izakaya; menus and staff communication will be in Japanese, though the modern setting and card-payment system make it navigable with basic Japanese or a translation app.

places.name: オステリア オルカドーロ (Osteria Orca d'Oro)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free menu, dedicated fryer, or allergen protocol is documented anywhere. Fresh handmade wheat pasta is a signature, and bread (focaccia) is served routinely. Cross-contamination from flour is a realistic concern in this kitchen. Guests with celiac disease should contact the restaurant directly before visiting; safe options are not assured without custom arrangement.
places.vegan_detail: No vegan menu or explicit accommodation listed. The kitchen works à la carte with daily market seafood, meat, and handmade wheat pasta. The chef is noted for adapting to guest requests, so vegetable-only plates may be possible if arranged in advance, but hidden animal-derived ingredients (fish stock, butter, bonito dashi) are standard in Italian cooking here and cannot be guaranteed absent without direct inquiry.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sun lunch 11:30–13:30, dinner 17:30–22:00. Closed Mon (if Mon is a holiday, closed the following Tue).
places.neighborhood: Nishi-ku, Hananoki (3 min walk from Jōshin Stn)
places.notes: Born in Shizuoka Prefecture in January 1976. Yokoyama is an Italian cuisine specialist who opened Osteria Orca d'Oro in Nagoya's Nishi Ward, operating it as owner-chef.

chef_bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi (opened as an independent osteria in Nishi Ward)
chef_bio.background: Born in Shizuoka Prefecture in January 1976. Yokoyama is an Italian cuisine specialist with about 30 years in the trade who opened Osteria Orca d'Oro in the Joshin (浄心) area of Nagoya's Nishi Ward, operating it as owner-chef.
chef_bio.philosophy: Yokoyama centres the menu on ingredients rather than a fixed list: he sources fresh seafood and vegetables from Yanagibashi Market and builds the day's dishes around what is finest. He describes 'deliciousness as the foundation' while aiming for visual beauty in every plate, and says he is grateful to each guest who chooses his restaurant from among the many options in the city.
chef_bio.specialty: Seasonal à la carte Italian built around Yanagibashi Market ingredients: house-made fresh pasta (tagliatelle, pappardelle-style, tagliolini) and acqua pazza (whole fish such as kinmedai simmered in a light broth). The menu changes daily based on the chef's market selection, and the cellar holds more than 200 Italian wines.

anecdotes.text: Fresh seafood and vegetables come from Yanagibashi Market, and the chalkboard specials change daily as a result.
The restaurant offers more than 200 Italian wines, positioning itself as a wine destination as much as a food destination in the Joshin area of Nishi Ward.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Hitosara's chef profile confirms Yokoyama Tenpei (b. Jan 1976, Shizuoka), ~30 years in Italian cuisine, Nishi-ku owner-chef, and his gratitude / 'deliciousness-first, visual-beauty' philosophy. Tabelog confirms Yanagibashi Market sourcing, kinmedai acqua pazza, house-made pasta and 200+ Italian wines; nagoya-meshi confirms the Joshin location and daily-changing menu.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Fresh handmade wheat pasta is a core menu item and flour is in active use throughout service; cross-contamination cannot be ruled out without direct confirmation from the kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce-based preparations are documented for this Italian kitchen, but standard wheat-containing Italian ingredients (pasta dough, bread, flour-dusted proteins) are present throughout the menu.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Butter, cream, fish stock, and cured meats are standard components of an Italian osteria kitchen; vegan cross-contact from shared surfaces and cookware is expected unless the kitchen is asked to accommodate a specific order separately.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen protocol, allergy menu, or staff allergy-handling procedure has been documented in any public source reviewed. The restaurant does note it will try to accommodate guest requests, but this is framed around taste preferences, not food safety.

positives.text: Chef adapts dishes to guest preferences on request — guests with dietary needs who call ahead may be able to arrange a custom plate, as the menu is inherently flexible and not bound to a printed list.

cultural_comfort.note: Owner-operated neighbourhood osteria near Joshin Station; no English menu or signage noted, and ordering centres on a chalkboard of daily specials — a working knowledge of Japanese is helpful.

places.name: フジヤマ55 金シャチ横丁店 (Fujiyama 55 Kinshachi Yokocho)
places.gf_detail: Noodles are house-made daily from Aichi-brand wheat flour (きぬあかり/ゆめあかり) plus kansui and potato starch — wheat is the primary ingredient. Broths contain fish-based dashi (bonito, mackerel) which typically include soy sauce with wheat. No gluten-free noodle substitute or GF accommodation is listed anywhere. Celiac diners should avoid.
