cultural_comfort.note: Part of a luxury international hotel (Espacio Nagoya Castle, member of The Leading Hotels of the World) with English-language website, international-facing booking platforms, and staff accustomed to foreign guests.

places.name: 鹿部戸 (Kabuto)
places.gf_detail: No official gluten-free information. The menu is confirmed to include fu (wheat gluten) (as in a 'simmered dish of fu, bamboo shoots, rape blossoms and taro'), and because French techniques are incorporated, wheat use is highly likely. Soy sauce is presumed to be regular, almost certainly wheat-containing. As this is an omakase course, allergy accommodation requires advance consultation. Not safe for celiacs.
places.vegan_detail: Omakase course only — a French-kaiseki menu using seafood, meat, eggs and dairy. No mention of vegan accommodation. The dashi is presumed to be seafood-based. As a fixed, non-customizable course, no substitutions can be expected. Not suitable for vegans.
places.neighborhood: Nagono, Nishi Ward (Shikemichi)
places.notes: The owner-chef trained for over 20 years in French kitchens. He then opened his restaurant amid the old townscape of Shikemichi, one of Nagoya's historic districts, and for more than 23 years has served original Japanese cuisine that fuses French technique with the spirit of kaiseki.

chef_bio.chef_name: 鹿部戸純生（かぶとじゅんき）
chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市西区那古野（四間道）。江戸・明治期の蔵と町家が連なる歴史的景観保存地区の一角に、古民家を再生した空間で営む。
chef_bio.background: オーナーシェフは20年以上フランス料理の現場で修業。 その後、名古屋の歴史地区・四間道(しけみち)の古い町並みの中に古民家を改築して店を構え、フランス料理の技法と懐石の精神を融合させた独創的な日本料理を長年にわたり提供し続けている。
chef_bio.philosophy: 「完全予約制おまかせコースのみ」という姿勢を貫き、季節の食材を丁寧に扱い、器やしつらえにも心を配る。
chef_bio.specialty: 季節の食材を活かしたフランス懐石おまかせコース。 烏賊を細麺状に仕立てた『烏賊のそうめん作り』、伊勢海老のグラタン、鯛茶漬けなど、フランスと和食の境界を軽やかに越える料理が印象的。

anecdotes.text: 亡き父が12〜13年前に通っていた店に子が初めて訪れて感慨にふけった、というレビューが残り、長年の常連を持つ地元の名店であることが窺える。
ナプキンが四間道の風情に合わせて用意されるなど、細やかなホスピタリティも話題に上る。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイトと食べログによると、オーナーシェフ(鹿部戸)は20年以上フランス料理を修業し、名古屋・四間道の古民家を改築した空間でフランス料理と懐石を融合した完全予約制のおまかせコースを提供。烏賊のそうめん作り、伊勢海老のグラタン、鯛茶漬けなどが名物。総席数24席で4〜20名の個室あり。ナプキンなど細やかなもてなしも評される。

gf_cross_contamination.text: フランス懐石のおまかせコースにつき、小麦（フランス料理の技法に由来するソースや揚げ衣）のクロスコンタミネーションリスクは排除できない。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 醤油の種類（小麦含有の有無）について公式情報なし。一般的な日本料理で使用される醤油には小麦が含まれる。

vegan_cross_contact.text: コース全体を通じて魚介・肉・卵・乳製品が使用されており、ヴィーガン向けクロスコンタクト回避は困難と推定される。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応について公式ウェブサイト・予約サイト等に明示的な記載なし。完全予約制のため、予約時に直接問い合わせることを推奨。

cultural_comfort.note: 完全予約制のおまかせ懐石で、予約・来店ともに日本語対応が基本。英語メニューや英語スタッフの情報なし。

places.name: からめ亭 丸の内店 (Karametei Marunouchi)
places.gf_detail: The core dish is wheat-based spaghetti and the sauce almost certainly contains wheat via soy sauce used in seasoning. The pasta itself is standard wheat spaghetti. No gluten-free noodle alternative is offered. No allergen accommodation is mentioned anywhere. Cross-contamination risk is high in an active pasta kitchen. Not suitable for celiac or wheat-allergic diners.
places.vegan_detail: The signature ankake sauce is simmered with beef, onions, carrots, potatoes, and tomatoes over two days. All dishes contain meat-based sauce. No plant-based or meat-free option is available; vegan diners cannot be accommodated.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Fri, day before holidays & day after holidays 11:00–19:00 (L.O. 18:30), Sat & holidays 11:00–15:00 (L.O. 14:30), closed Sun. Note: sources differ on hours (some list a 20:30 close). Confirm before visiting.
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi / 3 min walk from Marunouchi Stn
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Founded in 1979 in Nagoya's Chikusa ward as ソール本山 (Sole Motoyama), the restaurant later relocated and rebranded as からめ亭. Founder Shichi Shoji — who came to cooking with no formal culinary training — developed the recipe from scratch, refining it over roughly two years before it r

chef_bio.background: Founded in 1979 in Nagoya's Chikusa ward (Motoyama) as あんかけパスタ専門店ソール本山 (Ankake Pasta specialty shop Sole Motoyama), the restaurant later expanded and became known as からめ亭. Founder Shichi Shoji (志智均) developed the recipe from scratch, refining it over roughly two years before it reached its definitive form.
chef_bio.philosophy: Shoji coined the term 'あんかけスパゲッティ' after Nagoya's own あんかけうどん, giving a common name to a dish that already existed in the city's cafés. He has continued to develop new toppings and regional variations over four decades.
chef_bio.specialty: あんかけスパゲッティ — thick-cut spaghetti pan-fried, topped with a peppery brown gravy long-simmered from beef, onion, tomato, carrot, and potato and thickened with starch. The dish leans heavily on black pepper for heat.

anecdotes.text: Shoji has explained that he named the dish after Nagoya's ankake udon, which his sauce resembled; the borrowed name spread and is now the standard term for the dish city-wide.

In 2010 からめ亭 attempted a national expansion by opening in Chiba Prefecture, but the dish struggled outside Nagoya. Shoji later developed a milder 'Kansai Mild' version using kombu dashi to bridge the flavor gap.
The restaurant describes itself as the namesake of ankake spaghetti and counts itself among the genre's 'BIG3' founding shops, alongside Yokoi and So-re.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 回転portal・東海テレビ・つがなちゃん(anspa)によると、1979年に千種区本山で『あんかけパスタ専門店ソール本山』として開業し、のちにからめ亭へ。創業者・志智均が2年ほどかけて味を完成させ、『あんかけうどん』にちなんで『あんかけスパゲッティ』と命名し名称が広まった。2010年に千葉へ進出するも苦戦し、昆布ダシの『関西マイルド』を開発。ヨコイ・そ〜れと並ぶあんかけスパのBIG3を自称。ソースはトマトベースで胡椒が強い。

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen handles wheat pasta exclusively — this is an ankake spaghetti specialist. No gluten-free preparation zone or cross-contamination controls are documented anywhere.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Ankake sauce recipes for this style typically include soy sauce as a seasoning component; no wheat-free shoyu alternative is mentioned. Assume standard wheat-containing soy sauce.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All dishes are built on a beef-simmered sauce base; no vegan-safe preparation path exists on the menu.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy or allergen menu is documented at this restaurant. Contacting the restaurant directly (052-212-3537) before visiting is strongly advised for any dietary restriction.

cultural_comfort.note: A no-frills business-district lunch counter; menu is in Japanese only, staff likely speak minimal English, but ordering is straightforward — point at the menu board or say the dish name.

places.name: 鴨料理 風月 (Kamo Ryori Fugetsu)
places.gf_detail: No stated gluten-free accommodation. The duck tataki is noted to use Mie-prefecture soy sauce (regular soy sauce, highly likely to contain wheat). The warishita and sauces of the hotpot courses are also highly likely to use ordinary soy sauce. No mention of a dedicated fryer. Those with wheat allergy must confirm in advance when booking. Not safe for celiacs.
places.vegan_detail: As a specialist duck restaurant, every course uses duck (Kawachi duck). The dashi is presumed to be duck-based, so a plant-only meal cannot be accommodated. No mention whatsoever of a vegan alternative menu. Not suitable for vegans.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 17:30–22:30 (food L.O. 21:30, drinks L.O. 22:00), closed Sun and public holidays
places.neighborhood: Nagono, Nishi Ward (Shikemichi) / 4 min walk from Kokusai Center Stn
places.notes: One outlet of a Japanese-cuisine group run for 50 years by Miyaji LLP (1-18-10 Nagono, Nishi Ward, Nagoya). Under the concept of 'creating a new value in Japanese cuisine,' the group operates five establishments in the Nagono area, covering yakitori, duck cuisine, four-seasons cuisine and more. Kamo Ryori Fugetsu occupies a renovated traditional Japanese house and offers fully private rooms throughout, overlooking a courtyard garden.

roles: 完全個室・鴨料理専門店
chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市西区那古野（四間道エリア）
chef_bio.background: 合資会社宮治（名古屋市西区那古野1-18-10）が50年にわたり営む和食グループの一店舗。 グループは『新しい和食の価値観のクリエイト』をコンセプトに、四季の蔵 右近・やきとり 風月・鴨料理 風月・焼鳥ごりょうなど那古野／四間道エリアに5軒を展開する。 鴨料理 風月は伝統的な日本家屋を活かした空間に入り、坪庭の見える全席完全個室を備える。
chef_bio.philosophy: 「旨みたっぷりの赤身としつこくない脂で胃にもたれない」と店が説く厳選の河内鴨を用い、接待や記念日に使える完全個室で心のこもったおもてなしを届けることを掲げる。
chef_bio.specialty: 河内鴨のタタキ（店の名物として炭火で軽く焼き上げた河内鴨の一品）を看板に、河内鴨を用いた鴨しゃぶ・鴨すき・鴨陶板焼きなど鴨づくしの料理を供する。

anecdotes.text: 鴨料理 風月はやきとり 風月と同じ宮治グループが那古野／四間道エリアに展開する姉妹店で、『風月』の名を共有する。日本では古くから美味なもののたとえに『鴨の味』という言葉が使われるほど、鴨は食美として尊ばれてきた。
店側は河内鴨について、脂の融点が低く（20℃以下で溶けるとされる）不飽和脂肪酸を多く含み、胃にもたれにくいと説明する。河内鴨は大阪発祥の希少なブランド鴨として知られる。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 宮治グループ公式（miyaji-grp.net）で会社所在地・50年・5店舗・コンセプトを確認。ぐるなびで『河内鴨のタタキ＝風月名物』と全席完全個室、鴨しゃぶ等のメニューを確認。店名『風月』の由来は公表資料に説明なし。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテン交差汚染についての公開情報なし。コース料理の厨房内での小麦使用状況は不明。予約時に直接確認が必要。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 鴨のタタキには「三重県産醤油」を使用と記載あり。使用する醤油が小麦を含む通常醤油である可能性が高く、グルテン過敏症・セリアック病の方には注意が必要。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 全コースに鴨肉・鴨だしが使われる鴨専門店のため、動物性素材の排除は構造上不可能。ヴィーガンの交差汚染対応の記載も一切なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 英語・中国語・韓国語対応スタッフありと記載されており、アレルギーの事前相談は電話予約時（052-485-7808）に行うのが現実的。ただしアレルギー対応ポリシーの明文規定は公開されていない。

positives.text: 全席完全個室のため、他のテーブルからの食材飛散リスクは低い。個室ならではの落ち着いた環境でアレルギー情報を事前にスタッフへ伝えやすい。

cultural_comfort.note: 英語・中国語（簡体・繁体）・韓国語メニューあり、英語対応スタッフ常駐と明記されている。

places.name: ロカンダ オカーダ (Locanda Okada)
places.gf_detail: The menu is built around wheat-based handmade pasta (pappardelle, tagliatelle, ravioli, lasagna, pici, cappelletti, spaghetti) and dishes such as quiche and crostini — wheat is pervasive and central to the kitchen. No gluten-free pasta or dedicated GF options are listed. The restaurant states it can accommodate food allergies upon request, but there is no evidence of celiac-safe protocols, a dedicated fryer, or GF alternatives. Wheat-containing soy sauce use is not documented but standard Italian cooking at this level rarely relies on shoyu; the main celiac risk is the pasta itself and cross-contamination in a small kitchen producing pasta daily.
places.vegan_detail: The kitchen is strongly oriented toward meat and seafood — venison, wild boar, horse tartare, tripe, octopus, tuna, sardines. A small number of dishes are plant-forward on paper (seasonal vegetable salad ¥1,100, marinated carrots ¥650, marinated olives ¥650, pickles ¥650, minestrone ¥1,400, eggplant-tomato spaghetti ¥1,650), but the pasta contains eggs and the kitchen has no stated vegan protocol. Hidden animal products (butter, dashi, lard in pasta dough) are likely. Vegan diners should call ahead and ask specifically; do not assume any dish is vegan without confirming.
places.hours_raw: Tue 18:00–22:00 (L.O. food 21:00 / drink 21:30), Wed–Sun 12:00–15:00 (L.O. 14:00), 18:00–22:00 (L.O. food 21:00 / drink 21:30), closed Mon and the 1st Tuesday of each month. No lunch on Tuesdays.
places.neighborhood: Sengen-chō (Nishi-ku) / 2 min walk from Sengen-chō Stn
places.notes: Born in Gifu Prefecture in 1982, Okada graduated from the Tsuji Culinary Professional School in Osaka and spent roughly a decade building his Italian craft — beginning at an Italian restaurant in Kobe, then moving to Taberna Visconti in Nagoya. He later staged in Italy's Veneto a

chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市西区浅間町（浅間町駅至近、2019年2月開業）
chef_bio.background: Born in Gifu Prefecture in 1982, Okada graduated from the Tsuji Culinary Professional School in Osaka and spent roughly a decade building his Italian craft — beginning at an Italian restaurant in Kobe, then moving to Taberna Visconti in Nagoya as head chef. He later staged in Italy's Veneto and Marche regions and served as manager and head chef at THE CUPS café in Nagoya before opening Locanda Okada in February 2019.
chef_bio.philosophy: 本物の味をカジュアルに楽しめる、温かで優しい店づくり — bringing genuinely Italian flavours to a relaxed, neighbourhood table without the formality of fine dining.
chef_bio.specialty: Gifu-sourced game meat — venison and wild boar from Gero and Gujo — broken down in-house and worked into handmade pastas, terrines, and roasts; squid ink spaghetti is a crowd favourite alongside venison cappelletti and pappardelle al ragù di cervo.

anecdotes.text: Okada personally sources venison and wild boar from two hunting areas in Gifu — Gero and Gujo — and butchers the animals in-house, a level of commitment to ジビエ unusual even among Italian restaurants in Japan.

The dining room is described as a stylish designer space with a hand-painted mural, giving the intimate room — a counter plus small private rooms — an almost Florentine trattoria feel tucked into a quiet Nagoya side street.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: ヒトサラのシェフ紹介（person.html）で1982年岐阜生まれ、辻調卒、神戸→名古屋タベルナ・ヴィスコンティ、ヴェネト／マルケ研修、THE CUPS、2019年2月開業を確認。店舗ページでジビエ（下呂・郡上）自家解体、イカスミスパゲッティ・鹿カッペレッティ・鹿ラグーのパッパルデッレ、手描き壁画のデザイナーズ空間を確認。座席数は特定できず。

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen produces handmade wheat pasta daily; flour is an active, constant presence. No information about separation of gluten-containing prep areas or utensils is available. Cross-contamination risk for celiac diners must be considered high in the absence of contrary evidence.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce (shoyu) use is mentioned or documented in any source — Italian technique is the foundation. However, the kitchen's allergen handling protocols are not disclosed publicly; anyone with a wheat allergy should ask staff directly.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Pasta dough almost certainly contains eggs; the kitchen handles meat and seafood throughout service. No vegan-specific preparation protocols have been disclosed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Tabelog and Hitosara both note 'food allergy accommodations available' (アレルギー対応可), suggesting staff are accustomed to allergy enquiries. The extent of those accommodations — substitution, avoidance, or full protocol — is not documented. Calling ahead (052-887-5579) is strongly recommended.

positives.text: The restaurant explicitly lists food allergy accommodation as a feature, indicating a baseline willingness to discuss dietary needs with guests.

cultural_comfort.note: Small neighbourhood Italian with Japanese-language menus and staff; no English signage noted, though the atmosphere is casual and welcoming enough that basic pointing/gesturing will get you through.

places.name: 清平 (Kiyohira)
places.gf_detail: All noodles (udon, kishimen, soba, ramen-style) contain wheat. Standard Japanese soy sauce (koikuchi) contains wheat. Tempura is battered in wheat flour. No gluten-free noodle alternatives mentioned anywhere. Cross-contamination is certain given multiple wheat-based noodles produced in-house during breaks. Not suitable for celiac or wheat-allergy diners.
places.vegan_detail: Dashi is made with Makurazaki katsuobushi (bonito flakes) and Kagoshima kombu. Curry udon broth is chicken-bone based and contains pork. No plant-based broth options known. Not suitable for vegans or vegetarians avoiding fish stock.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun & holidays 11:00–14:30 (L.O. 14:20), 17:00–20:30 (L.O. 20:20). Lunch closes once sold out. Closed Wed.
places.neighborhood: 西区上名古屋 (Nishi Ward, Kamimagoya), 浄心駅徒歩8分
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Founded in 1972 (Showa 47) in the Kamimagoya neighborhood of Nagoya's Nishi Ward, Kiyohira has been feeding local families for over 50 years. The current proprietor, Oshio Kiyotaka, took over as second-generation owner in his early twenties after training under his father. He now

chef_bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture (西区上名古屋, 1972)
chef_bio.background: Founded in 1972 (Showa 47) in the Kamimagoya neighborhood of Nagoya's Nishi Ward, Kiyohira has been feeding local families for over 50 years. The current proprietor, Oshio Kiyotaka, took over as second-generation owner at age 23 after training under his father. He runs the restaurant alongside his mother (大女将) and wife Kimiko (貴美子).
chef_bio.philosophy: The founder built the shop on the principle of saying yes to customers, and the second generation has carried it forward. Over five decades the menu grew from a simple udon shop to more than 30 dishes — including fried cutlet, omelette rice, and fried rice — because the owner honours nearly every request, true to his father's legacy.
chef_bio.specialty: Curry udon — a rich curry broth ladled over hand-made udon; customers are given a cloth apron when they order it. The katsu curry udon (カツカレーうどん), topped with a breaded pork cutlet over freshly made noodles, is the house favourite.

anecdotes.text: The owner hand-makes all five noodle varieties — udon, kishimen, soba, Chinese-style, and miso-based — during the restaurant's mid-afternoon break to keep up with the wide-ranging menu.

When you order curry udon, staff bring a small handmade cloth apron to protect your clothes — along with hot tea and a hand towel — a thoughtful house touch that has become a hallmark of the experience.
The menu expanded dramatically because customers kept requesting dishes beyond the traditional udon repertoire, and the owner, faithful to his father's philosophy, kept adding them.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: locipoで1972年創業（西区上名古屋3-13-30）、2代目 小塩清貴が23歳で継承、母（大女将）と妻 貴美子と営む、麺5種（うどん・きしめん・そば・中華・味噌）を中休みに手打ち、30品超のメニュー拡大を確認。Rettyでカツカレーうどんが名物、注文時にエプロン提供を確認。

gf_cross_contamination.text: All noodles are wheat-based and hand-made on-site across five varieties (udon, kishimen, soba, ramen-style, miso). Cross-contamination with wheat throughout the kitchen is certain.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (含む小麦) is used in dashi-based dishes. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternatives are mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi stock is made from Makurazaki katsuobushi (bonito) and Kagoshima kombu. The curry broth contains chicken bone stock and pork. All broth-based dishes contain animal products.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy protocols, or staff allergy-handling procedures are documented on any known source. The restaurant does not appear to have experience catering to strict dietary restrictions. Call 052-531-3387 to inquire directly.

positives.text: The restaurant is highly attentive to hospitality in general — offering aprons for curry dishes and prompt service — suggesting staff are receptive to questions, even if dietary accommodation is limited.

cultural_comfort.note: A multigenerational neighborhood udon-ya with no English signage or menu; the staff are warm and service-oriented but communication will be in Japanese.

places.name: スパイスアワー (Spice Hour)
places.gf_detail: Wheat is present throughout: naan is a signature item, and standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is likely used in seasoning. The kitchen operates a tandoor oven and fries items such as cardamom karaage, indicating active use of wheat-flour batters. No gluten-free accommodation or labeling has been mentioned in any review, blog, or listing. Celiac diners should not assume safety and should inquire directly — ideally in Japanese — before visiting.
places.vegan_detail: Fully omnivore menu. Fish sauce (魚醤) is used as a core seasoning, bonito (鰹) features in at least one curry preparation, and lamb, chicken, and pork all appear on the menu. No vegan dishes or vegan-option labeling have been found across any source. The concept — spice bar with finishing curry — is built around meat and seafood-forward small plates.
places.hours_raw: Mon and Wed–Sun 17:00–22:00 (L.O. 21:30), closed Tue. Confirm hours on the official Instagram.
places.neighborhood: Sengen-cho and Shindo, Nishi Ward
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Spice Hour built its following through pop-ups: running curry pop-ups at borrowed venues such as DRUM in Fukiage, the guesthouse Yoruyonaka, and Early Birds, and appearing twice at the 'Curry Koshien.' On June 8, 2025 it opened a permanent shop in a renovated former bicycle store, styled as an old folk house, in Shindo, Nishi Ward, Nagoya. The kitchen is run by two people: one with three years' experience at several popular Nagoya cafes, the other with two years at the Indian restaurant known as 'Erick South.'

chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市西区新道（古民家風にリノベーションした空間）
chef_bio.background: スパイスアワーは間借り営業として吹上のDRUMやゲストハウスよるよなか、アーリーバーズといった場所でカレーポップアップを重ね、カレーの甲子園にも二度出場して支持者を増やした末に、2025年6月8日、名古屋市西区新道に古民家風の実店舗をオープンさせた（浅間町駅から徒歩約5分）。 厨房は2名体制で、一方は名古屋の人気カフェ数店で3年、もう一方はインド料理で知られる『エリックサウス』で2年の経験を積んでいる。
chef_bio.philosophy: 一品ごとにスパイスの個性を味わわせるシンプルさを軸に、スパイスとお酒の組み合わせを楽しむ『スパイス酒場』として機能する。 追いだしや旨味調味料を加えていないのに深みが出ているチキンカレーが評判で、店主は『何年もかけて、なぜか深みが出た』と語る。
chef_bio.specialty: カルダモン唐揚げ、タンドリーマトン・シークケバブなどのスパイス肴、〆のチキンカレー（シャバシャバ系）

anecdotes.text: オープン前夜の6月7日に『アワーの披露宴』と題したイベントを開催。Bar KanataやBUTTER NOTEをフードゲストに招き、アコースティックライブとDJも行った。
常連から『しっかりとしたタンドールがある』と評される本格設備を備える一方、〆カレーとしても機能する間口の広さが特徴。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログマガジン（473277）とoinagoyaで2025年6月8日開業、厨房2名（人気カフェ3年／エリックサウス2年）、追いだし・旨味調味料なしのチキンカレー、カルダモン唐揚げを確認。liveraryで6月7日『アワーの披露宴』（Bar Kanata・BUTTER NOTE）を確認。『元自転車屋』改装の裏付けは取れず削除。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Naan (wheat flour) is baked in the tandoor oven and served as a core item; the same tandoor is used for marinated meats such as lamb seekh kebab, meaning shared cooking surfaces are in active use.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No confirmation that gluten-free tamari or alternative soy sauce is used. Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is the default in this style of fusion spice cooking.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish sauce (魚醤) is used directly in at least one appetizer preparation (mint sauce and fish sauce namero); meat and seafood are cooked throughout the small kitchen, making vegan cross-contact highly probable.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information on staff allergy training or allergen labeling has been found in any source. Reservations are handled via Instagram DM, which could provide a channel to raise dietary needs in advance.

positives.text: Small 15-seat independent kitchen means a single team handles all cooking, reducing the complexity of communicating dietary needs — direct conversation with the chef-owner is feasible.

cultural_comfort.note: Reservations are made via Instagram DM in Japanese; no English interface or signage is indicated, though the young, informal owner is described as welcoming to all.

places.name: 和のフードホール hosa（蓬左） (Wa no Food Hall hosa (Hōsa))
places.gf_detail: Gluten is pervasive: kishimen is a wheat-flour flat noodle (the signature dish), soy sauce (wheat-containing) is used in dashi preparations and dressings, and tempura dishes appear on the kaiseki menu. No gluten-free labeling, dedicated fryer, or allergy accommodation system has been documented. The afternoon tea set includes wheat-based pastries. Not suitable for celiac diners.
places.vegan_detail: The kitchen is built around fish-based dashi as its philosophical core — 100 bonito fillets are hand-shaved every morning for the kishimen broth. Wagashi sweets use red bean paste, eggs, butter, and dairy. Seafood bowls feature fresh fish and shellfish. No plant-based or vegan dishes are offered, and hidden dashi (katsuo/katsuobushi) is present throughout the menu.
places.hours_raw: 10:00–17:00 (last order 16:30), daily; evening service (17:00–21:00) available by reservation for groups of 10+ at ¥8,800/person
places.neighborhood: Naka-ku, Sannomaru (around Nagoya Castle)
places.notes: Hōsa opened March 18, 2022 inside the Nagoya Noh Theatre (名古屋能楽堂) at the main gate of Nagoya Castle. It was conceived as a 食楽堂 (shokurakudō — a food-pleasure hall) with a mission to carry Owari-Nagoya's 400-year culinary heritage into the future by uniting three Edo-period Nagoya

chef_bio.chef_name: 近澤 (Chikazawa) — given name not published
chef_bio.origin: Sannomaru, Naka Ward, Nagoya — the historic castle precinct of the former Owari Tokugawa domain
chef_bio.background: Hōsa opened March 18, 2022 inside the Nagoya Noh Theatre (名古屋能楽堂) near the main gate of Nagoya Castle. It was conceived as a 食楽堂 (shokurakudō — a food-pleasure hall) with a mission to carry Owari-Nagoya's food heritage into the future by uniting three Edo-period Nagoya institutions under one roof, across roughly 112 seats spanning private tatami rooms, a casual food hall, and a retail shop.
chef_bio.philosophy: Three heritage brands — the ~300-year-old catering house Yaohiko Honten, the Owari Tokugawa clan's confectioner Kasho Hanakikyo (roots ~400 years back), and Nagomi Kashiya Nakamata (descended from an Edo-period Atsuta seafood business) — collaborate to present classic Nagoya flavors in a setting worthy of the castle district, while also innovating with items like the 'ふわふわ わぬき' butter dorayaki, best eaten immediately as the butter melts into the still-warm cake.
chef_bio.specialty: Kishimen paired with a seafood bowl and the Japanese Afternoon Tea Set (和フタヌーンティーセット) served on a Nagoya Castle-motif tiered hinoki-wood stand

anecdotes.text: Every morning the kitchen hand-shaves 100 sticks of dried bonito (katsuobushi) to draw the day's stock — this fresh dashi is considered the soul of the kishimen broth.

The butter dorayaki 'ふわふわ わぬき' is meant to be eaten right away — the butter melts into the still-warm, souffle-textured pancake so quickly that eating it at the counter is essentially required (reportedly within about two minutes).
The head chef, Chef Chikazawa (近澤総料理長), is an officially recognized 'あいちの名工' (Aichi master craftsman) who also serves as head chef of Hōzentei (宝善亭), the restaurant at the Tokugawa Art Museum.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: life-designs.jpで総料理長は『あいちの名工・近澤総料理長』で徳川美術館・宝善亭の料理長も務めると確認（バイオの『近藤俊雄』は誤り）。ssuブログ／tabelogで2022年3月18日開業、八百彦本店・花桔梗・なか又の3ブランド、鰹節100本の削り出し、わぬきバターどら焼き、和アフタヌーンティー、112席を確認。どら焼きの『2分』は要出典としてhanako引用で留保。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination protocols is publicly available. The kitchen handles kishimen wheat noodles, tempura, and wheat-based soy sauce as daily staples — shared preparation surfaces are likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard (wheat-containing) soy sauce is used in dashi preparations, dipping sauces, and the kaiseki courses. No tamari or gluten-free shoyu alternative has been mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi (katsuobushi) is central to the kitchen philosophy and present in broths, sauces, and likely wagashi fillings. No vegan-safe preparation line exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No documented allergy-handling policy or allergen chart was found. Phone reservations are available (052-253-8566); contacting ahead of any visit is strongly advised for any dietary restriction.

positives.text: All seating is non-smoking throughout the venue.
Wheelchair accessible with nearby dedicated parking (308 regular spaces at the Seimon-mae lot).

cultural_comfort.note: Located in a traditional Noh theatre with a refined, heritage-focused atmosphere; staff are welcoming but the setting is distinctly Japanese cultural — some menu items and signage may be Japanese-only.

places.name: やきとり 風月 名古屋本店 (Yakitori Fugetsu Nagoya Honten)
places.gf_detail: Yakitori is grilled on binchotan charcoal, which in principle is a wheat-free cooking method, but standard Japanese tare (dipping sauce) and soy sauce almost certainly contain wheat. Ponzu and other dressings used in sides likely contain soy sauce (wheat). Finishing dishes — tantanmen, chicken ramen, dan dan noodles — contain wheat noodles. No gluten-free menu, no allergen notation on Tabelog or HotPepper, and no dedicated fryer information found. A celiac diner would need to inquire carefully in advance; the kitchen may be able to offer salt-seasoned (塩) skewers only, but cross-contamination from tare and shared prep areas cannot be ruled out without direct confirmation.
places.vegan_detail: This is a dedicated yakitori (grilled chicken) restaurant with a kaiseki-style course format built around chicken — Nagoya Cochin and Mikawa red chicken are the centerpiece. Courses include chicken tataki, liver, gizzard, heart, and finishing noodles. Vegetable side dishes (seasonal vegetables with house miso) exist but function as accompaniments. There are no vegan courses and no indication the kitchen can build a vegan meal on request. Hidden animal products are virtually certain throughout.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 17:30–22:30 (food L.O. 21:30, drink L.O. 22:00); closed Sun & holidays
places.neighborhood: Nishi-ku, Nagono (Shikemichi) / 3 min from Kokusai Center Stn
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] The restaurant is operated by 合資会社宮治 (Miyaji Ltd.), a small company behind a small group of Nagoya dining concepts. The honten (main store) occupies a renovated traditional machiya townhouse — reportedly around 100 years old — on a quiet back lane one street removed from the main

chef_bio.origin: Nishi Ward, Nagoya; Shikankaidō (四間道) district, a preserved Edo-period merchant townscape near Kokusai Center Station.
chef_bio.background: The restaurant is operated by 合資会社宮治 (Miyaji), the small company behind a five-shop group of Nagoya dining concepts in the Nagono/Shikankaidō area. The honten (main store) occupies a renovated traditional machiya townhouse — reportedly around 100 years old — on a quiet back lane one street removed from the main avenue in the Shikankaidō (四間道) district of Nagono, Nishi Ward.
chef_bio.philosophy: Described by the operator as 'a luxurious yakitori restaurant for adults who want to enjoy delicious things in small portions and variety,' incorporating seasonality into grilled chicken. The hideaway location — tucked off the main road in the historic Shikankaidō streetscape — is intentional, creating a private, calm atmosphere suited to dates and business entertaining.
chef_bio.specialty: Kaiseki-style yakitori using Nagoya Cochin (名古屋コーチン), grilled over binchotan white charcoal and served in the rhythm of a formal kaiseki meal, with courses interleaving non-skewer dishes.

anecdotes.text: A reviewer from Hong Kong visiting in 2018 noted that between skewer courses, non-yakitori dishes such as tofu and salad were served — an unexpected kaiseki rhythm — and that the staff 'were very helpful and friendly and tried to translate,' making the experience accessible even without Japanese. The wing tip was singled out as 'very yummy.'

Japanese reviewers cite the contrast of crispy-exterior, juicy-interior skewers as the kitchen's defining technique — attributed to slow binchotan grilling — and highlight the lightly seared Nagoya Cochin tataki (finished with fragrant sesame oil) as a standout non-skewer dish.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 宮治グループ公式で運営会社・5店舗・四間道の隠れ家立地・『贅沢な大人のためのやきとり屋』コンセプトを確認。nagoya-meshiで備長炭焼き、名古屋コーチンのタタキ、パリッ／ジューシーの串、非串料理を挟むコース構成を確認。三河赤鶏とチーズ前菜は裏付けが取れず削除。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination protocols is publicly available. The kitchen handles wheat-containing tare sauce, soy sauce-based dressings, and wheat noodle finishing dishes in the same space as the grill — cross-contamination risk for celiacs must be assumed until directly confirmed otherwise.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard tare (焼き鳥のたれ) used on skewers almost certainly contains soy sauce brewed with wheat. Sesame-ponzu dishes and other à la carte items also likely use wheat-containing soy sauce. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce option is mentioned anywhere across sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is built around chicken and meat; animal products are present in every course element including broths, tare, and finishing noodles. No plant-only preparation area or vegan accommodation is referenced in any source.

staff_allergy_handling.text: One non-Japanese reviewer noted staff attempted to assist with translation and communication at the table, suggesting some goodwill toward guests with special needs. However, no formal allergen protocol or allergy-handling procedure is documented in any public source.

positives.text: Salt-seasoned (塩) yakitori skewers — if available and confirmed wheat-free — could in theory be the lowest-risk option for gluten-sensitive (non-celiac) diners, as plain charcoal grilling of chicken with salt involves no obvious wheat-containing ingredients.

cultural_comfort.note: Menu is Japanese-only; one TripAdvisor reviewer noted staff were helpful and tried to translate, but Japanese ability will make the experience smoother.

places.name: TRIVENI (Triveni)
places.gf_detail: Indian curry bases are often flour-free, but naan contains wheat gluten. No official mention of a gluten-free program or dedicated menu. Switching to rice may allow wheat to be avoided, but cross-contamination risk in the kitchen and soy-sauce use (in Nepalese dishes such as chicken sadeko) are unknown. Celiacs must phone to confirm before visiting.
places.vegan_detail: Tabelog mentions a vegetarian curry option. However, the mainstays are butter chicken curry, chicken tikka and other dishes using dairy and meat. The naan is also highly likely to contain dairy, and there is no explicit full-vegan accommodation. A more vegan-leaning order may be possible by switching to rice, but this must be confirmed.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Fri 11:00–14:30 (L.O. 14:15), 18:00–23:00 (food L.O. 22:00, drinks L.O. 22:30). Sat 18:00–23:00 (reservation required; parties of 10+; course of ¥5,500 or more). Closed Sun and public holidays.
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi, Naka Ward (4 min walk from Marunouchi Stn Exit 2)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A hideaway Indian/Nepalese restaurant and bar on the 2nd floor of a building in Marunouchi, Nagoya, which had its grand opening on June 30, 2025. It runs a dual concept: authentic spice curries by day and a bar with 30+ whiskeys and wines by night. Spice level can be chosen from 0 to 5, accommodating those who dislike heat. Authentic chefs pursue true Indian and Nepalese flavors while finishing dishes to suit Japanese palates.

roles: インド・ネパール料理シェフ
chef_bio.background: 2025年6月30日にグランドオープンした、名古屋・丸の内のビル2階に構える隠れ家型のインド・ネパール料理店兼バー（丸の内駅から徒歩約4分）。 昼は本格スパイスカレー、夜はウイスキー・ワイン30種以上を揃えるバータイムという二刀流のコンセプトを掲げる。 スパイスレベルは0〜5段階で選べ、辛さが苦手な人にも対応。 本場の料理人がインド・ネパールの味を追求しながら、日本人が食べやすいように仕上げている。
chef_bio.philosophy: ランチは本格スパイスカレー、夜はしっとりバータイム。 一人でも仲間とでも使えるオアシス的空間を目指す。
chef_bio.specialty: スパイスカレー（ナン・スープ・サラダおかわり自由）＋チキンティッカなどのネパール料理、オリジナルラッシー

anecdotes.text: グランドオープン当日のInstagram投稿で開店を告知し、昼カレー・夜バーの二刀流コンセプトを強調した。
ランチのナンは驚きのビッグサイズで、地元メディア（しゃちほこニュース）でも『写真で伝わる？』と紹介されるほどのインパクト。おかわり自由のナンと本場カレーが丸の内ランチの新名物に。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログでTRIVENIは2025年6月30日オープン、丸の内、昼カレー／夜バー、スパイス0〜5、ナン・スープ・サラダおかわり自由、ワイン30種以上を確認。チキンティッカ等ネパール料理あり。バイオの『フランゴ（ポルトガル語の鶏）』は文脈的誤りのため修正。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテン（小麦）の交差汚染リスクについて公式情報なし。ナン（小麦粉使用）と同一厨房での調理が想定されるため、セリアック病患者への安全確認は電話問い合わせが必須。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: カレーへの醤油使用は確認されていないが、チキンサデコ等のネパール料理に醤油系調味料が用いられる可能性がある。公式のアレルゲン情報は未開示。

vegan_cross_contact.text: バターチキンカレー等の乳製品を使ったメニューと同一厨房での調理。ヴィーガン向けの分離調理についての記載なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応や食制限への対応について公式サイト・口コミ上に明示的な記載なし。来店前の電話確認（052-253-7874）を推奨。

positives.text: スパイスレベルを0〜5段階で選択可能で、カスタマイズ性は高い。ライスへの変更注文が可能で、小麦（ナン）を避けた選択肢が存在する可能性がある。
ベジタリアンカレーオプションの存在がTabelog上で言及されており、植物性食材を優先する食事の起点となりうる。

cultural_comfort.note: 日本語環境のインド・ネパール料理店。スパイスレベル0〜5で注文できるなど日本語での説明・対応が中心で、英語対応の明示はない。

places.name: 鮨 しみず (Sushi Shimizu)
places.gf_detail: Standard Edomae sushi; conventional wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu) is used for neta glazing and dipping — a direct gluten source. Dashi in simmered dishes almost certainly uses conventional ingredients. No GF shoyu alternative, dedicated preparation, or allergy accommodation is mentioned anywhere on the official site or third-party listings. Celiac diners should call in advance, though accommodation is unlikely given the fixed omakase format.
places.vegan_detail: Pure omakase sushi featuring fish, shellfish, and seafood. No plant-based course or substitution is offered. Shari (rice) uses fish-stock-influenced preparation context; dishes include clams and tuna as signature items. Not viable for vegans.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Fri 18:30–23:00 (L.O. 22:00), Sat/Sun/holidays 18:00–23:00 (L.O. 22:00), lunch on irregular days (from 11:30 / from 13:30), irregular days off
places.neighborhood: 名西 / 浄心 (Nishi Ward)
places.notes: Naoki Shimizu trained in Tokyo before returning to Nagoya to open his own counter. The original location in Naka Ward (Shin-Sakae) opened in June 2017; in May 2022 he relocated to the current address in Nishi Ward, creating a quieter, more intimate hidden-gem setting near Joshin

chef_bio.background: Naoki Shimizu trained in Tokyo before opening his own counter in Nagoya. The original shop in Naka Ward (Shin-Sakae) opened June 1, 2017; on May 17, 2022 he relocated to the current address in Nishi Ward, a quieter, hidden-gem counter near Joshin Station. The restaurant was selected for a Michelin Plate in the 2019 Michelin Guide Aichi/Gifu/Mie edition.
chef_bio.philosophy: Shimizu wants guests to 'purely enjoy food' without preconception, pursuing flavour beyond convention while honouring Edomae tradition. He sources fresh local seasonal seafood and premium tuna from specialist wholesaler Yamako.
chef_bio.specialty: Reservation-only Edomae omakase. The shari uses locally grown Aichi rice ('Aichinokaori') seasoned with a proprietary blend of two red vinegars for depth plus white vinegar for balance; each piece is timed to be eaten at its peak.

anecdotes.text: The counter runs entirely on an omakase, fully reservation-based format, with pieces served one at a time and timed for the fleeting moment sushi is at its best.
Shimizu earned a Michelin Plate in 2019 while at his Shin-Sakae counter, then in May 2022 moved to a discreet Nishi Ward location near Joshin Station to create a calmer, more intimate room.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式（spirit.html）で『純粋に食を楽しむ』理念、愛知の香り米に赤酢2種＋白酢のシャリ、ヤマコの鮪を確認。tabelog移転ページで2017年6月1日 新栄開業→2022年5月17日 西区へ移転、浄心駅、2019年ミシュラン掲載（プレート）を確認。伊勢湾・三河湾の断定、からすみ手巻き・桑名の蛤・貫数は出典で裏付けできず一般化／削除。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination controls. The kitchen handles fish, shellfish, and wheat-based soy sauce throughout service with no stated separation protocols.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is used for neta glaze and accompaniment — a direct and unavoidable gluten source in the omakase format as served.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is fish- and seafood-based; no plant-only preparation stream exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy or dietary accommodation policy is published. The fixed omakase format leaves little room for substitution; guests with serious allergies should contact the restaurant directly before booking.

cultural_comfort.note: Counter-only omakase with a strict etiquette code — no strong fragrances, no wrist jewelry at the counter; reservations and communication are primarily in Japanese.

places.name: 会席料理・ひつまぶし 雅（MASA） (Kaiseki Masa (MASA))
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or allergy accommodation information is published on Tabelog, the official site, or any aggregator page. Traditional Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is almost certainly used in the eel tare (sauce) and kaiseki preparations. The eel is grilled with a house-made sauce and served with Koshihikari rice, but the sauce composition is not disclosed. No dedicated fryer information available. Celiac-safety cannot be confirmed; wheat exposure via tare/soy sauce and hidden dashi is highly likely. Diners with serious gluten sensitivity should call ahead: 052-228-3128.
places.vegan_detail: The menu centers on domestic Japanese eel (unagi) grilled over binchotan charcoal, served as hitsumabushi at lunch and as the centerpiece of kaiseki courses at dinner. No plant-based or vegan alternatives are listed. Traditional Japanese dashi (likely made with fish stock/bonito) is standard in kaiseki cuisine, and eel is the defining ingredient throughout the menu. Vegan diners cannot be accommodated here.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat & holidays 11:00–14:00 / 17:00–22:00, closed Sun (confirm holiday hours)
places.neighborhood: Hisaya-ōdōri / Izumi area (Higashi-ku)
places.notes: Opened in April 2024 in a discreet first-floor space in Nagoya's Higashi Ward, a short walk from Hisaya-Odori Station. The restaurant bills itself as a 隠れ家 (kakurega — hidden retreat), blending traditional techniques with a contemporary sensibility in a compact 18-seat room.

chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市東区泉（レジデンス泉1F）
chef_bio.background: Opened April 6, 2024 in a discreet first-floor space in Nagoya's Higashi Ward (Izumi), a short walk from Hisaya-Odori Station. The restaurant bills itself as a 隠れ家 (kakurega — hidden retreat), blending traditional techniques with a contemporary sensibility in a compact 18-seat room (8 counter, 4 private, plus additional seating).
chef_bio.philosophy: Refined yet approachable Japanese cuisine centered on quality domestic eel and seasonal ingredients, offered in a setting equally suited to solo counter dining and intimate private-room entertaining.
chef_bio.specialty: Hitsumabushi — safe, domestic Japanese eel hand-filleted, skewered and grilled over binchotan charcoal, served over clay-pot-cooked Koshihikari rice from Niigata's Minamiuonuma region; dinner shifts to seasonal kaiseki courses with charcoal-grilled preparations throughout.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant opened on April 6, 2024 and quickly became a destination for hitsumabushi and kaiseki in the Hisaya-Odori neighborhood, its 18 seats split between an 8-seat counter and private rooms.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: tabelogで2024年4月6日オープン、18席（カウンター8・個室4・他6）、東区泉、久屋大通駅至近を確認。gnaviこだわり・公式で国産うなぎのひつまぶし（備長炭焼き）、南魚沼産コシヒカリの土鍋炊き、季節の会席コースを確認。屋号のオーナー名は非公表につきnull。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No cross-contamination controls are disclosed. The kitchen prepares both lunch eel service and multi-course kaiseki dinners in a compact 18-seat restaurant; shared prep surfaces are likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Domestic eel is grilled with a house-made tare (sauce); traditional Japanese tare is soy-sauce based and nearly always contains wheat. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is built around unagi (eel) and fish-based kaiseki preparations; there is no plant-based section and no disclosure of vegan-safe handling.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy is published on the official site, Tabelog, Hot Pepper, or any aggregator. Diners with serious allergies should call 052-228-3128 before visiting.

positives.text: The restaurant does state use of domestic Japanese eel (国産うなぎ) only, and binchotan charcoal grilling — simple, whole-ingredient cooking with minimal industrial additives in the core protein.

cultural_comfort.note: A refined kaiseki and eel specialist in a quiet Nagoya side street; staff communication is in Japanese and the dining style — private rooms, counter omakase — assumes familiarity with formal Japanese dining conventions.

places.name: 手打そば処 谷屋 (Tebachi Soba-dokoro Taniya)
places.gf_detail: The standard soba is 二八 (nibachi) — roughly 80% buckwheat, 20% wheat flour — and is NOT gluten-free. A 十割 (juwari, 100% buckwheat) upgrade is offered for +¥110 and contains no wheat flour by recipe, but the noodles are made in a shared kitchen where wheat flour is handled daily. The tsuyu (dipping sauce and soup base) contains soy sauce made from wheat. Tempura batter contains wheat flour. No dedicated GF preparation has been confirmed by any source. People with celiac disease should be aware of high cross-contamination risk from shared equipment and wheat-containing dashi/tsuyu, even with the juwari noodle option.
places.vegan_detail: The dashi is made from a proprietary blend of katsuobushi (skipjack), mackerel, and bonito — not vegan. The tsuyu also contains soy sauce (wheat). Vegetable-only small plates (yuba sashimi, seasonal vegetables) may be available but there is no dedicated vegan menu. Guests with strict vegan requirements should call ahead; do not assume any hot dish is animal-free.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sat 11:00–21:30 (L.O. 20:30), closed Sun, also closed the 4th Monday of each month
places.neighborhood: Nishi-ku, Habashita, along the Horikawa (7–9 min walk from Sengen-chō Stn)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Owner-chef 谷幸憲 built his craft around an obsessive commitment to soba aroma and freshness. He sources buckwheat flour just once a year and grinds only what is needed the following day using a stone mill — a discipline that keeps the flour at peak fragrance. He personally eats two

chef_bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi. The restaurant occupies a pre-war warehouse that once belonged to a cotton merchant (綿問屋), renovated to preserve the original high ceilings, dark wooden beams, and earthen walls along the Horikawa river near Nagoya Castle.
chef_bio.background: Owner-chef 谷幸憲 built his craft around an obsessive commitment to soba aroma and freshness. He sources buckwheat flour just once a year and grinds only what is needed the following day using a stone mill — a discipline that keeps the flour at peak fragrance. He personally eats two bowls of soba every day, one hot and one cold, to verify the quality before opening.
chef_bio.philosophy: Tani adjusts his flour ratios daily according to weather and humidity, treating each batch of noodles as a fresh decision rather than a fixed formula. He regards maximising the natural aroma of buckwheat — through cold water in the dough, stone-milled same-day flour, and minimal handling — as the central task of the craft.
chef_bio.specialty: かもなんそば (Kamo Nan Soba) — duck and Welsh onion soba, the house signature. The duck is finished in its own rendered fat with a torch for richness, and the broth is a blend of light soy, bonito, mackerel, and skipjack. The juwari (100% buckwheat) upgrade produces pale, thin noodles with a delicate aroma, best eaten with a pinch of salt before the fragrance fades.

anecdotes.text: Tani eats 600–700 bowls of soba per year and tastes two bowls — one hot, one cold — every single day before service to calibrate the day's noodles.
He uses cold water rather than hot when making juwari (100% buckwheat) dough, an unconventional choice he maintains deliberately to preserve aroma.
The kamo nan soba was singled out by top chefs as a standout dish in Nagoya; the shop also carries a Michelin Plate for its aromatic, refined soba.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: テレビ愛知（yomu.tv-aichi）で谷幸憲、綿問屋の戦前倉庫改装・堀川沿い、蕎麦粉を年1回仕入れ翌日石臼挽き、年600〜700杯・毎日冷温2杯を味見、十割に冷水使用、かもなんそば（自家脂でバーナー仕上げ／薄口・鰹・鯖・宗田の出汁）をトップシェフが絶賛、ミシュランプレートを確認。全て裏付けあり。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat flour is used daily in the kitchen for standard nibachi (二八) noodles and tempura batter, creating a shared-environment cross-contamination risk even when the juwari (十割) noodle option is ordered. No source confirms dedicated equipment or separate preparation areas for buckwheat-only orders.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The tsuyu (dipping broth and hot soup base) is described as containing soy sauce (醤油) blended with bonito, mackerel, and skipjack dashi. Standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat; no tamari or GF soy sauce alternative has been mentioned by any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The dashi used in all soba broths is an animal-based blend (katsuobushi/skipjack, mackerel, bonito). Even the dashi-maki tamago (rolled omelet) is described as having a strong dashi flavour. There is no plant-based broth option confirmed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information about staff allergy training or allergy-handling procedures has been found in any source. Guests with celiac disease or severe wheat allergy should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.

positives.text: A 十割 (juwari, 100% buckwheat flour) noodle option is available for +¥110. By recipe the noodles contain no added wheat flour, which may suit those with mild wheat sensitivity, though celiac-level safety cannot be confirmed given the shared kitchen environment.

cultural_comfort.note: Traditional soba-ya atmosphere in a historic warehouse building; no English menu or staff expected, but ordering is simple — point at the laminated menu and indicate nibachi (standard) or juwari (100% buckwheat, +¥110).

places.name: レストラン ペルージュ (Restaurant Perouges)
places.gf_detail: No dedicated gluten-free menu. Classic French kitchen: all stocks (fond de veau, consommé) made in-house from scratch, and house-baked bread is served — both are standard wheat risk points. Soy sauce is unlikely in this French kitchen, but cross-contamination from bread and flour-thickened sauces is a real concern. The restaurant explicitly asks guests to disclose allergies at time of reservation; same-day requests cannot be accommodated. A celiac diner should call ahead and have a detailed conversation before booking.
places.vegan_detail: Not a vegan-friendly restaurant. The kitchen features pigeon rôti, foie gras and rabbit pâté, veal, and wild game (gibier) as signature proteins. Organic and pesticide-free vegetables are a proud focus (~80–90% of produce), but they are used as accompaniments in a fully omnivorous French menu. No vegan course or substitution is listed or implied.
places.hours_raw: Mon and Thu–Sun 11:30–13:30 (L.O. 13:30), 18:00–21:00 (L.O. 21:00); closed Tue and Wed
places.neighborhood: Nagono (Nishi Ward) / 3–5 min walk from Kokusai Center Stn
places.notes: Born in Osaka in 1977. Influenced by a grandmother who ran a restaurant, he entered the culinary world. After graduating from culinary school, he trained in Japanese and French cuisine at hotels and restaurants in Nagoya, then opened his own restaurant in Nagono in 2008.

chef_bio.chef_name: 今泉智成（いまいずみ としなり）
chef_bio.origin: 2008年開業。那古野の古い町家を改装した隠れ家的な2フロア構造。
chef_bio.background: 1977年大阪生まれ。 祖母が料理店を営んでいたことに影響を受け料理の道へ。 調理師学校卒業後、名古屋市内のホテルや料理店で和食・フランス料理の修業を積み、2008年に那古野に独立開業。
chef_bio.philosophy: 「レストランの食事は『美味しく』『楽しく』『優雅で』『身も心も癒されるようなひと時』でなければならない」。 素材の美味しさを最大限に引き出すため、フォン・ド・ヴォーやコンソメなどの基礎出汁はすべて一から仕込む。 オーガニック野菜との出会いはナチュラルワインへの興味から始まり、現在は使用野菜の約80〜90%が有機栽培品。
chef_bio.specialty: ランド産鳩のロティやフォアグラ・ジビエを軸とした本格フランス料理コース。 無農薬野菜・天然魚介・抗生物質不使用の畜肉を使用。

anecdotes.text: シェフは「素材がオーガニックだと最初にアピールするより、まず料理を食べて美味しいと感じてもらい、後でそれが有機野菜だったと知ってもらいたい」という姿勢を持つ。有機野菜との向き合い方はナチュラルワインを好むようになったことがきっかけで、自然な農法と自然なワイン造りの共通点に気づいたと語る。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 農水省東海（maff）で2008年那古野開業、今泉智成、有機野菜80〜90%、ナチュラルワインを契機とした有機との出会い、基礎出汁を一から仕込む姿勢を確認。ヒトサラで1977年大阪生まれ、祖母の料理店の影響、調理師学校卒、名古屋のホテル・料理店で和食／フレンチ修業、ランド産鳩ロティ等を確認。

gf_cross_contamination.text: House-baked bread is served as part of the meal, meaning wheat flour is actively used in the kitchen. Flour-thickened sauces are standard in classical French cooking. No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or protocol is mentioned.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: This is a French kitchen; soy sauce is not a listed ingredient. However, no explicit confirmation that soy sauce or wheat-based condiments are absent from all preparations.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Animal proteins (pigeon, veal, foie gras, rabbit, wild game) are central to the menu. Complete separation from vegan dishes is not possible in this kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Tabelog listing explicitly states: 苦手な食材、アレルギー等はご予約時にお伝えください。当日ですと対応しかねる場合がございます。（Please inform us of food dislikes and allergies at time of reservation; same-day requests may not be accommodable.）

positives.text: All stocks and sauces made in-house from scratch by the chef, giving the kitchen full control over ingredients — facilitating custom preparation when notified in advance.
80–90% of vegetables used are certified organic or pesticide-free, sourced directly from named farms; antibiotic-free meats and wild-caught seafood used throughout.

cultural_comfort.note: French-style fine dining operated by a Japanese chef-couple; menu and communication are in Japanese, but the Western dining format is familiar and the staff are experienced handling reservation-time allergy notifications.

places.name: 和風ラザニア専門店 Y (Wafu Razania Senmonten Wai (Japanese-Style L)
places.gf_detail: This is a wheat-pasta lasagna specialty shop — gluten is the core ingredient. All lasagna varieties are built on standard wheat pasta sheets. Soy sauce (wheat-based) is incorporated directly into the sauce blend alongside Hatchomiso. No gluten-free pasta alternative is mentioned anywhere. No dedicated fryer information found. Not safe for celiac diners.
places.vegan_detail: No vegan options documented. The signature dish is lasagna layered with cheese-based béchamel (white sauce), tomato sauce, and Japanese condiments including Hatchomiso and soy sauce — dairy is integral. Menu also includes items like shrimp fry doria and beef tongue lasagna. No plant-based or dairy-free alternatives are mentioned on the official site, Tabelog listing, or any third-party reviews.
places.neighborhood: Naka-ku, Ninomaru, Kinshachi Yokochō Muneharu Zone
places.notes: Chef Shinohara Hidekazu spent 18 years in Milan, where he trained under Nobuyuki Matsuhisa (NOBU) at NOBU Armani and went on to open Zero Milano — a creative sushi restaurant whose clientele included soccer legend Roberto Baggio. His Milan-era work earned him a place in Who's Who

chef_bio.origin: Nikaho City (にかほ市象潟町), Akita Prefecture, Japan
chef_bio.background: Chef Shinohara Hidekazu spent 18 years in Milan, where after heading a Tokyo sushi restaurant he trained at NOBU Armani (of the Nobu Matsuhisa group) and went on to open Zero Milano — a creative fusion-sushi restaurant whose clientele included soccer legend Roberto Baggio. His Milan-era work earned him a place in Who's Who: Chefs, a US publication ranking the world's top 100 culinary professionals. He later reverse-imported the concept to Japan with Zero Tokyo in Ginza, before launching this lasagna-focused concept at Kinshachi Yokocho in July 2023.
chef_bio.philosophy: Shinohara built the concept on a conviction that Italian and Japanese culinary sensibilities share deep common ground — both prize seasonality, craftsmanship, and umami. The shop is branded simply 'Y', a single-letter nod to its 和 (wa, Japanese) × 伊 (i, Italian) fusion, positioning lasagna not as foreign import but as a form Japanese ingredients inhabit naturally.
chef_bio.specialty: 和風ラザニア (Japanese-style lasagna) — six layers of Italian pasta sheets with tomato sauce, béchamel, Hatchomiso, and soy sauce, in rotating varieties such as Salmon & Avocado, Mixed Mushroom, and Whitebait & Lemon Pepper using shirasu from Shinojima (篠島, Aichi Prefecture)

anecdotes.text: Roberto Baggio was among the celebrity regulars at Shinohara's Zero Milano in Italy — a clientele that reportedly also included figures tied to Dolce & Gabbana and the founder of Franck Muller — drawn by his Japanese-Italian fusion cuisine long before the concept returned to Japan.
The lasagna itself uses six layers of Italian pasta sheet bound with tomato sauce, béchamel, and — the Nagoya twist — Hatchomiso and soy sauce, marrying Italian structure with Owari flavour.

Kinshachi Yokocho's Muneharu Zone opened in 2019 beside Nagoya Castle's Ninomaru grounds; 和風ラザニア専門店 Y joined the complex at its July 2023 opening, part of a wave of concept restaurants targeting castle-area visitors.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: choco0824で篠原秀和は秋田県にかほ市象潟町出身、イタリア18年、NOBU ARMANI、ゼロミラノ、Who's Who Chefs世界TOP100、銀座zero Tokyoを確認。lemon8でゼロミラノにロベルト・バッジョらが通ったと確認。tokai-tvで6層生地＋トマト・ホワイト・八丁味噌・醤油のラザニアを、kinshachi-yokochoで篠島産しらす等の品揃えを確認。出身地『Nago／Akita』と『Shikoji島』は誤りのため修正。店名Y=和+伊の語源は出典不明のため緩和。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat pasta is the foundational ingredient of every dish; cross-contamination throughout the kitchen is near-certain. No separate preparation areas for allergen-free cooking are documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce (which contains wheat) is used directly in the lasagna sauce blend alongside Hatchomiso. Wheat is present in both the pasta layers and the sauce itself — there is no wheat-free version of any menu item.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy (béchamel/white sauce, cheese) and meat proteins are core to the menu; no vegan preparation track exists. Cross-contact with animal products is unavoidable.

cultural_comfort.note: English menus are available; the venue sits inside Kinshachi Yokocho, a tourist-oriented food complex adjacent to Nagoya Castle, designed for broad accessibility including international visitors.

places.name: 馳走菴 ひじり (Chisoan Hijiri)
places.gf_detail: Wheat-containing soy sauce (koikuchi shoyu) is a staple of Kyoto kaiseki and almost certainly present in marinades, braises, and dipping sauces. Tempura (wheat batter) appears on the à la carte menu. No gluten-free claims, tamari use, or allergen menu exist on the official site or review pages. Celiac diners should inquire directly by phone before booking; outcomes are unverified.
places.vegan_detail: Classic Kyoto kaiseki built around dashi (kombu and katsuobushi), seasonal fish, Nagoya Cochin poultry, and soft-shell turtle. Kyoto vegetables and obanzai-style simmered dishes feature prominently but are cooked in fish-based broths. No vegan course exists; advance inquiry is essential and outcomes are uncertain.
places.hours_raw: Closed Mon, Tue–Sun 17:00–23:00 (L.O. 22:30)
places.neighborhood: Higashi-ku, Higashi-kataha-chō, 8 min walk from Takaoka Stn
places.notes: Chisoan Hijiri is an intimate Kyoto-style kaiseki restaurant in Nagoya's Higashi Ward, steps from the Higashikatahata intersection. The chef trained in Kyoto before opening here, bringing authentic techniques and a personal procurement network that sources Kyoto vegetables, seaso

chef_bio.origin: Kyoto culinary tradition transplanted to Nagoya
chef_bio.background: Chisoan Hijiri is an intimate Kyoto-style kaiseki restaurant in Nagoya's Higashi Ward (東片端町), steps from the Higashikatahata intersection. The chef trained for years at a Kyoto establishment before opening here, bringing authentic Kyoto techniques and a personal procurement network that sources Kyoto vegetables, seasonal fish, and specialty items directly.
chef_bio.philosophy: Seasonal, ingredient-led cooking in a calm atmosphere modelled on Kyoto aesthetics: the interior uses lattice woodwork imported from Kyoto. The menu changes daily based on procurement, and the chef occasionally offers surprise off-menu dishes to counter guests. Listed in the Michelin Bib Gourmand.
chef_bio.specialty: Soft-shell turtle (suppon) — available essentially year-round in multiple preparations (also appearing as a complimentary chawanmushi paired with early-season matsutake) — alongside a rotating Kyoto-style kaiseki menu built on whatever the chef sources each day.

anecdotes.text: Fellow chefs praise the restaurant's Kyoto training lineage — one recommender trained together with the chef in Kyoto and later worked at the same Nagoya restaurant — and describe the plating as 'beautiful and highly original' (盛り付けがとてもきれいで独創性があり).

A food blogger describes the chef sourcing wild ayu (sweetfish) from the Wara River (和良川) in Gujo, Gifu Prefecture, salt-grilled and celebrated as 'Japan's finest ayu' (日本一美味しい鮎), reflecting the chef's reach beyond standard market channels.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Hitosara chef reviews confirm the Kyoto training lineage and praise for original plating (独創性); the official site confirms the Kyoto-imported lattice interior and a suppon course; foodmation2018 blog confirms year-round suppon and Wara-River ayu billed as Japan's finest.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available on cross-contamination controls; the kitchen prepares wheat-battered tempura and standard soy-sauce-based dishes.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Wheat-based soy sauce is standard throughout Kyoto kaiseki cooking and appears inherent to the braises, simmered dishes, and seasoning at this restaurant; no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is noted anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi made from katsuobushi (bonito flakes) is the backbone of Kyoto kaiseki; fish and poultry are central to the menu, making vegan cross-contact virtually certain without specific advance arrangement.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy-handling policy, or gluten-free accommodation is mentioned on the official site or any reviewed source; the restaurant takes reservations by phone (052-963-0073) and dietary needs should be communicated at the time of booking.

positives.text: Courses are prepared to order in small batches at a 7-seat counter, giving the chef direct visibility over every dish and some flexibility to accommodate requests if communicated well in advance.

cultural_comfort.note: Intimate counter-style kaiseki restaurant oriented toward Japanese business entertaining; the chef interacts with guests in Japanese, and the menu is entirely in Japanese with no English materials noted.

places.name: 肉うどん さんすけ (Niku Udon Sansuke)
places.gf_detail: The restaurant's core product is handmade wheat udon noodles. The broth uses Imokei tamari soy sauce (from Gifu Prefecture), which is often lower-gluten than standard shoyu, but the shop is entirely built around wheat noodles and there is no gluten-free alternative offered. Cross-contamination is certain. Not suitable for celiac diners.
places.vegan_detail: Broth is made with pork bone, chicken oil (chiyu), and animal-based dashi — no plant-based option available. Toppings include chashu pork as the centerpiece. Nothing on the menu is suitable for vegans or vegetarians.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Tue, Fri, Sat & holidays: 8:00–14:00, 17:30–20:50 / Wed, Thu: 11:00–14:00, 17:30–20:50 / Sun: 8:00–15:00 / closed over New Year period
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Owner Katō spent years training in ramen rather than udon — he worked at the legendary Ramen Jiro Mita main branch, then ran the Ramen Jiro Ōmiya offshoot for eight years before pivoting to udon in Nagoya. He opened this counter in October 2017 at age 29, channeling his ramen ped

chef_bio.background: Owner Katō Shōgo built his craft in ramen rather than udon. After about a year in an izakaya kitchen and roughly five years of ramen training — including time at the legendary Ramen Jirō Mita main branch — he opened his own Ramen Jirō Ōmiya shop in Saitama at age 29 and ran it for eight years, before pivoting to udon in Nagoya. He opened this Marunouchi counter on 17 October 2017, channeling his ramen pedigree into a bowl that straddles both worlds.
chef_bio.philosophy: Always satisfy the customer's appetite (必ずお腹を満足させる). Katō applies ramen-kitchen discipline — notably a three-day-marinated house chashu — to an udon format, producing generous portions that reviewers describe as addictive despite their richness.
chef_bio.specialty: さんすけうどん — handmade thick flat noodles in a rich, tamari-soy-forward broth (the tamari sourced from a Gifu maker) topped with house chashu and sweet-savory braised pork; the bowl reads so much like Yokohama iekei ramen that noodle-hunters do a double-take.

anecdotes.text: The shop's name, さんすけ, references Oda Nobunaga's childhood name (三介) — a playful local anchor for what is otherwise a very non-traditional udon.
A food blogger visiting in summer 2025 noted customers eating with smiles and laughter — a warm contrast to the tense, almost martial atmosphere of the Ramen Jiro style the owner came from.
The broth prompted one writer to ask 'Is this just Yokohama ie-kei ramen?' — the tamari-soy, pork-bone, chicken-oil combination reads unmistakably ramen to veteran noodle-hunters.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: The official amebaownd site gives owner 加藤省吾, opening 17 Oct 2017, the motto 必ずお腹を満足させる, a first shop opened at 29 in Ōmiya run for eight years, three-day-marinated chashu, and Gifu tamari soy. The sehensucht blog confirms the Ramen Jiro Mita/Omiya pedigree, the 三介 name origin, and the iekei-like broth.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat noodles are the restaurant's entire product; flour is present throughout the kitchen. No gluten-free alternative exists and cross-contamination is unavoidable.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The broth uses Imokei tamari soy sauce from Gifu Prefecture. Tamari is typically made with little or no wheat, but this has not been confirmed as certified gluten-free and is served in a wheat-noodle kitchen.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Pork chashu, chicken oil, and animal dashi are core broth components. There is no vegan path through the menu.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Staff are noted as patient and thorough in explaining the menu to first-time visitors, but no allergy or dietary accommodation protocols have been documented by any source.

cultural_comfort.note: Cash-only counter shop with a local-regular feel; staff are described as attentive and happy to explain the menu, but all signage and interaction is in Japanese.

places.name: グラッツィエ・ディ・クオーレ (Grazie di Cuore)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free offering is listed or advertised. All pizzas and fresh pasta are wheat-based; the restaurant's own website highlights Italian wheat flour (イタリア産小麦) as a point of pride in its pizza dough. There is no dedicated GF kitchen, no mention of GF flour alternatives, and no allergen menu. Cross-contamination from flour-heavy cooking is a real concern. The restaurant does accept allergy-substitution requests verbally, but suitability for celiac diners has not been established.
places.vegan_detail: No vegan designation. The menu is built around meat, cheese, seafood, and egg-enriched pasta (carbonara uses Nagoya Cochin egg). Organic vegetables appear throughout — salads, roasted vegetables, and sides — but no dish is explicitly vegan. The restaurant notes it accommodates ingredient-substitution requests at time of visit, so it is worth asking, but no vegan commitment is made.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Wed, Fri–Sun 11:00–14:00 (L.O. 13:30) / 17:30–22:00 (L.O. 21:00), closed Thu (open on holidays)
places.neighborhood: 5 min walk from Jōshin Stn (Nishi-ku, Kaminagoya)
places.notes: Born in Tokyo in 1982, Terasawa Takashi discovered a love of cooking during part-time work at a family restaurant chain, where he stayed for eight years. He then spent three years at a pizza specialty shop, followed by three years at an Italian restaurant, before being entrusted

chef_bio.background: Born in Tokyo in 1982, Terasawa Takashi discovered a love of cooking during part-time work at a family-restaurant chain, where he stayed for eight years. He then spent three years at a pizza specialty shop, followed by three years at an Italian restaurant, before being entrusted with the kitchen at Grazie di Cuore.
chef_bio.philosophy: 'Joshin Italian' (浄心イタリアン) — Italian food seasoned and sourced to suit Japanese palates so that diners of every age feel at home with it (日本人が食べやすい味付けや食材にした料理は若い方から年配の方にも好評).
chef_bio.specialty: Hand-made pizza on a custom blend of Italian wheat flour rested for half a day, fired to a crisp exterior and chewy interior; house-made roast beef is another signature.

anecdotes.text: Terasawa's pizzas became so sought-after by fellow restaurateurs that the shop launched a frozen-pizza wholesale line — now supplying Italian restaurants, cafes, izakayas, and bars across the region (desserts are also popular with yakiniku shops).

The chef's route into professional cooking began entirely through family-restaurant part-time work; his eight years there gave him the fundamentals before he moved to specialist pizza and Italian training.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Suchikon-tatsujin confirms chef 寺澤昴志, born Tokyo 1982, and the 8yr/3yr/3yr training path; grazie.nagoya confirms the Joshin-ku location, Japanese-palate philosophy, and (via listings) the Italian-wheat dough rested half a day; the business page confirms the frozen-pizza wholesale line.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen works extensively with Italian wheat flour for fresh pasta and pizza dough; no GF-separated preparation area or equipment is mentioned.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce-based dishes are evident on the Italian menu, but standard wheat-containing Italian ingredients (pasta dough, pizza dough) are used throughout.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Cheese, prosciutto, and animal-based ingredients appear in nearly every menu section; no vegan-safe prep line is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The restaurant states it accepts ingredient-substitution requests for allergies or dietary preferences at the time of visit (要相談).

positives.text: Organic vegetables (有機野菜) are used throughout the menu in salads, pasta, and side dishes.

cultural_comfort.note: A neighborhood Italian with local regulars and no evident English signage or menu; basic Japanese phrases will get you far, and staff are described as friendly and welcoming.

places.name: 手打ちそば 創愿 (Teuchi Soba Sougen)
places.gf_detail: The restaurant specialises in 十割そば (juwari soba) — 100% buckwheat noodles, which contain no wheat flour. This is a genuine option for gluten-avoiders. However, the standard offering also includes 二八そば (niwari, 20% wheat) and 更科そば (sarashina, also wheat-blended), so you must explicitly request the 十割. The tsuyu is made with a 20-year-old kaeshi base using shoyu from Shinshu (Nagano) — standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat. Cross-contamination risk exists because wheat-containing soba is kneaded and cut in the same kitchen. No dedicated gluten-free kitchen is claimed. Tempura is served, meaning wheat flour and shared frying oil are present. Suitable for gluten-preference only, not celiac safety.
places.vegan_detail: Soba noodles themselves can be ordered without animal products, but the tsuyu (dipping broth) is almost certainly based on katsuobushi (bonito) and/or conventional Japanese dashi — no vegan broth option is advertised. Signature dishes include kamo-nanban (duck broth) and pork dipping soba; tempura is on the menu. Vegans should call ahead to ask whether a plain cold soba with no dipping sauce or a substitute is possible.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Fri 11:00–15:00 (L.O. 14:30), 17:30–21:00 (L.O. 20:00), Sat 11:00–14:30 (L.O. 14:00), 17:30–20:30, closed Sun, holidays, and Mondays following a holiday
places.neighborhood: Naka-ku, Marunouchi / Hisaya-ōdōri
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened on 27 March 2006 in a ground-floor space of the Nakabun Building in Nagoya's Marunouchi office district. The owner-operator hand-rolls every portion of soba each morning using stone-milled buckwheat ground fresh daily. The shop's kaeshi (soy-sauce reduction that forms the

chef_bio.background: Opened on 27 March 2006 on the ground floor of the Nakabun Building in Nagoya's Marunouchi office district (5 minutes from Hisaya-odori Station). The owner-operator hand-rolls every portion of soba each morning using stone-milled buckwheat ground fresh daily. The shop's kaeshi (the soy-sauce base of the tsuyu) has been continuously replenished since opening and has now reached about 20 years of age.
chef_bio.philosophy: The owner selects buckwheat and adjusts the milling to coax the maximum aroma and flavour from the grain itself, offering both juwari (100% buckwheat) and delicate sarashina. The tsuyu is built on a single carefully chosen Shinshu (Nagano) shoyu that has not changed since the shop opened.
chef_bio.specialty: Juwari soba (十割そば) — 100% buckwheat noodles; also kamo-nanban (duck) dipping soba and seasonal tempura

anecdotes.text: The shop's kaeshi has been continuously replenished since the 2006 opening and now spans roughly 20 years — a hallmark of a traditional soba-ya — paired with a single Shinshu shoyu unchanged since day one.
An early food-blog review noted that 100% buckwheat soba was available as a small (¥100) upcharge over the standard nihachi, with pork and duck dipping broths as the signature warm options.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog confirms the 27 Mar 2006 opening, Nakabun Bldg location, self-milled juwari/sarashina and duck-broth soba; the official about page (indexed) confirms the unchanged Shinshu shoyu and a ~20-year kaeshi; the ameblo blog confirms the ¥100 juwari upcharge and pork/duck dipping broths.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat-containing niwari (80% buckwheat / 20% wheat) and sarashina soba are made in the same kitchen as the juwari (100% buckwheat) soba; shared surfaces and equipment are likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The tsuyu is built on a 20-year-old kaeshi made with Shinshu shoyu — standard Japanese soy sauce brewed with wheat. All broth-based and dipping dishes will contain wheat-derived soy sauce.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Tempura is on the menu, meaning wheat-batter frying takes place in the kitchen. Duck (kamo) and pork dipping broths are signature items, so animal products are central to the cooking environment.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy statement, or staff allergy-handling protocol has been published on any source reviewed. Guests with serious allergies should call 052-951-1226 before visiting.

positives.text: Juwari soba (十割そば, 100% buckwheat, no wheat flour in the noodle itself) is a standing menu option and represents a genuine wheat-free noodle choice for those avoiding gluten by preference.

cultural_comfort.note: A quiet, polished soba-ya in a business district with no English signage noted; an English menu is reportedly available, but interaction will be primarily in Japanese.

places.name: 手打蕎麦 ふたば (Teuchi Soba Futaba)
places.gf_detail: The menu includes both 二八そば (nihachi: 80% buckwheat, 20% wheat — not gluten-free) and 十割そば (juwari: 100% buckwheat — wheat-free as a flour). However, this is a general soba kitchen with no dedicated gluten-free protocol confirmed. The dashi broth uses bonito (likely with standard soy sauce containing wheat). Tempura is also on the menu, introducing wheat batter and shared-fryer risk. Celiacs should call ahead; the juwari soba is the only potentially lower-wheat option and cross-contamination cannot be ruled out.
places.vegan_detail: The dashi stock is bonito-based (katsuo dashi), making the standard soba broth non-vegan. The tsuyu served with cold seiro soba is also fish-based. Duck dishes are on the menu. Vegetable tempura options exist but share the kitchen. No plant-based broth alternative is mentioned anywhere. Vegans should ask if a konbu-only dashi is available; assume not unless confirmed.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun 11:00–14:30 / 18:00–21:00, closed Wed, also closed the 3rd Thursday of each month
places.neighborhood: 浅間町 / 押切 (Nishi Ward)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] The menu includes both 二八そば (nihachi: 80% buckwheat, 20% wheat — not gluten-free) and 十割そば (juwari: 100% buckwheat — wheat-free as a flour). However, this is a general soba kitchen with no dedicated gluten-free protocol confirmed. The dashi broth uses bonito (likely with standard

chef_bio.philosophy: A calm, unhurried neighborhood soba shop where the noodles are rolled fresh daily and the atmosphere encourages lingering over soba-yu at the end of the meal.
chef_bio.specialty: Hand-rolled nihachi (80/20 buckwheat-wheat) and juwari (100% buckwheat) seiro soba, cut thin with a firm bite; duck-nanban seiro and tempura seiro (shrimp and vegetable) are the standout dishes.

anecdotes.text: Despite being a small shop (about six seats), diners line up at the door by the 6 pm dinner opening — a reliable sign of neighborhood loyalty.
A soba researcher visiting in 2024 noted the mild, pale-coloured broth and hand-rolled noodles at roughly 130–140 g per serving, and left wishing they had asked about the buckwheat's origin.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Nihon-soba.jp (2024) confirms nihachi/juwari seiro, duck-nanban and tempura seiro, the mild pale broth, ~130-140g thin firm noodles, the 6pm dinner opening, six seats, and that the reviewer didn't learn the buckwheat origin; 4travel corroborates the queue at opening.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area is documented. The kitchen handles both wheat-containing nihachi noodles and tempura batter alongside the 100% buckwheat juwari soba, making cross-contamination a realistic concern for celiacs.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The tsuyu dipping broth and kakejiru hot broth are almost certainly made with standard soy sauce (which contains wheat) and bonito dashi — no wheat-free tamari alternative is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bonito-based dashi is the standard broth throughout; duck dishes are prepared in the same kitchen. No plant-based alternative broth has been confirmed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen handling procedures or staff allergy awareness information has been published for this restaurant. Phone ahead (052-522-5660) for any dietary requirements.

positives.text: The juwari (十割) seiro uses 100% buckwheat flour with no wheat added to the noodle itself — the safest option on the menu for those avoiding wheat in the noodle strand, subject to kitchen cross-contamination caveats.

cultural_comfort.note: A quiet neighborhood soba-ya with no English signage or menu noted; a few Japanese phrases for ordering or pointing at the menu is enough to get by.

places.name: 濃厚中華そば 佐とう (Noko Chuka Soba Sato)
places.gf_detail: The broth is built on tamari soy sauce — even tamari often contains trace wheat in Japanese commercial production. No gluten-free claims or allergen accommodations are mentioned anywhere. The noodles themselves contain wheat. No dedicated fryer information available as this is a noodle shop. Not safe for celiac diners.
places.vegan_detail: The broth is a pork bone and pork meat (豚骨・豚肉) base seasoned with tamari soy sauce. Chashu (braised pork belly) is the signature topping. No vegan or vegetarian options mentioned. Not suitable for vegans or vegetarians.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Fri 11:00–14:00, 17:30–20:30 (closes once soup runs out) / Sat & holidays 11:00–14:00 / closed Sun and the 2nd Saturday
places.neighborhood: Naka-ku, Marunouchi / 3 min walk from Marunouchi Stn
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened in October 2018 in Nagoya's Marunouchi business district, Noko Chuka Soba Sato was born from a bold genre pivot: the owner had previously run a popular donburi (rice bowl) restaurant called Le Granbowl at or near the same location, building deep expertise in meat preparati

chef_bio.background: Opened in October 2018 in Nagoya's Marunouchi business district, Noko Chuka Soba Sato was born from a bold genre pivot: the same premises had housed the owner's popular donburi (rice-bowl) restaurant, Le Granbowl (れ・ぐらんぼる), where he built deep expertise in meat preparation before committing fully to ramen. It is a small (~16-seat) counter-focused shop.
chef_bio.philosophy: Despite the dramatic visual of a meat-piled bowl, the broth is treated as the centerpiece — a concentrated pork-based stock seasoned with dark tamari soy and finished with subtle garlic and sweet notes — with the toppings as supporting cast.
chef_bio.specialty: 濃厚チャーシュー中華そば — a rich tamari-soy broth loaded with roughly ten thin slices of precisely prepared pork belly arranged in a flower-like spread across the bowl, over medium-thick, slightly firm noodles.

anecdotes.text: The shop transformed from a donburi specialist (Le Granbowl) into a ramen destination, carrying over the owner's confident meat-butchery skill into the chashu preparation that now defines the bowl.
A table condiment of kombu vinegar (昆布酢) is provided to cut the richness of the broth and add umami — an unusual and praised touch that regulars use to shift the flavour mid-bowl.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Jouhou.nagoya confirms the 11 Oct 2018 opening in Marunouchi, ~16 seats, the Le Granbowl donburi predecessor, the tamari broth and decoratively piled chashu; nagoya-meshi confirms roughly ten flower-arranged pork-belly slices and the kombu-vinegar condiment.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No gluten-free accommodations exist. Wheat noodles are the core product cooked in the same kitchen, making cross-contamination certain.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Broth is seasoned with tamari soy sauce; tamari in Japanese commercial production frequently contains trace wheat, and no wheat-free tamari claim is made.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All broth is pork-based; no plant-based or separate cooking line exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling protocols or allergen menus are mentioned on any source reviewed. Phone lines are closed during service hours.
