cultural_comfort.note: Counter-style ramen shop serving a Japanese business-district crowd; no English menu or staff noted, but ordering is straightforward with a short menu.

places.name: 楽いち (Rakuichi)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free claims or accommodations found. The tare (seasoning sauce) in tonkotsu ramen is typically wheat-based soy sauce, making the broth unsafe for celiac diners. Noodles are standard wheat noodles. Gyoza and other sides contain wheat. Cross-contamination risk is high in this tiny open counter kitchen. Not suitable for gluten-free diners.
places.vegan_detail: The broth is tonkotsu (pork bone) and the tare almost certainly contains soy sauce. Chashu pork is a standard topping. No plant-based options are listed. This shop is not suitable for vegans.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sat 11:30–14:00, 18:00–23:00 / closed Mon and Sun
places.neighborhood: Meijō Park / Nishi-ku, Kaminagoya (~8 min walk from Meijō Kōen Stn)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A small, hidden-gem counter ramen shop tucked in a quiet residential street in Nishi Ward, close to Meijo Park. Reviewers describe it as a place that feels more like a neighbourhood izakaya than a ramen shop, with regulars ordering beer and appetisers alongside bowls.

chef_bio.background: A small, hidden-gem counter ramen shop (about ten seats) tucked in a quiet part of Nishi Ward near a driving school, close to Meijo Park. The proprietor runs it solo, and reviewers describe it as feeling more like a neighbourhood izakaya than a ramen shop, with regulars ordering beer and appetisers alongside bowls.
chef_bio.philosophy: Unpretentious neighbourhood comfort — the worn exterior belies a welcoming, easy atmosphere where solo diners and locals linger.
chef_bio.specialty: Tonkotsu ramen with a light, aromatic-oil-enriched pork-bone broth; the house 'Rakuichi-aji' (楽いち味, a lighter tonkotsu) and 'Chotteri-aji' (ちょってり味 — slightly rich) styles are the calling cards, served with a choice of thin or medium-thick noodles; gyoza and vegetable tempura round out the menu.
chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog reviews confirm the Nishi-ku location ~590m from Meijo-Park Station, the light aromatic tonkotsu broth, the 楽いち味 and ちょってり味 styles, thin/medium-thick noodle choice, ~10 counter seats, a solo owner, and the beer-and-appetizer izakaya feel.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available. Open counter kitchen in a 10-seat shop makes cross-contamination highly likely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tonkotsu tare in Japanese ramen shops standardly contains wheat-based soy sauce; no wheat-free tare is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All broth and toppings are pork-based; no plant-based lane exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling information found; the shop has no online menu disclosures and is cash-only with no reservation system, making advance allergy communication difficult.

cultural_comfort.note: A tiny 10-seat counter shop with a convivial but Japanese-language-only environment; the owner is friendly, but no English signage or menus are evident.

places.name: 焼鳥 あらい (Yakitori Arai)
places.gf_detail: Standard yakitori tare (sauce) almost universally contains wheat-based soy sauce. No gluten-free menu, tamari substitution, or allergy accommodation is documented anywhere across Tabelog, HotPepper, or review sources. Some plain salt-grilled (shio) skewers could theoretically be lower-risk, but cross-contamination on shared grills is unaddressed. Celiac diners should call ahead and confirm soy sauce type and grill sharing practices before visiting.
places.vegan_detail: A dedicated yakitori specialist built around charcoal-grilled chicken and meat skewers. No plant-based or vegan options are listed on any reviewed source. Hidden animal content (chicken fat, dashi) is likely throughout.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Thu, Fri 17:00–00:00 (next day) (food L.O. 23:00, drink L.O. 23:30), Sat/Sun/holidays 17:00–23:00 (food L.O. 22:00, drink L.O. 22:30), closed Tue and Wed
places.neighborhood: 浅間町 (Asama-cho), 西区, 名古屋
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Standard yakitori tare (sauce) almost universally contains wheat-based soy sauce. No gluten-free menu, tamari substitution, or allergy accommodation is documented anywhere across Tabelog, HotPepper, or review sources. Some plain salt-grilled (shio) skewers could theoretically be

chef_bio.origin: Nagoya (Nishi-ku, near Asama-cho Station) — opened 20 November 2024
chef_bio.specialty: Charcoal-grilled yakitori featuring Nagoya Cochin (名古屋コーチン), a prized heritage chicken breed local to Aichi Prefecture, paired with an abundant selection of Japanese sake.

anecdotes.text: Opened 20 November 2024 in a quiet neighbourhood near Asama-cho Station, just west of Nagoya Castle. Reviewers found the quality-to-price ratio almost too generous — one calling the food 'delicious in one word, far superior to other yakitori shops' — with early praise for chicken heart (hatsu) and the springy, moist Nagoya Cochin.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog reviews confirm the 20 Nov 2024 opening, the location near Asama-cho Station, Nagoya Cochin, an abundant sake list, praise for value ('far superior to other yakitori shops'), and standout hatsu (heart); oinagoya corroborates the recent opening, Nagoya Cochin and sake range.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shared charcoal grill used for all skewers; no documentation of separate grill sections or protocols for allergen separation.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard yakitori tare typically contains wheat-based soy sauce; no tamari or gluten-free alternative is documented for this restaurant.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All documented menu items are meat-based; animal fat and drippings on a shared grill make vegan cross-contact a near-certainty.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information found on staff training for allergy handling; no reviews mention dietary accommodation requests being made or fulfilled.

cultural_comfort.note: A neighbourhood yakitori bar with a counter atmosphere and Japanese sake focus; English menu or English-speaking staff are not documented, so some Japanese or translation help is advisable.

places.name: 中華そば 白壁 あおい (Chuka Soba Shirakabe Aoi)
places.gf_detail: Wheat is central to this restaurant: house-made thin wavy noodles contain wheat, and the shoyu tare almost certainly contains wheat (standard Japanese soy sauce). No gluten-free noodle alternative or allergen accommodation is offered or mentioned anywhere. The mazesoba and all other menu items similarly contain wheat noodles. Not safe for celiac or wheat allergy.
places.vegan_detail: The broth is built on animal stock — chicken carcass, pork bone, and beef — with bonito (katsuobushi) dashi also referenced by reviewers. Signature chashu pork is a standard topping. There are no plant-based options on the menu. Not suitable for vegans.
places.hours_raw: Tue, Wed, Fri, Sat, Sun & holidays 11:30–14:00, 18:00–21:30 / closed Mon and Thu
places.neighborhood: Higashi-ōte Stn / Shirakabe / northeast of Nagoya Castle
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] The shop was founded in 1997 by the Sawa family in the Shirakabe district northeast of Nagoya Castle. Ryuichiro Sawa inherited it from his parents and continues to run it today. He is also the founder of the Fujiyama 55 tsukemen chain, but this original family counter is where hi

chef_bio.origin: Hida-Takayama, Gifu Prefecture (culinary inspiration); shop in Nagoya's Shirakabe neighbourhood since 1997
chef_bio.background: The shop was founded in 1997 by the Sawa family in the Shirakabe district northeast of Nagoya Castle. Ryuichiro Sawa took it over from his parents at age 35 and continues to run it today. He is also the founder of the Fujiyama 55 tsukemen chain (now 55style Co.), but this original family counter — an eight-seat, cash-only shop — is where his roots lie.
chef_bio.philosophy: Faithfully carrying on the gentle, deeply savory Takayama-style ramen the shop's first generation created. Sawa preserves the inherited chuka-soba recipe while adding his own items (miso ramen, maze-soba) over the years.
chef_bio.specialty: Takayama-style shoyu ramen — a clear soy broth drawn from chicken bones, pork and beef, served with thin, house-made high-hydration wavy noodles

anecdotes.text: The shop's Takayama-style recipe traces to its first-generation founders; reviewers recount an obsession with a Hida-Takayama taste that could not be forgotten, and repeated trips to Takayama to study and perfect the style — lore reflected in the broth's distinctive sweetness and depth.

Despite a dark-coloured shoyu broth, the soup delivers a gentle, not harsh flavour — reviewers consistently note the appearance is more assertive than the taste, which is soft and umami-rich (animal stock of 鶏ガラ, トンコツ, 牛肉).

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog and aunblog confirm the 1997 founding, the first-generation Takayama recipe now inherited, house-made high-hydration noodles, and the dark-but-gentle chicken/pork/beef broth; 55style/Fujiyama55 and the aunblog confirm owner 澤竜一郎 inherited the shop from his parents at 35 and founded Fujiyama 55.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (shoyu) contains wheat; this restaurant uses shoyu tare in its broth and no tamari or wheat-free alternative is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All broth is animal-based (chicken, pork, beef, and bonito); no plant-based cooking zone exists in this tiny 8-seat counter kitchen.

cultural_comfort.note: Tiny 8-seat counter shop with no English signage or menu; ordering is straightforward but a point at the menu approach is needed for non-Japanese speakers.

places.name: オステリア オルモ (Osteria OLMO)
places.gf_detail: Italian cuisine in which pasta (wheat flour containing gluten) is central. Many wheat-using items — semolina fritto, bucatini, gnocchi and more. Use of soy sauce, dashi, etc. is unknown. Guests are advised that with notice given by two days in advance the restaurant will consider accommodating allergies, but there is no information on a dedicated gluten-free menu or cross-contamination prevention. Celiacs must confirm details directly in advance.
places.vegan_detail: No fully vegan menu. Centered on Italian cuisine that makes heavy use of meat, fish and dairy (eel, guinea fowl, stracciatella cheese, etc.). It is noted that dietary requests conveyed by two days in advance may be accommodated ('dislikes/avoided ingredients up to two days ahead'). However, the extent of accommodation depends on how the course can be altered.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sat 18:00–23:00 (L.O. 21:00), closed Sun and Mon. Irregular temporary closures and occasional Sun/Mon opening (announced on Instagram).
places.neighborhood: Nagono (Nishi Ward) / 6 min walk from Kokusai Center Stn
places.notes: Owner-chef Kazuya Sato trained at Aroma Fresca in Ginza and elsewhere before opening this restaurant in Nagono, Nagoya in November 2014. The space is a renovated machiya townhouse built in 1941, standing along Nagoya's 'Shikemichi,' characterized by a warm, wood-based atmosphere.

roles: オーナーシェフ（佐藤一也）
chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市西区那古野、2014年11月開業
chef_bio.background: オーナーシェフの佐藤一也氏は、銀座のアロマフレスカ（Aroma Fresca）や名古屋の個人店などで研鑽を積んだのち、東京での修業を経て名古屋に移り、2014年11月に那古野に当店を開いた。西区の「式見道（しけ道）」沿いに建つ1941年（昭和16年）築の町家を改装した空間で、当時の建具を活かした木を基調とする温かみのある雰囲気が特徴。
chef_bio.philosophy: 「日本の恵みを感じる一皿」を追求しつつ、あくまでイタリア料理の技法・エッセンスを損なわない料理づくりを信条とする。単なる「和イタリアン」に陥らない独自性を意識し、自然派ワインと合わせて提供する。
chef_bio.specialty: 日本の旬食材を用いたイタリア料理。市場の鮮魚・肉や、全国の農家から直接仕入れる自然栽培野菜を惜しみなく使い、香りと温度にこだわった一皿を提供する。

anecdotes.text: 店名「OLMO」は、店側の説明ではイタリア語で「ケヤキ（欅）」を指す言葉とされ、欅のテーブルを囲んで温かいイタリア料理と自然派ワインを楽しむという、土地に根ざした料理への意志が込められている。
ヒトサラに寄稿した推薦シェフは「カジュアルながらも非常に繊細で美味しい。関東の有名店で修業したシェフが作り出す季節ごとのメニューと、細部に至る丁寧な仕込みが随所に感じられる」と評している。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: WEB大人の名古屋 and Gault&Millau confirm chef 佐藤一也, Nov 2014 opening, the 1941 machiya on Shikendo in Nagono, Tokyo (incl. Ginza Aroma Fresca) training, the Japanese-ingredient-forward-but-not-wa-Italian philosophy, natural wine, and the shop's stated OLMO=keyaki name-story. No published sommelier named 愛さん could be verified, so that role was removed.

gf_cross_contamination.text: 専用グルテンフリー調理器具・フライヤーの分離使用に関する情報は公式サイト・各種レビューとも一切記載なし。パスタ・セモリナ粉・小麦フリットを多用するキッチンでの交差汚染リスクは高いと考えられる。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 醤油・だし（小麦含有の可能性）の使用有無についての情報は公開されていない。イタリア料理が主体だが、日本の旬食材を多用するため隠し味として使用している可能性を排除できない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 乳製品（ストラッチャテッラ、ゴートチーズ等）・魚介・肉を同一キッチンで扱う。ヴィーガン専用調理ラインの情報なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 「アレルギーや苦手食材は2日前までにお知らせいただければ対応します」と案内されている（食べログ情報）。対応の具体的範囲（代替食材の内容・除去の確実性等）は不明。セリアックや重篤なアレルギーの方は予約時に直接詳細を確認することを強く推奨。

positives.text: 完全予約制のため、事前にアレルギー・食材の希望を伝える機会が確保されている。少人数制（15席）で、シェフが一人ひとりの状況に配慮しやすい環境。

cultural_comfort.note: 完全予約制の高級イタリアン。日本語での事前コミュニケーション（アレルギー等の申告）が必須で、英語対応の確認情報はない。

places.name: 丸の内蕎麦 (Marunouchi Soba)
places.gf_detail: Traditional soba tsuyu is made with wheat-containing soy sauce (醤油). Tanuki soba — the signature menu item — is topped with tenkasu (tempura batter scraps), which are fried in oil shared with wheat-battered tempura. Buckwheat soba noodles can contain wheat depending on blend (no juwari/100% soba confirmed here). No GF accommodation or awareness indicated at this casual ticket-machine shop. Not appropriate for celiac diners.
places.vegan_detail: The broth is described as 和風出汁 (Japanese-style dashi), which at a standard casual soba shop of this type is virtually always made with katsuobushi (bonito flakes). No vegan-friendly broth option is indicated anywhere. Menu items include meat soba (肉そば). This place is not suitable for vegans.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Fri 11:00–14:15 (L.O. 14:15), 17:30–20:30 (L.O. 20:00); closed Sat/Sun/holidays
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi / around Nagoya Castle
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Traditional soba tsuyu is made with wheat-containing soy sauce (醤油). Tanuki soba — the signature menu item — is topped with tenkasu (tempura batter scraps), which are fried in oil shared with wheat-battered tempura. Buckwheat soba noodles can contain wheat depending on blend (no

chef_bio.specialty: たぬき蕎麦（天かす入り温かいそば）

anecdotes.text: シンプル イズ ベスト をコンセプトに、丸の内のオフィス街で働く人や近隣（東京福祉大学など）の学生といったランチ客に、しっかりとした和風出汁の温かいそばを500〜750円台で提供。券売機で食券を購入するスタイルが手軽さを演出している。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Okazakimonape confirms the ticket-machine ordering, tanuki soba at ¥500, a ¥500-750 range, a tasty wafu dashi, the 'simple is best' concept, and a clientele of Marunouchi workers and nearby students (Tokyo Fukushi University).

gf_cross_contamination.text: たぬき蕎麦のトッピングである天かす（揚げ玉）は小麦粉の天ぷらバッターと同じ油で揚げられており、グルテン交差汚染のリスクが高い。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: そばつゆには通常の醤油（小麦含有）が使用されており、GF対応の代替醤油の情報は確認されていない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: だしは和風出汁（鰹節ベースと推定）であり、植物性ブロスの提供は確認されていない。肉そばメニューも存在する。

cultural_comfort.note: 券売機で食券を購入する完全日本語運用の立ち食いスタイル；英語メニューや英語対応スタッフの存在は確認されていない。

places.name: La・麺喰亭 (La Menkuitei)
places.gf_detail: Standard ramen shop using wheat noodles. Soy sauce in the broth is almost certainly wheat-containing (standard Japanese shoyu). No gluten-free noodle alternatives, dedicated fryer, or allergen accommodation mentioned in any source. Gyoza wrappers are wheat-based. Not suitable for celiac diners.
places.vegan_detail: All broths are animal-based: chicken clear broth or tonkotsu (pork bone). No plant-based broth options found. Toppings include chicken chashu and pork chashu. Gyoza are also on the menu. No vegan options identified across any source reviewed.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 11:30–14:30, 18:00–23:00; Sun & holidays 11:30–15:00, 17:30–22:00. Closures listed as irregular on Tabelog; one source (nagoya-meshi.com) cited Wednesday closures but this may be outdated.
places.neighborhood: 東大手・東区 (Higashioote, Higashi Ward)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] La・麺喰亭 opened on June 5, 2013 in the quiet Shomokucho neighborhood of Nagoya's Higashi Ward, a short walk from Higashioote Station. It operates as a small independent counter ramen shop with approximately 20 seats.

chef_bio.background: La・麺喰亭 opened on 5 June 2013 in the quiet Shomokucho neighborhood of Nagoya's Higashi Ward, a short walk from Higashioote Station. It operates as a small independent counter ramen shop.
chef_bio.philosophy: The kitchen favors a health-conscious, low-oil approach: reviewers consistently note you can finish the soup without heaviness. Lunch sets include complimentary rice and pickles, and affordable add-ons (chashu rice bowls, gyoza) keep the experience accessible.
chef_bio.specialty: 鶏レモンラーメン (Chicken Lemon Ramen, ¥870) — a salt-based clear broth infused with chicken umami, topped with sliced lemon, tender chicken chashu, and white scallion. The broth is intentionally low-oil, emphasizing clean chicken flavour with a bright citrus lift. A second broth option — tonkotsu (pork bone) — is available for those preferring a richer bowl.

anecdotes.text: One reviewer described the lemon ramen as 'not just novelty — it was genuinely delicious,' noting that the acidity complements rather than overwhelms the simple broth, making it ramen you'd want to eat endlessly.
The shop is described by regulars as 'a cozy place where you can chat about ramen with the friendly owner,' signaling an engaged, hands-on proprietor running a tight counter operation.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog confirms the 5 Jun 2013 opening, Shomokucho/Higashi-ku location near Higashiote, cash-only counter operation and 'friendly owner' feel; nagoya-meshi confirms the 鶏レモンラーメン at ¥870, salt-based low-oil clear chicken broth with lemon and chicken chashu, the tonkotsu alternative, and lunch rice/pickles.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese shoyu (wheat-containing soy sauce) is used in ramen broths at this type of shop; no tamari or wheat-free alternative is mentioned in any source.

cultural_comfort.note: Counter-only ramen shop with Japanese-language menus displayed above the kitchen; no English signage noted, but solo dining is common and the ordering process is straightforward point-and-pay at the counter.

places.name: 焼鳥ごりょう (Yakitori Goryo)
places.gf_detail: No GF claims found anywhere. Standard yakitori tare (basting sauce) and shoyu-based seasonings almost certainly use wheat-containing soy sauce. The course includes a chicken dashi soup whose thickeners and seasonings are unknown. A yakitori kitchen grilling whole chickens over charcoal has no dedicated fryer risk, but cross-contact from shared utensils and sauces is a real concern. Guests with celiac disease must contact the restaurant directly before booking to ask about soy sauce type and tare ingredients.
places.vegan_detail: The entire menu is chicken-only omakase. Courses include chicken dashi soup, multiple grilled chicken cuts, and ikura (salmon roe) on the finishing rice dish. No plant-based alternatives exist and there is no indication the kitchen can accommodate vegan requests.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat & holidays 17:30–22:30 (food L.O. 21:30, drink L.O. 22:00), closed Sun
places.neighborhood: 那古野・四間道 (Nagono / Shikemichi), 国際センター駅2番出口より徒歩3分
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] 焼鳥ごりょう opened in July 2024 in a renovated historic townhouse in Nagono (那古野), one of Nagoya's most atmospheric old districts near Shikemichi. It is operated by 合資会社宮治 (Miyaji Partnership), a Nagoya-based hospitality company. The 12-seat counter-only space evokes the intimacy of a

chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市西区那古野・四間道エリア
chef_bio.background: 焼鳥ごりょう opened in July 2024 (July 9) in a renovated historic townhouse in Nagono (那古野), one of Nagoya's most atmospheric old districts near Shikemichi, a few minutes from Kokusai Center Station. It is operated by 合資会社宮治 (Miyaji Partnership), a Nagoya-based hospitality company. The 12-seat counter-only space evokes the intimacy of a high-end sushi bar, and the kitchen works exclusively with whole female Mikawa red chickens (三河赤鶏の雌) — butchering only as many birds as the day's reservations require.
chef_bio.philosophy: Live, waste-nothing whole-bird cookery: every seat is booked before a single chicken is ordered, ensuring absolute freshness. The open counter lets diners watch the entire process from raw bird to finished skewer over charcoal.
chef_bio.specialty: Omakase yakitori course built around whole-bird Mikawa red chicken, including rare cuts such as uchi-momo (inner thigh, one per bird), furisode (wing tip), and bonjiri (boneless tail), finished with kinoko takikomi rice and ikura.

anecdotes.text: On Monday and Thursday evenings younger staff take the counter at a discounted ¥5,000 (later ¥5,500) 若手応援 course — a deliberate training programme that regulars use to discover emerging talent.

Reviewers noted that staff come outside to greet arriving guests, and that some visits ended with a small souvenir (お土産) from the kitchen — touches that give the place a hospitality ethos beyond mere grilling.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 合資会社宮治の公式サイトと食べログ口コミが、2024年7月開業、那古野・四間道の古民家改装、カウンター12席のみ、予約分だけ三河赤鶏の雌の丸鶏を捌く方式、月・木限定の若手応援コース5,000円、きのこ炊き込みご飯といくらの締めを裏付ける。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on fryer use (no fried items seen in menus), but shared charcoal grills, tare basting sauce, and seasoning bottles are common cross-contact vectors in a yakitori kitchen. No GF protocol is documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce type (tamari vs. wheat-containing) is not disclosed anywhere. Standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat; yakitori tare is typically soy-sauce-based. Assume wheat present until confirmed otherwise.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is animal-based (chicken, salmon roe, beer/wine). There is no vegan pathway and therefore no relevant cross-contact mitigation to assess.

staff_allergy_handling.text: A Tabelog review notes staff asked 'Is rare chicken (sasami) acceptable?' before serving, suggesting some attentiveness to individual needs, but no formal allergy protocol or allergen menu has been published.

positives.text: No deep-frying observed in the menu; whole-bird charcoal grilling with no batter or breading on skewers is the core technique, which limits some wheat exposure vectors compared to a mixed-menu izakaya.

cultural_comfort.note: Reservation-only 12-seat counter experience conducted entirely in Japanese; staff greet guests outside and the intimate omakase format assumes familiarity with the rhythm of a Japanese counter.

places.name: ウッタムカレー 丸の内店 (Uttam Curry Marunouchi)
places.gf_detail: Indian curry sauces at this type of restaurant are typically flour-free, but naan is wheat-based and baked in a shared tandoor oven. Rice is available as a naan substitute. No gluten-free labeling, dedicated procedures, or allergy handling information is published anywhere for this restaurant. Soy sauce is not a typical ingredient in Indian/Nepalese curry, but the kitchen's practices regarding cross-contact are unknown. Anyone with celiac disease should phone ahead before visiting.
places.vegan_detail: Several plant-based curry choices — vegetable curry, chickpea masala, bean curry, spinach-potato curry, eggplant dry curry — and a vegetable Indian fried rice. However, naan contains dairy (ghee/butter), and there is no stated vegan certification or confirmed dairy-free preparation. Curries appear to be prepared without fish-based dashi or bonito, consistent with South Asian cooking tradition, but no explicit allergen information is published and cross-contact with dairy in the tandoor oven cannot be ruled out. Ask staff to confirm before ordering.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–14:30 / 17:00–22:00 daily; open daily, no fixed holidays
places.neighborhood: Naka-ku, Marunouchi
places.notes: Uttam Curry opened its Marunouchi branch in December 2013, bringing a compact eatery-style Indian and Nepalese curry shop to the office district just south of Nagoya Castle. It is part of a small multi-location operation — a predecessor Kokusai Center location predates it. Review

chef_bio.background: Uttam Curry opened its Marunouchi branch around December 2013 (a December 2013 review describes it as a newly-opened Indian restaurant in Marunouchi), bringing a compact eatery-style Indian and Nepalese curry shop to the office district just south of Nagoya Castle. It is part of a small multi-location operation — a predecessor Kokusai Center location predates it.
chef_bio.philosophy: Spice levels are kept mild by default to suit Japanese palates, with customization available on request — the stated aim is that everyone from children to adults can enjoy curry.
chef_bio.specialty: Large hand-stretched naan baked to order in a tandoor oven, paired with a wide range of curries spanning chicken masala, vegetable and spinach-shrimp options, plus Nepalese dishes; the choice curry lunch set at ¥790 is the calling-card order for the weekday office crowd.

anecdotes.text: The original Uttam Curry location reportedly sold authentic chicken curry and naan for ¥500 at a community event in Nishi Ward, which is how one long-time reviewer first discovered the restaurant before it expanded to Marunouchi.
Weekday lunch service is consistently full; a 2025 reviewer noted the restaurant offers one free extra naan (追加一枚無料) with the lunch set, a touch that has earned repeat visitors.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログのランチメニューと口コミが、丸の内駅徒歩5分、チョイスセット790円、ナン追加一枚無料、平日ランチ満席を裏付け。別口コミが西区のイベントで500円のチキンカレー＆ナンを販売していた原点と国際センターの旧店を伝える。ヒトサラはネパール料理も提供と記載。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Naan is wheat-based and baked in a shared tandoor oven used for all breads and tandoori items; no separate GF baking surface or protocol is mentioned anywhere.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce is listed in the menu descriptions; Indian and Nepalese curry recipes at this restaurant do not appear to use soy sauce, but no allergen statement is published and kitchen practices are unconfirmed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy (ghee/butter) is used in naan and likely in tandoori dishes; the tandoor oven is shared. Vegan curries are not labeled as prepared separately from dairy-containing items.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy protocol, or dietary accommodation procedure is published. One reviewer noted some language barrier with staff. Phone inquiry (052-202-0177) before visiting is advised for any serious dietary restriction.

positives.text: Multiple vegetable curries are available daily — vegetable curry, chickpea masala, bean curry, spinach-potato, and eggplant dry curry — giving plant-based diners real choices without relying on meat-free adaptations.
Rice is available as a substitute for naan at no stated extra charge, providing a wheat-free carb option if the curry sauce itself is flour-free.

cultural_comfort.note: Nepalese-run restaurant with Japanese-language menus; staff are friendly though reviewers note some language barrier — pointing at the menu works fine, and service is welcoming to all diners.

places.name: あんかけ太郎 名古屋城金シャチ横丁店 (Ankake Taro Nagoya Castle Kinshachi Yokocho)
places.gf_detail: Ankake spaghetti is fundamentally a wheat-pasta dish — gluten is the core ingredient. The sauce also typically contains Worcestershire sauce, which includes wheat-derived vinegar and malt, and bouillon products that may carry gluten. No gluten-free pasta option is offered or mentioned anywhere on the official site or third-party listings. This spot is not suitable for anyone avoiding gluten.
places.vegan_detail: Ankake spaghetti as served here is not vegan. The signature sauce is built on Worcestershire sauce (which contains anchovies), and the noodles are traditionally coated in lard before saucing. Meat toppings (sausage, bacon, minced meat) are standard across most menu items. A "Japonaise" style listed on the broader menu uses soy-dashi seasoning, adding another non-vegan element. No plant-based alternatives are offered or advertised.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–15:30 (L.O.) / 17:30–21:00, open daily (except Dec 31–Jan 1)
places.neighborhood: Nagoya Castle, Kinshachi Yokochō Muneharu Zone
places.notes: Ankake Taro is a Nagoya-born operator dedicated to a single dish: ankake spaghetti, the city's beloved comfort-food oddity in which fat spaghetti noodles are smothered in a thick, spiced tomato-Worcestershire gravy. The brand runs five locations across Naka Ward, and this outpost

chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市中区（名古屋城エリア）
chef_bio.background: Ankake Taro is a Nagoya-born operator dedicated to a single dish: ankake spaghetti, the city's beloved comfort-food oddity in which fat spaghetti noodles are smothered in a thick, spiced tomato-Worcestershire gravy. The brand runs five locations across central Nagoya (Chojamachi, Sakae 4-chome, this castle outpost, Meieki Sun Road, and Terrasse Nayabashi), and this branch — the fourth to open — sits inside the 宗春 (Muneharu) Zone of Kinshachi Yokocho, the castle-gate dining precinct built to revitalize the area at Nagoya Castle's east gate.
chef_bio.philosophy: The owner describes the Kinshachi Yokocho location as a responsibility: to be a place that locals who work nearby and tourists alike genuinely need — a real neighbourhood lunch counter that carries Nagoya's food culture forward and spreads ankake spaghetti wider.
chef_bio.specialty: あんかけスパゲティ（トッピング違いを含む）

anecdotes.text: The sauce is deliberately tuned down on spice compared to some rival ankake-spa shops, a choice to make it accessible to children and older diners — and portions run the full range from a 250 g 'small' all the way to a 1 kg 'mega'.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 金シャチ横丁公式が、名古屋城東門・宗春ゾーン内、市内3店に続く4店目、辛さ控えめで子どもから年配まで楽しめる点、名古屋の食文化を担い世界へ広めたいというオーナーの想いを掲載。公式サイトは市内5店舗（長者町・栄4丁目・金シャチ横丁・名駅サンロード・テラッセ納屋橋）とS250g〜M1000gのサイズ展開を示す。

gf_cross_contamination.text: The entire menu is wheat-pasta based and the kitchen is not set up to avoid gluten cross-contact. There is no gluten-free pasta, no GF sauce variant, and no allergen management information published.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The Japonaise menu style uses soy-dashi seasoning; standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat. No wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All cooking stations handle lard-coated noodles and animal-based stocks simultaneously; there is no separation for plant-based preparation.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen chart or staff allergy-handling protocol is published on the official site or any listing reviewed. Callers may reach the restaurant at 052-212-5554 to ask directly.

cultural_comfort.note: A casual counter-and-table pasta spot geared toward local workers and Nagoya Castle tourists — English menus are not confirmed; basic pointing/ordering is manageable but staff likely Japanese-only.

places.name: 納屋天 (Nayaten)
places.gf_detail: Traditional fugu cuisine relies heavily on wheat-containing soy sauce for ponzu dipping and seasoning. Dashi is fish-based (fugu broth) so dairy-free but not wheat-free by default. No GF adaptations or tamari substitutions are mentioned anywhere in reviews or listings. Fried fugu (kara-age) involves wheat-flour batter. Staff allergy communication should be attempted before booking, but celiac safety cannot be assumed at this type of traditional specialist counter.
places.vegan_detail: Exclusively a fugu (blowfish) speciality restaurant. Every dish centres on pufferfish — sashimi (tessa), hot pot (tetchiri), fried fugu, grilled shirako (milt). There are no plant-based or vegan dishes.
places.hours_raw: Seasonal only: open from the autumn equinox (approx. late September) through the spring equinox (approx. late March). Dinner service only; exact nightly hours not published. Closed entirely June–September (off-season). Call 052-961-8222 for current schedule.
places.neighborhood: 白壁 (Shirakabe), 東区 (Higashi Ward) — 2-minute walk from Higas
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Nayaten is a tiny, counter-only fugu specialist tucked into the residential Shirakabe neighbourhood of Nagoya's Higashi Ward, a short walk from Higashiote Station and less than a kilometre from Nagoya Castle. It is run by an elderly husband-and-wife couple and operates strictly w

chef_bio.background: Nayaten is a tiny, counter-only fugu specialist (about 7-8 seats) tucked into the residential Shirakabe neighbourhood of Nagoya's Higashi Ward, a two-minute walk from Higashi-Ote Station and less than a kilometre from Nagoya Castle. It is run by an elderly husband-and-wife couple and operates strictly within the fugu season — from the autumn equinox through to the spring equinox — honouring the traditional view that fugu is at its finest in the cold months.
chef_bio.philosophy: Understated hospitality in a hidden-gem setting: the couple prepare everything themselves, engage guests warmly, and keep the menu tightly seasonal, letting premium fugu speak for itself.
chef_bio.specialty: Thick-cut fugu sashimi (tessa) — unusually generous slices that concentrate the fish's umami rather than the near-translucent cuts typical elsewhere; also highly regarded for grilled shirako (white milt), tetsu-chiri hot pot, and the proprietors' own deeply infused hire-zake (fin sake).

anecdotes.text: Regulars describe the counter as a place where you drink and eat happily alongside the chef and proprietress — a rare intimacy at this price point.

First-time visitors cannot simply call to reserve; the convention is to be introduced by an existing regular before a table is offered, making this one of Nagoya's genuinely hard-to-enter neighbourhood counters.
The restaurant is described as extremely difficult to find — a hidden gem tucked in a residential lane — yet beloved by its small circle of loyal regulars.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログとRettyが、名古屋市東区白壁（東大手駅至近）の隠れ家、ご夫妻が営むカウンター7〜8席のみ、秋分から春分のみ営業、厚切りのふぐ刺し・焼き白子・てっちり・ひれ酒、初来店は常連の紹介制、という点を一致して伝える。

gf_cross_contamination.text: This is a traditional Japanese fugu counter. Wheat flour is used in fried fugu preparation (kara-age) and wheat-based soy sauce is standard in ponzu and seasoning throughout the menu. Cross-contamination risk in a tiny 8-seat counter kitchen with no separation of cooking surfaces must be assumed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce is mentioned in any review or listing. Traditional wheat-containing shoyu is the default for ponzu dipping and cooking.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Every component of the menu involves fugu or fish-derived stock. There is no vegan menu path and no plant-based preparation area.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information on allergy communication or accommodation has been published. Given this is a tiny counter run by two people with no English-language presence, allergy discussions would need to happen in Japanese at the time of reservation.

positives.text: The restaurant is non-smoking throughout (全席禁煙), and fugu itself — as a white fish served as sashimi or in broth — is naturally free of gluten when prepared without soy sauce or flour coating. Guests with specific needs should raise this at booking.

cultural_comfort.note: Phone reservation in Japanese required; first-time guests are typically introduced by a regular — this is a local regulars' counter with no English interface or tourist infrastructure.

places.name: 千力 (Senriki)
places.gf_detail: Tempura is the entire concept: wheat-flour batter is inseparable from every dish. The tsuyu (sauce) and dipping sauces also typically contain wheat-based soy sauce. There is no gluten-free preparation available and no indication the kitchen can accommodate wheat avoidance. Completely unsuitable for celiac diners.
places.vegan_detail: The menu is built around seafood tempura — shrimp, scallops, and mixed kakiage — with no plant-based main options. Dashi is almost certainly katsuobushi-based. Nothing here works for vegans.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sat 11:00–15:00 (L.O. 14:30), 17:00–22:00 (L.O. 21:30); closed Sun & holidays. Monday varies by source; confirm.
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Senriki is a small, independently run tempura and donburi shop tucked into the office backstreets of Marunouchi, a five-minute walk from Marunouchi Station. It has built a loyal following among nearby office workers who queue before the doors open at lunch.

chef_bio.background: Senriki is a small, independently run tempura and donburi shop tucked into the office backstreets of Marunouchi, a five-minute walk from Marunouchi Station (a 6-seat counter plus four tables). It has built a loyal following among nearby office workers who queue before the doors open at lunch.
chef_bio.philosophy: The kitchen leans into salt as a seasoning philosophy, letting the natural sweetness of the seafood and vegetables speak. Diners can choose salt (shio), tsuyu sauce, or miso — but regulars overwhelmingly recommend salt.
chef_bio.specialty: Shio kakiage-don — a generous mixed fritter of small shrimp, mushrooms, and scallops served over rice with a clean salt seasoning rather than the heavy sweet sauce found at most tempura shops.

anecdotes.text: A blogger arrived before noon on a weekday to find a queue already formed. A fellow diner in line said unprompted: 'the salt kakiage bowl is my recommendation' — reinforcing it as the house specialty.

The restaurant offers a tabe-kurabe (食べ比べ, comparison) set at around ¥1,300 that lets diners order two mini bowls side by side — a practical way to sample the salt versus miso preparations in one sitting.
A daily 'kimagure-don' (気まぐれ丼, whim bowl) rotates on the kitchen's mood — past examples include a Hida-beef-and-tomato salted beef bowl and crab with smoked salmon and yuzu pepper.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: ブログ(an-alcott)と名古屋めしが、丸の内駅徒歩5分・カウンター6席＋テーブル、開店前の行列、塩かき揚げ丼が看板、しお・つゆ・みその選択、食べ比べセット約1,300円、日替わりの気まぐれ丼（飛騨牛トマト塩牛丼など）を裏付ける。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat flour batter is used for every dish — there is no separation of gluten-containing and gluten-free cooking at any stage.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tsuyu-based dipping sauce and seasoning sauces are standard Japanese preparations and almost certainly contain wheat-based soy sauce; no tamari or GF alternative is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All mains contain seafood (shrimp, scallops); dashi is presumed katsuobushi-based with no plant-based alternative mentioned.

cultural_comfort.note: A compact neighborhood lunch spot in an office district — no English menu or signage, but the photo menu and simple ordering process make it manageable for non-Japanese speakers.

places.name: 日本料理 丈 (Nihon Ryori Jo)
places.gf_detail: Standard kaiseki uses wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu) in sauces and marinades, and dashi may include wheat-containing ingredients. No gluten-free menu is offered. The restaurant's policy states allergen requests must be made at the time of booking — no post-arrival adjustments are guaranteed. There is no dedicated gluten-free program, no dedicated fryer disclosed, and no evidence of cross-contamination protocols. Celiac diners should contact the restaurant directly before reserving.
places.vegan_detail: Traditional kaiseki menus rely heavily on dashi (typically katsuobushi/bonito-based) and seafood courses. No vegan menu or plant-based alternative is advertised. The counter-style omakase format ("御誂え料理") allows some customization if discussed at time of reservation, but vegan accommodation is not confirmed and would require direct inquiry before booking.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sun 11:30–14:30, 18:00–22:00; closed Monday (or Tuesday if Monday is a public holiday)
places.neighborhood: Nishi-ku, Hinokuchi-chō / ~10 min walk from Sengen-chō Stn
places.notes: Nagata Isao was born in 1972 in Gifu Prefecture and entered the culinary world at age 18, spending his formative years at the original Hotel Nagoya Castle and its sister property Castle Plaza. He became head chef at Castle Plaza in 2009, then head of Japanese cuisine at Hotel Nag

roles: Supervising Producer: 佐々木浩 (Sasaki Hiroshi) — proprietor of Gion Sasaki, Kyoto (3 Michelin stars)
chef_bio.origin: Opened October 1, 2025 as the flagship Japanese restaurant of Espacio Nagoya Castle, a new luxury hotel erected on the historic site of Hotel Nagoya Castle. The hotel's Japanese cuisine lineage stretches back decades, and 丈 is designed as its direct heir — with a chef who cooked for dignitaries during the 2005 Aichi Expo now leading the kitchen.
chef_bio.background: Nagata Isao was born in 1972 in Gifu Prefecture and began his culinary path at age 18, learning Japanese cuisine at the sister hotel Castle Plaza. He became head chef of the Castle Plaza restaurant in 2009 and of the Nagoya Castle hotel restaurant in 2013, later serving as a banquet Japanese-cuisine chef. When the hotel closed in November 2020 he moved to Nagoya Kanko Hotel, becoming head chef of its Japanese restaurant 呉竹 (Kuretake) and, from January 2022, its head of Japanese cuisine. When Espacio Nagoya Castle opened in October 2025 — built on the site of the former Hotel Nagoya Castle — he returned as head chef of 丈. In 2022 he received the Aichi Prefecture Excellent Technician award (愛知県優秀技能者表彰／あいちの名工).
chef_bio.philosophy: The restaurant operates under the motto 温故知新 — 'learn from the past to create the new' — a guiding principle of its supervising chef Hiroshi Sasaki of Gion Sasaki in Kyoto. The name 丈 (jo) is chosen deliberately: it carries the meaning of integrity and uprightness, and shares the same reading as 城 (castle), linking the restaurant's identity to Nagoya Castle directly outside its windows.
chef_bio.specialty: Counter-style kaiseki (カウンター割烹) featuring seasonal omakase courses — 御誂え料理 — timed and paced to each diner, with ingredients sourced nationwide for peak seasonal quality (46 seats, plus private rooms).

anecdotes.text: Nagata Isao handled the Japanese cuisine served to dignitaries and VIP guests at the state guest house during the 2005 Aichi Expo — a formative test of high-stakes hospitality that now informs his exacting standards at 丈.
The name 丈 shares its reading (じょう / jo) with 城 (castle), quietly embedding Nagoya Castle into the restaurant's very identity — reviewers note the wordplay as intentional.

Hiroshi Sasaki — the three-Michelin-star chef who supervises 丈 from his Kyoto base — is scheduled to personally cook a one-night special event at the restaurant on July 26, 2026 for ¥40,000 per guest, offering diners a direct encounter with his technique.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: エスパシオ公式とエスパシオエンタープライズのプレスリリースが、永田功の経歴（1972年岐阜生、18歳でキャッスルプラザ、2009年キャッスルプラザ料理長、2013年ナゴヤキャッスル料理長、2020年11月名古屋観光ホテル『呉竹』料理長、2022年1月和食料理長、2025年『丈』料理長、2022年愛知県優秀技能者=あいちの名工受賞、2005年愛・地球博で迎賓館の和食を担当）と、佐々木浩(祇園さゝ木)監修・温故知新・丈=城の由来を裏付け。gourmetpressが2026年7月26日の佐々木浩特別会4万円を掲載。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or cross-contamination protocols are disclosed. A kaiseki kitchen uses wheat-based shoyu and mirin routinely across multiple dishes; shared prep surfaces and equipment are the norm in this format.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese kaiseki relies on shoyu (soy sauce brewed with wheat) as a foundational seasoning in sauces, marinades, and finishing. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere in available sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi (stock) in kaiseki is typically made with katsuobushi (bonito flakes) or niboshi (dried sardines) and is used throughout the menu — in soups, sauces, and seasonings. No plant-based dashi option is advertised.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The official site states that allergy and dietary concerns must be communicated at the time of reservation. No adjustments are guaranteed after arrival. Guests with serious allergies should call 052-908-8930 (10:00–18:30) before booking.

positives.text: The counter omakase format ('御誂え料理') allows the kitchen to pace and adjust individual courses in real time, which in principle creates more opportunity for dietary discussion than a fixed set menu — though this is subject to advance notice at booking.

cultural_comfort.note: A formal hotel kaiseki restaurant operating on simultaneous fixed seatings with a smart-casual dress code — English is unlikely to be spoken fluently at the counter, and interaction with the head chef is in Japanese.

places.name: 柳城 NAGOYA CASTLE (Ryujo Nagoya Castle)
places.gf_detail: Standard Cantonese kitchen with significant wheat exposure: soy sauce (shoyu) is used throughout savory dishes, dim sum wrappers contain wheat flour, and cornstarch-thickened sauces may be cross-contaminated. No gluten-free menu, dedicated fryer, or GF soy sauce alternative is documented. The hotel notes allergy accommodations should be requested in advance and may be declined if safety cannot be guaranteed. Not safe for celiac without direct, detailed inquiry with the kitchen.
places.vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu exists. Cantonese cuisine here centers on live-tank seafood, dim sum (typically contain lard or egg wrappers), Nagoya cochin chicken, and braised pork belly. Hidden animal ingredients (oyster sauce, lard in pastry, dashi-equivalent stocks) are standard in this style. Vegan requests should be made well in advance; the kitchen may be able to accommodate with prior notice but this is not confirmed.
places.hours_raw: Lunch 11:30–14:30 (L.O. 13:30) / Dinner 17:30–22:00 (L.O. 20:30); open daily, no regular closure days listed
places.neighborhood: 西区 (Nishi Ward) / 浅間町・名古屋城エリア
places.notes: 柳城 NAGOYA CASTLE opened on October 1, 2025, as the signature Chinese restaurant of Espacio Nagoya Castle, a luxury hotel that occupies a historic site roughly 330 meters from Nagoya Castle's walls. The restaurant carries on the 柳城 (Ryujo) brand, referencing the castle's old name.

roles: Head Chef: 内藤勝博 (Katsuhiro Naito)
Dim Sum Chef (special-grade / 特級点心師)
chef_bio.background: 柳城 NAGOYA CASTLE opened on October 1, 2025, as the signature Chinese restaurant of Espacio Nagoya Castle, a luxury hotel that stands just across the moat from Nagoya Castle. The restaurant carries on the 柳城 (Ryujo) name, referencing the castle's old designation.
chef_bio.philosophy: Head chef Katsuhiro Naito draws on traditional Cantonese technique while weaving in the umami of local Aichi ingredients — Nagoya cochin and Okazaki ouhan chicken among them — to express the essence of Chinese cuisine through a Japanese-ingredient lens. Beverage pairings (Chinese wines, teas) are curated by in-house sommeliers and tea masters.
chef_bio.specialty: Cantonese crispy pork (広東式クリスピーポーク) and hand-crafted dim sum by a dedicated special-grade dim sum chef; live-tank seafood dishes prepared to order.

anecdotes.text: Chef Naito is cited as the first Japanese chef to reach the top 10 in a world Cantonese cuisine competition (広東料理の世界大会で日本人初のトップ10) — a distinction highlighted in the hotel's opening coverage.

The restaurant overlooks Nagoya Castle and takes its name from the castle's historical designation 柳城 (Ryujo — Willow Castle), connecting the dining experience to the landmark visible through its windows.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 名古屋情報通と一休レストランが、内藤勝博料理長=広東料理の世界大会で日本人初トップ10、広東式クリスピーポーク、特級点心師の点心、生簀の活魚、名古屋コーチン・岡崎おうはんなど地元愛知食材の活用を伝える。PR Timesが2025年10月1日開業・名古屋城の堀向かいの立地を裏付け。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about cross-contamination controls is publicly documented. Standard Cantonese kitchen workflows involve shared woks, utensils, and prep surfaces with wheat-containing ingredients throughout service.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Wheat-containing soy sauce is a foundational ingredient in Cantonese cooking and used throughout the menu. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Lard is traditional in dim sum pastry, and oyster sauce and other animal-derived condiments are ubiquitous in Cantonese kitchens. No vegan cross-contact protocols are documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The hotel states that allergy accommodations require advance notice and may be declined if customer safety cannot be guaranteed; requests should be directed to the reservation desk (052-908-8930, 10:00–18:30).

positives.text: Luxury hotel setting with dedicated reservation staff, making advance allergy communication more feasible than at a typical independent restaurant.

cultural_comfort.note: Luxury hotel restaurant (Espacio Nagoya Castle) with international guest infrastructure; English-language reservation platforms (TableCheck, Ikyu) available and hotel staff accustomed to overseas visitors.

places.name: おばんざいあふろ (Obanzai Afuro)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free labelling or accommodation is mentioned anywhere. Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is almost certainly used in dashi-maki tamago and braised dishes. Tempura-battered items may appear. Cross-contamination risk is real in a 13-seat kitchen with no dedicated GF protocol. Celiac diners should not assume safety; ask specifically about soy sauce and cooking oils before ordering.
places.vegan_detail: Traditional obanzai cooking built on dashi broth; reviewers cite fish-based dashi (鰹・魚介出汁) as central to the flavour. Dishes like braised pork (角煮) and mackerel (ほっけ) appear on the menu alongside vegetable small plates. No vegan options are advertised, but plant-forward dishes (tofu with greens, seasonal vegetables) do appear — ask staff what is prepared without dashi or animal protein. Hidden fish-based dashi is the main concern.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Tue, Thu–Sun 17:00–00:00 (next day) (L.O. 23:00), closed Wed
places.neighborhood: Naka-ku, Marunouchi (2 min walk from Marunouchi Stn)
places.cuisine: Izakaya, obanzai (Kyoto home-style dishes), Japanese cuisine
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A small obanzai counter that opened in September 2025 in the Horie Building on Marunouchi-dori, a short walk from Marunouchi Station. Run by a female proprietor, the place seats just 13 at a counter and a compact private nook.

chef_bio.background: A small obanzai counter that opened on September 1, 2025 in the Horie Building on Marunouchi-dori, about a two-minute walk from Marunouchi Station. Run by a female proprietor, the place seats just 13 across a counter and a compact private nook (up to four).
chef_bio.philosophy: Gentle, home-style flavours rooted in careful dashi technique; a welcoming spot for solo diners seeking a quiet neighbourhood counter, including those arriving late in the evening.
chef_bio.specialty: Dashi-forward obanzai small plates — seasonal vegetable simmers, dashi egg roll (dashimaki tamago), and braised dishes paired with sake and shochu.

anecdotes.text: One early reviewer noted that, despite the name conjuring an image of an afro-wearing cook, the establishment is run by a warm female proprietor who makes solo diners feel at ease.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログが、2025年9月開業、丸の内堀江ビル（丸の内駅徒歩約2分）、女性店主、カウンター＋4名個室で計13席、だしを効かせたおばんざい・だし巻き玉子、一人客にも居心地よい雰囲気を伝える。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available. The kitchen serves a full izakaya menu in a compact space; cross-contamination cannot be ruled out.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard wheat-containing soy sauce is expected in braised dishes (kakuni), egg roll, and simmered vegetable plates. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi (fish stock) is central to the cooking style per multiple reviews; plant-based dishes are likely prepared in the same pots and with the same dashi as fish dishes.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy or staff training information has been reported. The restaurant is a solo-operator counter; direct verbal communication with the proprietor is the only available channel.

cultural_comfort.note: Small counter-style izakaya run by a solo female proprietor; no English signage or menus reported, suited to Japanese-comfortable diners.

places.name: 大銀杏 名古屋西店 (Ooicho Nagoya Nishi-ten)
places.gf_detail: Standard yakitori with no gluten-free declaration. Tare (dipping sauce) used on many skewers contains wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu). The house salt blend is made from bonito and kombu dashi and is advertised as additive-free, but wheat-free status is unconfirmed. Charcoal grilling reduces cross-contamination risk versus frying, but shared grills and shared prep areas are standard. No dedicated GF menu or allergen guide found on any source. Celiacs should ask about tare vs. shio (salt) options and confirm shoyu ingredients before ordering.
places.vegan_detail: This is a dedicated yakitori restaurant — the entire menu revolves around grilled chicken and offal. The house seasoning salt is made from bonito (katsuobushi) and kombu dashi, making even the salt-grilled items non-vegan. No plant-based skewer alternatives are offered. Vegetarians and vegans have essentially no options here beyond side salads or cold tofu if available, but these are not confirmed allergen-free.
places.hours_raw: 17:30–22:30 (L.O. 22:00); closed Thu (some sources note irregular closures)
places.neighborhood: Josai, Nishi-ku — 3-min walk from Asama-cho Station (Tsuruma
places.notes: Ooicho is a Nagoya-based charcoal yakitori chain with locations in Sakae, Meieki, and this Nishi-ku outpost near Asama-cho Station. No founding date or owner biography is publicly documented.

chef_bio.background: Ooicho is a Nagoya-based charcoal yakitori chain with locations in Sakae, Meieki, Yanagibashi, and this Nishi-ku outpost near Asama-cho Station. No founding date or owner biography is publicly documented.
chef_bio.philosophy: The kitchen prioritizes quality local chicken and grilling over high-heat binchotan charcoal on a custom extra-large grill to seal in natural umami, letting the ingredient speak rather than heavy seasoning.
chef_bio.specialty: Charcoal-grilled chicken skewers using Kinsou-dori (錦爽どり), an Aichi-area brand chicken, finished with a housemade salt blended from bonito and kombu dashi — no chemical additives.

anecdotes.text: Rare cuts such as haatsu-moto (chicken heart base, requiring 5-6 birds for a single skewer) reflect the kitchen's commitment to whole-bird butchery.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 大銀杏公式サイトと名古屋めしが、栄・名駅・柳橋・名古屋西の各店、職人特注の火力の強い特大焼台と備長炭、鰹・昆布だしを効かせた無添加の自家製塩、錦爽どり（愛知のブランド鶏）の使用、ハツ元は1本に5〜6羽必要な希少部位、という点を裏付ける。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated GF kitchen or allergen separation is documented. Shared charcoal grill used for all skewers including tare-marinated items.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tare (sweet soy-based glaze) used on standard yakitori skewers contains wheat. Shio (salt-grilled) option uses housemade salt from bonito and kombu dashi; wheat-free status of this salt is unconfirmed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All skewers are animal-based and the grill is entirely shared. Bonito-kombu salt is used across the menu. No vegan-safe items identified.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu or allergy handling policy is listed on any available source. Phone inquiry (052-325-5626) before visiting is strongly advised for any dietary restriction.

positives.text: Charcoal grilling rather than deep-frying eliminates shared-fryer cross-contamination risk. Shio (salt-only) skewers avoid tare sauce for those avoiding soy; ask staff to confirm which skewers can be ordered shio.

cultural_comfort.note: Neighborhood izakaya atmosphere near a residential area of Nishi-ku; Japanese menus and counter seating; basic Japanese useful for ordering at the charcoal counter.

places.name: 鰻 木屋 (Unagi Kiya)
places.gf_detail: Traditional unagi tare (kabayaki glaze) is made with standard wheat-containing soy sauce — a significant gluten source. Dashi used in soups is fish-based but likely made with standard ingredients. No GF claims on the menu or website. The restaurant does not advertise any allergen accommodations. Guests with celiac disease should call ahead (052-951-8781) and ask specifically about the soy sauce and tare; substitution is unlikely at a traditional specialist of this type. Cross-contamination risk from shared kitchen surfaces must also be assumed.
places.vegan_detail: Entirely eel-focused menu. All dishes built around unagi; soups are fish-based (鯉 or dashi); no plant-based options offered. Not suitable for vegans or vegetarians.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sat 11:00–13:30 (lunch only). Saturday dinner by full reservation only (around 17:30). Closed Sun, Mon, holidays, and on Doyō-no-ushi-no-hi (midsummer eel day).
places.neighborhood: 東区 東大手 (Higashi Ward, Higashi-Ote)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Founded in the late Edo period, Unagi Kiya is one of Nagoya's oldest surviving eel restaurants, now run by its fifth generation of ownership. It sits a short walk from Higashi-Ote Station in the historically layered Higashi Ward, not far from Nagoya Castle.

chef_bio.background: Founded in the late Edo period (江戸末期), Unagi Kiya is one of Nagoya's oldest surviving eel restaurants, now run by its fifth generation. It sits about a three-minute walk from Higashi-Ote Station in the historically layered Higashi Ward, not far from Nagoya Castle.
chef_bio.philosophy: Simplicity and continuity: well water is still used to rest live eel before grilling, and the menu is kept deliberately short.
chef_bio.specialty: Unagi don (eel rice bowl) and ohitsu-mabushi — the house style features Kansai-influenced belly-split eel grilled directly over binchotan charcoal without steaming, producing a crisper exterior than Edo-style preparations. The thick-cut eel is sourced from Kagoshima, stored live in well water before service, and finished with a rich house tare.

anecdotes.text: The restaurant keeps live eel in well water before preparation — a practice said to reduce stress in the fish and improve texture and flavor at service.
Signed celebrity memorabilia is displayed inside (including an autograph from actress Hashimoto Ai), evidence of a long-standing following beyond the neighborhood regulars.
Grilled liver (kimo-yaki) and umaki (eel-and-egg omelet) are not offered — the menu is kept short — though a liver soup (kimo-sui) accompanies the eel-rice sets.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログと公式サイトが、江戸末期創業・現5代目、東大手駅徒歩約3分、備長炭で焼く関西風（腹開き・蒸さない）でサクッとした食感、井戸水で活鰻を寝かせる点、おひつまぶし・うな丼を裏付ける。ブログ(ameblo)が鹿児島県産の厚みある鰻、著名人のサイン色紙、肝焼き・うまきは提供なし（肝吸いは付く）ことを伝える。老舗の継ぎ足しダレを断定する一次資料は確認できず、記述を『自家製の濃いめのタレ』に緩和。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Traditional unagi tare (kabayaki glaze) is made with standard wheat-containing soy sauce. No GF alternative is offered or advertised. Cross-contamination from shared grilling surfaces and kitchen equipment must be assumed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The signature tare — built up over generations from standard Japanese soy sauce (which contains wheat) — is central to every eel dish on the menu. There is no wheat-free soy sauce option indicated anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is eel-based; fish dashi is used in all soups. No plant-based cooking surfaces or utensils are separated. Not applicable for vegan diners.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy is published on the official site or Tabelog. Reservations are phone-only; calling ahead at 052-951-8781 is the only way to discuss dietary needs before visiting.

positives.text: Eel is prepared fresh to order (approximately 20–40 minutes), meaning there is no pre-made batch sitting with mixed ingredients — though the tare itself remains a fixed wheat source.

cultural_comfort.note: A quiet, traditional unagi specialist with Japanese-only signage and menus; staff interaction is polite but not English-oriented — pointing to menu items works fine for ordering.

places.name: ブランカ (gallery+cafe blanka)
places.gf_detail: The main menu items — focaccia, quiche pastry and pasta — contain wheat. No explicit gluten-free accommodation. Whether soy sauce is used is unknown. No mention of a dedicated fryer or cross-contamination measures. Celiac accommodation cannot be expected; advance confirmation is essential.
places.vegan_detail: Many menu items are vegetable-centered, but the mainstays include quiche (using egg and cream), focaccia and chiffon cake, which contain dairy and eggs. Lunch courses also feature pork (from Chita) and seafood. There is no explicit full-vegan accommodation, and whether a hidden dashi (bonito, dried sardine) is used is also unknown. Phoning to confirm in advance is strongly recommended.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sat 11:30–16:30 (lunch L.O. 14:00), 18:00–22:00 (dinner L.O. 21:00); Sun 11:30–16:30; closed Mon (also reportedly closed the first Tuesday of each month)
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi, Naka Ward (along the Horikawa; 5 min walk from Marunouchi Stn Exit 8)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A gallery cafe created by renovating the historic storehouse (kura) of Oki Masa Shoten, a lumber merchant founded in 1907 (Meiji 40). Located along the Horikawa, it is a rare space where gallery and cafe merge, characterized by high ceilings and the warmth of wood. Its three gallery spaces host contemporary-art exhibitions, pop-ups, readings and more.

chef_bio.origin: 愛知県名古屋市中区丸の内（堀川沿い）
chef_bio.background: 明治40年（1907年）創業の材木商・沖正商店が営む老舗の蔵をリノベーションして誕生したギャラリーカフェ。 堀川沿いに位置し、高い天井と木のぬくもりが特徴的な空間で、ギャラリーとカフェが一体となった稀有な場所。 3つのギャラリースペースでは現代アートの展覧会、ポップアップ、朗読会などが開催される。
chef_bio.philosophy: アートを身近に感じられる場所をつくりたいという想いから生まれた空間。 高知県嶺北地域の野菜や愛知県産の地場野菜を中心に、身体にやさしい素材を大切にしたビストロ料理を提供。 亜硫酸塩添加量を抑えたナチュラルワインも揃える。
chef_bio.specialty: キッシュランチ（1日5食限定）／無農薬野菜たっぷりのサラダ

anecdotes.text: 2019年「愛知サラダメシ総選挙」でブロンズ賞を受賞。地元・無農薬野菜を活かした彩り豊かなサラダが評価された。
明治40年創業の材木商が営む蔵をリノベーション。天井の高い開放的な空間に、独立設計の3つのギャラリーとカフェが共存し、アート鑑賞客も食事客も快適に過ごせる。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 名古屋めしとmyplが、明治40年創業の材木商の蔵をリノベした堀川沿いのギャラリーカフェ、独立設計の3ギャラリー＋カフェ、高知県嶺北・愛知の無農薬野菜、亜硫酸塩控えめのナチュラルワイン、1日5食限定のキッシュランチ、2019年愛知サラダメシ総選挙ブロンズ賞、を裏付ける。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテンフリー対応・交差汚染対策についての記載は公式サイト・各グルメサイトのいずれにも確認できなかった。フォカッチャやパスタなど小麦料理が主力のため、厨房内での小麦粉との接触は避けられない可能性が高い。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 醤油の使用有無については公開情報に記載なし。フレンチ・ビストロ系メニューのため頻繁な使用は考えにくいが、確認は取れていない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: キッシュ・フォカッチャ・シフォンケーキなど乳製品・卵を含む料理が中心。完全ヴィーガンへの交差接触対策についての記載なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: Tabelogの情報として「予算やメニューに関するご要望にも対応可能」との記載があり、事前相談に応じる姿勢は示されているが、アレルギー対応の専門的なプロトコルの有無は不明。来店前の電話確認（052-265-5557）を強く推奨。

positives.text: 無農薬野菜（高知県嶺北地域産・愛知県産）を積極使用。野菜中心のメニュー構成で、植物性素材の選択肢は他の一般的なカフェより多い。
2019年「愛知サラダメシ総選挙」ブロンズ賞受賞。野菜料理へのこだわりが第三者機関によって評価されている。

cultural_comfort.note: メニューや案内は日本語中心だが、カード決済対応・予約可能でアクセスしやすい環境。英語対応の明示はなし。

places.name: LIT（リト） (LIT (Rito))
places.gf_detail: Ankake spaghetti is built around wheat-flour noodles, and there is no mention whatsoever of gluten-free accommodation. It should be regarded as unsuitable for celiacs or those with wheat allergy.
places.vegan_detail: The signature offerings are seafood dishes such as the fish plate, and while the sauce is described as a mild, vegetable-based umami, there is no explicit vegan accommodation. Whether any meat- and fish-free option exists is unknown, so confirmation before visiting is needed.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Wed and Sat–Sun 11:30–15:00 (L.O. 14:30); Thu also open evenings 18:00–20:00 (L.O. 19:30); closed Mon and Fri. Occasional temporary closures (check Instagram).
places.neighborhood: Kaminagoya, Nishi Ward / 10 min walk from Joshin Stn
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Originally started as the spice-curry 'LIT COFFEE,' it then spent about three years serving ankake pasta from a food truck at events nationwide. On January 12, 2026 it opened a permanent shop in an alley in Kaminagoya, Nishi Ward, Nagoya. The horse in the shop's logo evokes an 'image of moving about freely'; the New Year opening also coincided with 'the timing of the Year of the Horse.'

roles: オーナーシェフ（名前非公開）
chef_bio.origin: 名古屋市西区上名古屋
chef_bio.background: もともとスパイスカレーの「LIT COFFEE」として出発し、その後約3年間キッチンカーであんかけパスタを各地のイベントで提供してきた。 2026年1月12日、名古屋市西区上名古屋（浄心駅近く）の路地に実店舗をオープン。 店のロゴに馬をあしらったのは「自由に動き回るイメージ」から。 年明けの開業は「午年のタイミング」とも重なった。
chef_bio.philosophy: キッチンカー時代の熱量をそのまま実店舗に持ち込む、移動する食の精神。 レトロ喫茶の雰囲気の中で、名古屋めしのあんかけパスタを真剣に掘り下げる。
chef_bio.specialty: あんかけスパゲッティ（S200g/M300g/L400g・20種以上のトッピングから選択可）。 看板はフィッシュプレート：揚げた白身魚に野菜の甘みを引き出したマイルドなあんかけソースをかけたもので、塩気と甘みのバランスが特徴とされる。

anecdotes.text: キッチンカー時代から看板だったフィッシュプレートは「旨みがぎゅっと詰まった」一皿として、実店舗オープン直後から話題を集め、営業時間中に売り切れることも珍しくない。
店のロゴはキッチンカー時代から使い続けている馬のモチーフ。「自由に動き回る」イメージを体現し、2026年の午年開業とのタイミングも「素敵な一致」と地元メディアが紹介。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 名古屋情報通と食べログが、スパイスカレー『LIT COFFEE』から出発し約3年キッチンカーであんかけパスタを提供、2026年1月12日に西区上名古屋（浄心駅近く）で実店舗開業、馬ロゴ＝自由に動くイメージで午年開業と一致、看板のフィッシュプレート、S200g/M300g/L400g・20種超のトッピング、を裏付ける。（2026年は午年であり、原文の『寅年』は誤りのため削除）

cultural_comfort.note: 小さなカウンター中心の店で英語メニューや英語対応の記載はなく、日本語での注文が基本となる。

places.name: ふじ原 (Fujiwara)
places.gf_detail: Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is a foundational ingredient in kappo cooking at this level. Wheat appears in soy sauce marinades, tare, and likely in many braised and simmered preparations. Tempura is not prominent on documented menus, but shared prep surfaces and no dedicated GF protocols are documented. The chef is known for accommodating individual requests, so a celiac diner should communicate clearly at the time of reservation; however, the kitchen is not certified GF and cross-contamination risk cannot be ruled out.
places.vegan_detail: This is a traditional kappo restaurant built around seasonal seafood, fish-based dashi (likely katsuobushi/konbu blends), and standard Japanese soy sauce containing wheat. No vegan menu or plant-based options are advertised. Signature dishes include eel, sashimi, beef tendon, and sea urchin. A diner with advance notice might discuss a vegetable-forward arrangement with the chef — he has stated "please speak up casually if there's something you'd like to eat" — but no plant-based or vegan course is documented, and animal-derived dashi is almost certain to appear throughout.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 17:30–22:00, closed Sun & holidays. No lunch service.
places.neighborhood: Higashi-ku, Izumi / Hisaya-ōdōri
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Born in December 1969 in Aichi Prefecture, Fujiwara trained for 16 years at the celebrated Osaka kaiseki restaurant Sakuichi (作一) before returning to Nagoya and opening ふじ原 in 2004. The restaurant relocated and was fully renovated in 2016 to its current third-floor home inside th

chef_bio.background: Born in December 1969 in Aichi Prefecture, Fujiwara trained for 16 years at the Osaka Japanese restaurant Sakuichi (作一) before returning to Nagoya and opening ふじ原 in 2004. The restaurant relocated and was fully renovated in 2016 to its current third-floor home inside the White Mates building near Hisaya-Odori.
chef_bio.philosophy: Fujiwara says he works so as never to 'bring shame to the name of Sakuichi,' where he learned his fundamentals — a commitment to thorough, quality work inherited from his Osaka training. He emphasises seasonal ingredients and wants guests to feel they can speak up casually about what they'd like to eat, making high-end kappo feel approachable.
chef_bio.specialty: Seasonal kappo-style Japanese cuisine rooted in Osaka (Naniwa-ryori) tradition: a rotating à la carte board of over 100 items, including a seasonal sashimi assortment (toro, sawara, ribbonfish, red seabream, squid and more), wild eel grilled two ways (white-grilled shirayaki and sauce-glazed / うなぎの二色焼), and steamed sweet bream with bamboo shoot (甘鯛の若竹蒸し).

anecdotes.text: The restaurant's daily menu is displayed on a board at the entrance rather than printed, with no prices listed — a reflection of the omakase spirit where trust in the chef guides the meal, with the chef periodically bringing extra dishes unbidden. A 2024 diner reported spending ¥18,480 for roughly two hours including several à la carte dishes and shochu.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: ヒトサラの人物ページによれば、藤原光章は1969年12月愛知県生まれ、大阪の日本料理『作一』で16年修業後、2004年にふじ原を開業、2016年に移転・改装。看板は天然うなぎの二色焼、甘鯛の若竹蒸し、季節の刺身盛り合わせで献立は100種類以上。gourmandブログが値段非表示の品書きと2024年に約¥18,480の来店例を記載。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free protocols or kitchen separation is documented. As a traditional kappo kitchen, wheat-containing soy sauce is a near-universal base ingredient across sauces, braises, and marinades, making cross-contamination a realistic concern.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (koikuchi shoyu, which contains wheat) is the expected foundation of this cuisine style. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternatives are mentioned on any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi (likely katsuobushi) is a foundational element of kappo cooking. Seafood and animal proteins are present throughout service. No vegan cross-contact controls are documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The chef has stated guests should 'speak up casually if there's something they'd like to eat,' suggesting some willingness to accommodate requests, but no formal allergy protocol or allergen menu is documented on any platform.

positives.text: Owner-chef format with direct counter interaction means dietary needs can be communicated personally to the person preparing the food, which is more reliable than communicating through front-of-house staff in a large restaurant.

cultural_comfort.note: Upscale counter-style kappo restaurant where interaction with the owner-chef is central to the experience; English-language support is not indicated anywhere, and the menu is written on a board in Japanese with no prices listed.

places.name: 金鯱寿司 (Kinshachi Zushi)
places.gf_detail: Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) is used throughout; the menu features tempura (Mikawa clam tempura noted), shari rice seasoned with red and white vinegar (check for mirin), and dashi-based dishes — none of which are labelled gluten-free. No dedicated GF accommodation or tamari option is advertised anywhere. Celiac diners should call ahead and must ask specifically about soy sauce and hidden wheat in dashi and vinegar seasonings.
places.vegan_detail: Entirely seafood and animal-product focused: nigiri, sashimi, chawanmushi (egg custard), crab cream croquette, eel, Nagoya miso-seasoned beef, and dashi-based preparations. No plant-based alternatives are offered or implied.
places.neighborhood: Naka-ku, Ninomaru / Kinshachi Yokochō (1 min walk from Nagoyajō Stn)
places.notes: Muto grew up watching his father run a tonkatsu restaurant called Nabeyaki in Nagoya's Kita Ward. Initially reluctant to follow a culinary path, he was won over after spending one week helping his father and witnessing firsthand how much joy food brought to regular customers. Tha

roles: 寿司職人 (sushi chef at counter)
chef_bio.background: Muto grew up watching his father run a tonkatsu restaurant called なべや (Nabeya) in Nagoya's Kita Ward. Initially reluctant to follow a culinary path, he was won over after spending one week helping his father and witnessing firsthand how much joy food brought to regular customers. That experience drove him to pursue hospitality with a goal of reaching even more people than his father had.
chef_bio.philosophy: Guard the best of traditional Edo-mae sushi while pursuing new challenges others haven't attempted — blending seasonal local ingredients (Mikawa large clams, Gifu Hatsushimo rice, Nagoya miso beef) with refined craftsmanship.
chef_bio.specialty: Creative Nagoya-style nigiri using both red and white vinegar for the shari, chosen strategically per ingredient; signature shrimp piece arranged to resemble Nagoya's golden kinshachi ornament.

anecdotes.text: Muto's father told stories of customers' delight with obvious joy; witnessing that firsthand was the moment Muto committed to the food industry — he wanted to 'expand food's possibilities and reach more people with skills surpassing my father's.'

The head chef at the counter trained at a sushi institution and was considered for a principal (headmaster) position there before joining Kinshachi Zushi — underscoring the technical rigour behind the restaurant's approach.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 金シャチ横丁公式のニューストピックによれば、オーナー武藤輝将の父は名古屋市北区でとんかつ店『なべや』を営み、一週間の手伝いを機に飲食の道を志した。江戸前を守りつつ挑戦する方針で、三河大アサリ・岐阜ハツシモ米・名古屋味噌の牛肉など地元食材を使い、赤酢と白酢を使い分ける。海老を金鯱に見立てた握りが名物。カウンターの職人は寿司学校の校長候補だった経歴を持つ。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura appears on the menu (Mikawa clam tempura), meaning wheat flour is in active use in the kitchen — cross-contamination risk for celiac diners is real and unaddressed by any public source.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce is mentioned on any platform. Standard wheat-containing Japanese soy sauce must be assumed for all nigiri, sashimi dipping, and seasoning.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is built around seafood and animal products; dashi (fish stock) is almost certainly present in multiple preparations. No vegan cross-contact mitigation is mentioned.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy-handling policy, or staff training information has been disclosed on any public platform. Direct telephone inquiry (052-386-6326) is the only recourse.

positives.text: Omakase counter format means direct chef interaction — a motivated diner can discuss ingredient concerns piece by piece with the person preparing the food.

cultural_comfort.note: Located in Kinshachi Yokocho, a tourist-facing food district built around Nagoya Castle, so staff are accustomed to non-Japanese visitors; basic Japanese greetings will suffice, though menus and staff communication are primarily in Japanese.

places.name: 二の丸茶亭 (Ninomaru Chatei)
places.gf_detail: Pure matcha and most traditional wagashi (nerikiri mochi, mitsumame bean jelly) are rice- and bean-based and typically contain no wheat. However, the Gold Shachi Morning set includes toast (明確に小麦含む). No dedicated gluten-free kitchen, no allergen labeling, no staff allergy training confirmed. Cross-contamination risk is unknown. Celiac diners should stick to matcha with mitsumame or nerikiri and confirm ingredients on the day — avoid the morning toast set entirely.
places.vegan_detail: Traditional wagashi (nerikiri, mitsumame) and plain matcha are typically free of meat, fish, and dairy — making them incidentally vegan in most cases. Mitsumame contains agar jelly, sweet azuki beans, and fruit, all plant-based. However, no vegan labeling exists, no confirmation that dashi or animal-derived additives are absent from sweets, and the Gold Shachi Morning toast almost certainly contains butter/milk. Ask about specific items before ordering.
places.hours_raw: 9:00–16:45 (last order 16:30); Gold Shachi Morning 9:00–11:00 limited qty daily; on the 8th of each month a formal tea ceremony is held and morning service is unavailable that day
places.neighborhood: 中区 本丸 (Nakaku, Honmaru — inside Nagoya Castle)
places.notes: 二の丸茶亭 is a traditional tearoom operated by the Nagoya Castle Promotion Company (名古屋城振興株式会社) inside the grounds of Nagoya Castle, situated beside the designated scenic Ninomaru Garden — historically the garden of the Owari Tokugawa lords, the largest castle-lord residential garden

roles: City-affiliated tearoom operated by the Nagoya Castle Promotion Association (名古屋城振興協会)
chef_bio.origin: 名古屋城内 二之丸庭園 (Ninomaru Garden, Nagoya Castle)
chef_bio.background: 二の丸茶亭 is a traditional tearoom operated in connection with the Nagoya Castle Promotion Association (名古屋城振興協会) inside the grounds of Nagoya Castle, situated beside the nationally designated scenic Ninomaru Garden — historically the garden of the Owari Tokugawa lords and, at roughly 30,000 square meters, the largest castle-lord residential garden in Japan. The tearoom serves visitors as an integrated rest stop within one of Japan's most-visited castle complexes.
chef_bio.philosophy: A place to slow down mid-castle-visit and experience a moment of traditional tea culture, framed by a nationally designated scenic garden — the tearoom's ethos is quiet appreciation of season and setting rather than culinary ambition.
chef_bio.specialty: Matcha tea served with seasonal wagashi confections; gold-leaf matcha (金箔抹茶) is the premium signature offering

anecdotes.text: A replica of the golden tea kettle (金の茶釜の写し) — said to be modeled after one cast from melted gold shachihoko roof ornaments destroyed by wartime fire — is displayed inside the tearoom. On the 8th of each month a formal tea ceremony (お点前) is held using this kettle, and every Friday matcha is brewed from water heated in it for guests.

The Gold Shachi Morning (金シャチモーニング), offered daily 9:00–11:00 in limited quantities, pairs matcha, coffee, or orange juice with an original gold-shachi-shaped sweet toast topped with real gold leaf — a playful nod to Nagoya Castle's gilded roof ornaments.

Set near the east gate rather than the main entrance, 4travel reviewers note the tearoom is consistently less crowded than the main castle area — a quiet 穴場 (hidden gem) for a rest, with red cloth laid over the chairs and decorative red umbrellas.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 名古屋城公式サイト・busho-heart・名古屋観光情報等によれば、二の丸茶亭は名勝・二之丸庭園（尾張徳川家の大名庭園、約3万㎡で日本最大級）に隣接し、名古屋城振興協会が運営。抹茶・季節の和菓子・金箔抹茶を提供し、毎日9:00～11:00に金シャチモーニング（金箔入り金シャチトースト）を数量限定で出す。戦火のシャチホコを溶かして作ったと伝わる金の茶釜のレプリカを展示し、毎月8日にお点前、毎週金曜にこの釜の湯で抹茶を点てる。4travelでは空いた穴場と評される。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about kitchen separation or cross-contamination controls is publicly available. The tearoom is a small traditional operation with no allergen documentation found online.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce-based dishes appear on the menu. The menu is limited to matcha, wagashi sweets, and mitsumame — soy sauce is not a standard ingredient in any of these. However, wagashi recipes are not disclosed and hidden wheat starch in confections cannot be ruled out.

vegan_cross_contact.text: No dairy or egg dishes visible on the menu alongside the matcha and wagashi offerings, but no formal separation protocol confirmed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information found regarding staff allergy training or allergy-aware ordering procedures. The tearoom is a compact, city-run facility without documented allergy protocols.

positives.text: The core menu — plain matcha and mitsumame (agar jelly, sweet azuki beans, fruit) — uses ingredients that are traditionally wheat-free, dairy-free, and meat-free, making incidental safety plausible for those with moderate sensitivities.
Menu is short and simple (matcha, wagashi, mitsumame), reducing the number of unknowns compared to a full kitchen restaurant.

cultural_comfort.note: Nagoya Castle draws international visitors and has basic English signage, but the tearoom itself is a quiet, traditional Japanese space where ordering is done simply by pointing at the menu; minimal English interaction expected from staff.

places.name: カバッチ (Kabacchi)
places.gf_detail: The restaurant emphasizes organic, additive-free ingredients and avoids chemical seasonings, but makes no gluten-free claims. Brazilian-style stews and grills may involve wheat-containing soy sauce or marinades. No dedicated GF menu, no allergen labeling found online. Cross-contamination risk is unknown. Celiac diners should contact the restaurant directly before visiting.
places.vegan_detail: The menu centers on Brazilian meat dishes (picanha steak, moqueca seafood stew, beef-tongue curry) with organic vegetables on the side. No dedicated vegan dishes are listed. Hidden animal-based broths and animal proteins are likely throughout. Ask staff before ordering.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Fri 11:00–14:00 (L.O. 13:30), 18:00–21:00 (L.O. 20:30); closed Sat, Sun and public holidays
places.neighborhood: Marunouchi, Naka Ward (4 min walk from subway Marunouchi Stn Exit 8)
places.notes: Kabacchi is a Brazilian-cuisine cafe that opened on the 1st floor of the HP Marunouchi Building in Marunouchi, Nagoya in March 2026. Under a concept combining pesticide-free vegetables delivered directly from local farmers with traditional Brazilian dishes, it features a retro, cute interior that is easy for even a woman dining alone to enter.

chef_bio.background: カバッチは2026年3月2日に名古屋・丸の内のHP丸の内ビル1階にオープンしたブラジル料理カフェ。農薬不使用・減農薬の地元農家直送野菜とブラジル伝統料理を組み合わせたコンセプトを掲げ、女性一人でも入りやすいレトロで可愛らしい内装が特徴。
chef_bio.philosophy: 化学調味料に頼らず素材本来の旨みを大切に。地元農家から届くオーガニック野菜と南米の家庭料理の知恵を融合し、体に寄り添う食事を提供する。
chef_bio.specialty: ピカーニャ（ブラジル式300gステーキ）、ムケッカ（ブラジル伝統のココナッツミルクベースのシーフードシチュー）、姉妹店の牛タンを使った牛タンカレーランチ
