chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト（owst）・ホットペッパー・食べログ・地図情報によれば、カバッチは2026年3月2日に名古屋・丸の内のHP丸の内ビル1階にオープンしたブラジル料理カフェ。無農薬・減農薬の地元農家直送野菜と化学調味料不使用を掲げ、女性一人でも入りやすいレトロで可愛らしい内装。ピカーニャ、ムケッカ、姉妹焼肉店の牛タンを使った牛タンカレーランチが看板。

cultural_comfort.note: Welcoming solo-diner layout and online reservation via HotPepper suggest a moderate degree of accessibility, though the menu and signage are Japanese-only.

places.name: vegetable cafe ＆ seafood bar saien (saien)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free labeling or dedicated GF menu exists. The menu includes multiple wheat-containing items: burgers with buns, tempura (red snapper tempura listed), curry (roux likely contains wheat), and standard Japanese soy sauce used throughout. Soy sauce in Japan typically contains wheat. No dedicated fryer information found. Cross-contamination risk is high in a busy kitchen that fries tempura and uses wheat-based sauces. Celiacs should not eat here without direct staff consultation and should be prepared for limited safe options.
places.vegan_detail: The menu is vegetable-forward but definitively omnivorous. Lunch-focused chopped salads can be customized with vegetable toppings, and a vegetable curry is listed. However, the menu also includes tebasaki (chicken wings), oysters, wagyu beef, seafood, and Nagoya specialty meat dishes. No dishes are labeled vegan, and hidden animal-derived ingredients (dashi, bonito, cheese) are likely present in many preparations. Vegans should inquire carefully before ordering.
places.hours_raw: 10:30–22:30 (Sun only 10:30–20:00); closures follow Nagoya Castle
places.neighborhood: 中区二の丸, 金シャチ横丁宗春ゾーン（Nagoya Castle Kinshachi Yokocho）
places.cuisine: Organic vegetable cafe, chopped salad, seafo
places.notes: An independent dining concept opened within Kinshachi Yokocho, the food and souvenir complex built in 2018 alongside Nagoya Castle as part of a city-led revitalization project. The Soharu Zone where saien sits is themed around Tokugawa Munenari, one of the castle's historical lor

chef_bio.origin: Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture; within the Kinshachi Yokocho development built in 2018
chef_bio.background: An independent dining concept in Kinshachi Yokocho, the food and souvenir complex built in 2018 alongside Nagoya Castle as part of a city-led revitalization project. The 宗春 (Muneharu) Zone where saien sits is themed around Tokugawa Muneharu, the seventh Owari lord.
chef_bio.philosophy: The name 'saien' fuses two Japanese ideas: 菜園 (saien, vegetable garden) and 野菜で宴 (yasai de utage, a feast with vegetables). Owner Muto-san states the kitchen's commitment is to vegetables with vibrant colors used abundantly, sourced from the restaurant's own farm, paired with fresh seafood brought in daily from the market.
chef_bio.specialty: Chopped salad bowls (lunch) made with own-farm organic vegetables; soft-shell crab burger

anecdotes.text: The restaurant's concept draws on New York salad culture — building a complete, satisfying meal around a composed salad — and transplants it to the shadow of Nagoya Castle, combining it with local seafood and Nagoya specialties like tebasaki and dote-ni.

A collaboration menu tied to Nagoya's three great unifiers (Nobunaga, Hideyoshi, Ieyasu) featured a roasted-miso vegetable platter, an abalone cheese risotto with raclette, and red-snapper tempura-fritter with matcha salt, each priced at ¥999 as a nod to the castle grounds' history.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 金シャチ横丁公式・jouhou.nagoyaによれば、saienは名古屋城横の金シャチ横丁（2018年開業）の宗春ゾーンにある野菜×シーフードの店で、店名は『菜園』と『野菜で宴』を掛ける。自社農園のオーガニック野菜と市場直送の魚介を使い、ランチはNYサラダ文化に着想したチョップドサラダ、ソフトシェルクラブバーガーが看板。手羽先・どて煮など名古屋名物や、三英傑コラボの各¥999メニューも提供。オーナーは金鯱寿司と同じ武藤氏。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is on the menu (red snapper tempura listed in collaboration menus), indicating wheat-flour frying occurs in the kitchen. No information about a dedicated fryer or separate frying station was found.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce is mentioned anywhere in the menu or restaurant descriptions. Standard Japanese soy sauce (which contains wheat) is expected to be used in seasoning.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen prepares meat (tebasaki chicken wings, wagyu steak, pork), dairy (raclette cheese dishes), and seafood alongside vegetable dishes on the same line; no separation policy is documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy, allergy menu, or staff allergy training information was found across any source reviewed (official site, Tabelog, HotPepper, Retty, or blog coverage).

positives.text: The restaurant's core identity is vegetable-forward: it grows its own organic vegetables on a dedicated farm and sources fresh seafood daily, meaning the produce quality is high and plant-based dishes are a genuine focus rather than an afterthought.

cultural_comfort.note: A Japanese-language environment inside a popular tourist-facing complex at Nagoya Castle; staff serve international visitors regularly but menus and signage are primarily in Japanese.

places.name: Cafe & Bar REAL (Cafe&Bar REAL / カフェ&バー リアル)
places.gf_detail: A singer-songwriter-run cafe/bar that opened Sep 2024, marketing itself as a 「グルテンフリー食材を使用したCafe＆Bar」/「名古屋・グルテンフリーカフェ」 with rice-flour burgers, pizza, cheesecake and scones. This is bare 'uses GF ingredients' marketing — the JP guide ashiya-obentofesta explicitly notes 「完全なグルテンフリー専門店であるかどうかは確認が取れていません」 (cannot confirm it is a complete gluten-free specialist) and no dedicated wheat-free-kitchen claim is published. Risk items remain: a functioning bar serves barley beer/alcohol on-site, buckwheat mazesoba tare likely uses wheat soy sauce unless tamari, and cross-contact controls are unstated. Held at ask — confirm sauces, beer separation and shared equipment before eating.
places.vegan_detail: Not a vegan venue — 100% pork burger patties and dairy cheesecake feature. Soy-milk and non-caffeine drink options exist, so a few items may be adaptable; confirm case by case.
places.hours_raw: Cafe 11:00–15:00 / bar 18:00–23:00 (Sat/Sun/holidays continuous 11:00–23:00) / closed Mon (if a holiday, closed the following Tue)
places.neighborhood: 太閤通, Nakamura-ku (中村区太閤通), Nagoya
places.cuisine: Gluten-free cafe & bar — rice-flour burgers/pizza, buckwheat mazesoba
places.notes: [GF] Rice-flour cafe/bar (open Sep 2024) marketing GF ingredients, not a verified dedicated kitchen. A live bar pours barley beer/alcohol on the same premises and the 十割 buckwheat mazesoba sauce likely contains wheat soy sauce, so celiacs must ask about tamari, dedicated prep and cross-contact. Treat as ask, not celiac-safe.

chef_bio.background: Cafe&Bar REAL opened in September 2024 (13 Sept) on 太閤通 in Nakamura-ku, about a 3-minute walk from Taikodori Station. It is run by a singer-songwriter owner who focuses on gluten-free eating for health and built the shop around it. Lunch centres on a rice-flour hamburger with a house-baked bun and a 100%-pork binder-free patty, plus rice-flour cheesecake and scones; evenings run as a casual bar with drink-and-snack sets. Organic coffee/tea, non-caffeine and soy-milk options are offered.
chef_bio.philosophy: 「美味しく、健康的に」— not compromising taste just because it is gluten-free (グルテンフリーだからといって味を妥協しない).
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour hamburger (自家製バンズ・豚肉100%パティ) and rice-flour pizza, with buns and pizza dough baked in-house

anecdotes.text: The owner is a singer-songwriter who opened the cafe with a focus on health and gluten-free eating, aiming to help people with gluten sensitivities.
Burger patty is 100% pork with no binder; buns, pizza, cheesecake and scones are made in-house from rice flour with no additives.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト（cafebarreal.com）が名古屋・グルテンフリーカフェと標榜。Yahoo!エキスパート記事（大熊聡子）がシンガーソングライターのオーナーとGFメニューを紹介し、2024年9月13日オープン・太閤通と伝える。ashiya-obentofestaがメニューを列挙しつつ『完全なグルテンフリー専門店であるかどうかは確認が取れていません』と注意。

gf_cross_contamination.text: ashiya-obentofesta: 完全なグルテンフリー専門店であるかどうかは確認が取れていません — cannot confirm it is a complete GF specialist; contact directly for celiac/severe allergy.
Operates a live bar; barley beer/alcohol on the same premises is a cross-contact and dedicated-status concern.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 十割 (100% buckwheat) mazesoba is offered, but its tare/sauce likely uses wheat-containing soy sauce unless tamari — unstated; ask.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Pork patties and dairy on-site; not a vegan kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No published allergen/cross-contact protocol; JP guide advises confirming directly for celiac.

positives.text: Owner personally follows a gluten-free diet after illness; in-house rice-flour buns, pizza, cheesecake, scones; soy-milk/non-caffeine drinks.

places.name: COrPO (やさしいスイーツカフェ COrPO)
places.gf_detail: COrPO is a dedicated gluten-free cafe and bakery: it bills itself as a shop that 「小麦を使用しない、全てグルテンフリーの商品を提供しております」 (uses no wheat and provides exclusively gluten-free products) and 「小麦粉、白砂糖不使用」 (no wheat flour, no refined sugar). Wheat flour is not used or brought onto the premises; the rice-flour bread is 100% 米粉 (Kumamoto rice flour) with 「小麦粉一切不使用」, and even lunch seasonings are stated to be entirely gluten-free. Outside food is prohibited except certified wheat-free baby food, which further limits any wheat cross-contact. As always with a single kitchen, verify shared-fryer / airborne-flour handling directly, but the venue's whole model is wheat-free by design.
places.vegan_detail: Not a vegan venue. The menu centers on dairy, eggs and meat — roast pork, loco moco hamburger, shrimp, French toast, cream-topped crepes and tart cream. A few items lean plant-forward (e.g. the seasonal 夏野菜カレー summer-vegetable curry, and a kids' hamburger made without eggs using a miso binder), but none are confirmed fully vegan. Ask staff about a specific dish if you need it dairy/egg-free.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Tue, Thu–Sun, holidays & day before holidays 11:00–18:00 (L.O. 17:30) / closed Wed. Lunch 11:00–14:00.
places.neighborhood: 中川区荒子 (Arako, Nakagawa-ku), Nagoya
places.cuisine: Gluten-free cafe — rice-flour bread, lunch plates, tarts & parfaits
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] Fully wheat-free cafe in Arako (4 min from Arako Station on the Aonami Line, ~13 min from Takahata subway). Everything sold — rice-flour breads, lunch plates and tarts — is gluten-free, and wheat flour is neither used nor allowed onto the premises, which makes it one of the safer celiac stops in western Nagoya. It runs in cooperation with a pastry shop (reported as Firenze/フィレンツェ), but the COrPO shop itself is wheat-free; still confirm frying and airborne-flour handling if you are highly sensitive. Closed Wednesdays; whole tarts/cakes need ~3 days' advance order.

chef_bio.background: COrPO opened in Arako, Nakagawa-ku, Nagoya as a gluten-free specialty cafe and bakery, run in cooperation with a local pastry shop reported by Nagoya food media as Firenze (フィレンツェ). The name COrPO is Italian for 'body' (身体), reflecting a body-kind, gut-friendly concept aimed at people with wheat allergy and those wanting to improve intestinal health. The shop's stated aim is an inclusive table where people with and without allergies can eat together without restriction, offering bread, lunches and sweets made without wheat flour or refined sugar and with an emphasis on organic ingredients. Individual owner/chef names are not published in Japanese sources reviewed.
chef_bio.philosophy: 「身体に 家族に 地域にやさしい パン・スイーツのある食生活を提供するお店」 — food that is kind to body, family and community; wheat- and refined-sugar-free, organic-leaning, and inclusive of allergy and non-allergy diners alike.
chef_bio.specialty: 100% rice-flour (米粉) bread, gluten-free lunch plates, and seasonal fruit tarts/parfaits made without wheat flour or white sugar

anecdotes.text: The signature rice-flour bread is made from Kumamoto rice flour with 「小麦粉一切不使用」 (absolutely no wheat flour) and can be bought to take away without dining in.
COrPO means 'body' in Italian, chosen to signal a body-conscious, gluten-free and organic dining concept.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト・食べログ・なごなび・名古屋メシ等によれば、名古屋市中川区荒子のグルテンフリー専門カフェで、小麦粉・白砂糖不使用、米粉パンやランチ・タルトを提供。パティスリー（フィレンツェ）と協力して運営。店名COrPOはイタリア語で『身体』の意。店主・シェフの個人名は公表されていない。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Dedicated gluten-free specialty shop: 「小麦を使用しない、全てグルテンフリーの商品を提供しております」— no wheat is used and all products are gluten-free; outside food is banned except certified wheat-free baby food.
Rice-flour bread is 100% 米粉 with 「小麦粉一切不使用」 (no wheat flour whatsoever).

soy_sauce_wheat.text: All lunch seasonings are stated to be gluten-free (「調味料もすべてグルテンフリー」-style claim), consistent with the shop's wheat-free premises.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Positioned explicitly for people with wheat/flour allergy and gut-health goals; the whole operation is wheat-free by design.

positives.text: Entire menu — breads, lunches, sweets — is gluten-free; wheat flour is neither used nor brought onto the premises.
Uses no wheat flour and no refined white sugar, with organic-leaning sourcing.

places.name: Creperiz Stand Nagoya (Creperiz Stand Nagoya)
places.gf_detail: A 100% rice-flour crepe stand run by a Hokkaido rice-farming family (木村農園), using their own ゆめぴりか rice. The shop's own claim: 「米粉を100%使用、クレープもクリームも小麦不使用」; press describes it as 「小麦もオーツ麦も蕎麦も使っていない、米粉100%のグルテンフリークレープ専門店」 (no wheat, oats or buckwheat — 100% rice-flour gluten-free crepe specialist). Even the custard cream is made from rice flour rather than wheat (米粉から特製ライスカスタード). No formal celiac cross-contamination protocol is published, but the whole stand is wheat-free; highly sensitive celiacs should confirm shared-utensil handling in person.
places.vegan_detail: Crepes use butter and a rice-flour custard cream (dairy), and toppings include cheese, egg and prosciutto, so it is not vegan by default. No dedicated vegan crepe is listed. Vegans should ask whether a butter/cream-free option is possible.
places.hours_raw: 10:00–20:00, no fixed closing day (RELOCATING — Osu shop closes end of March 2026; reopening near Nagoya Station ~mid-2026)
places.neighborhood: 中区大須 (Osu), Naka-ku, Nagoya — relocating near Nagoya Station
places.notes: [GF] 100% rice-flour crepe stand — crepe batter AND custard cream are wheat-free (no wheat, oats or buckwheat). RELOCATION ALERT: the Osu shop (中区大須3-30-25 大信ビル 合点承知1階) is scheduled to close at the end of March 2026 for building seismic-reinforcement work, and the stand plans to reopen near Nagoya Station around mid-2026 — confirm current location and hours via Instagram @creperiz_sta.nagoya before visiting. Stand-only, takeout, no seating.

chef_bio.origin: Rankoshi, Hokkaido (蘭越町) — 木村農園 rice farm
chef_bio.background: Creperiz Stand is run by the fifth generation of 木村農園, a long-established rice-farming family in Rankoshi, Hokkaido. They first opened the parent café 'cafe creperiz' in Hokkaido in March 2021 to showcase their own ゆめぴりか rice, then debuted the Nagoya stand in Osu (Naka-ku) on 7 February 2025. The concept is a 100% rice-flour crepe — including a rice-flour custard cream — made without wheat, oats or buckwheat so that people with wheat allergies can enjoy crepes.
chef_bio.philosophy: Showcase the family's own Hokkaido rice by making crepes and cream entirely from rice flour, so that wheat-allergic customers can enjoy crepes with no wheat, oats or buckwheat.
chef_bio.specialty: 米粉100% crepes with rice-flour custard cream (e.g. いちごWクリーム strawberry double-cream)

anecdotes.text: The custard cream is intentionally made from rice flour rather than wheat — 米粉から特製ライスカスタード.
A wheat-allergic reviewer reported being able to ask the staff about ingredients and eat with peace of mind (安心して食べられる).

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: jouhou.nagoyaは米粉100%・小麦不使用（生地もカスタードも）、北海道蘭越の米農家（木村農園5代目）が運営、2021年3月に道内の親店、2025年2月7日に大須の名古屋スタンドを開業と記載。nhkomorebiは小麦・オーツ麦・蕎麦不使用で、小麦アレルギーの客が店員に確認して安心して食べられたと報告。Vegewel #2544がGFメニュー・大須の住所・10:00～20:00・スタンド営業を掲載。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 100% rice-flour stand — no wheat, oats or buckwheat used, and even the cream is rice-flour based, so wheat cross-contact is inherently minimal. No formal celiac protocol published.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Butter, dairy custard cream, cheese and egg are used; not a vegan kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: A wheat-allergic reviewer said she felt able to ask staff about ingredients and ate confidently.

positives.text: Batter AND custard cream are 100% rice flour; explicitly no wheat, oats or buckwheat.
Rice-farmer-run using the family's own Hokkaido ゆめぴりか rice.

places.name: en-kitchen cafe (エンキッチン)
places.gf_detail: en-kitchen is an entirely wheat-free vegetable cafe: the venue describes itself as a 「卵・牛乳・小麦粉を使用しない野菜カフェ」 (vegetable cafe that uses no eggs, milk or wheat flour), and its own claim is that 「店内の全てのメニューが小麦・卵・乳製品不使用…調理器具も完全に分けており、小麦粉の持ち込みは一切禁止」 (every item on the menu is wheat/egg/dairy-free, cooking utensils are completely separated, and bringing any wheat flour onto the premises is strictly forbidden). Bread is baked from 米粉 (rice flour) rather than wheat, and Japanese reviewers confirm that non-allergy-safe food may not be brought into the shop (アレルギー対応でない食品の持ち込みは禁止). Because the whole kitchen is wheat-free this is one of the safest options in Nagoya for celiacs, but as always confirm seasoning details (tamari vs. wheat soy sauce) with staff on the day.
places.vegan_detail: The cafe is plant-forward and egg- and dairy-free across the entire menu, and all in-store sweets are vegan (卵・乳製品・小麦粉を使用していない). Several mains — the gluten-free brown-rice pasta, the vegan sandwich and the kids' plate — are fully vegan, and staples like the 健美膳 lunch set and the vegetarian curry can be made vegan on request. Some dishes (e.g. a fish sauté noted in one write-up) may include fish, so specify a vegan version when ordering.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sun 10:30–19:00; closed Mondays
places.neighborhood: 中区大井町 (Higashi-Betsuin, Naka-ku, Nagoya)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] Fully wheat-free vegetable cafe near Higashi-Betsuin station: no wheat flour is used anywhere, utensils are kept separate, and wheat flour is banned from the premises, so cross-contact risk is very low. Bread is rice-flour (米粉) based and all sweets are wheat/egg/dairy-free. It is plant-forward but not 100% vegan (some fish options), so celiac vegans should still confirm both axes; ask staff to confirm wheat-free soy sauce (tamari) for any seasoned dish.

chef_bio.background: Owner-chef Chiaki trained at Tsuji culinary school (辻調) in French and Italian cuisine, apprenticed in Italian restaurants, and assisted the food researcher Aiko Tanaka before working abroad in Canada, New York and Australia. She founded en-kitchen in Tokyo in 2010, opened the Nagoya vegetable cafe in 2016 specialising in egg-, dairy- and wheat-free cooking, and established the company in 2017. Tabelog notes that Chiaki has managed food allergies herself since childhood, which shaped the shop's allergen-free focus.
chef_bio.philosophy: 「食の選択肢を日本へ、世界へ」 — to expand food choices (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free and allergen-free) in Japan and worldwide, supporting well-being and everyday dining for people with dietary restrictions.
chef_bio.specialty: Egg-, dairy- and wheat-free plant-based cooking, gluten-free lunches and 米粉 (rice-flour) baking.

anecdotes.text: Owner Chiaki has personally managed food allergies since childhood, which underpins the cafe's entirely allergen-conscious menu.
en-kitchen began in Tokyo in 2010; the Nagoya cafe opened in 2016 as a 卵・牛乳・小麦を使用しない野菜カフェ (vegetable cafe using no eggs, milk or wheat).

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト（en-kitchen.com / about-us）によると、代表のChiakiは辻調で仏伊料理を学び、料理研究家・田中愛子氏のアシスタントを経て海外（カナダ・NY・豪州）で活動後、2010年に東京でen-kitchenを、2016年に名古屋の野菜カフェを開業、2017年に法人化。食べログには『幼少期から食物アレルギーと付き合ってきた』旨の記載があり、卵・牛乳・小麦粉不使用の方針の背景となっている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Venue's own claim: 「店内の全てのメニューが小麦・卵・乳製品不使用…調理器具も完全に分けており、小麦粉の持ち込みは一切禁止」 — every menu item is wheat/egg/dairy-free, utensils are completely separated, and no wheat flour may be brought onto the premises.
The entire cafe uses no wheat flour (卵・牛乳・小麦粉を使用しない野菜カフェ); bread is made from rice flour (米粉パン), so there is no in-house wheat baking to cross-contaminate.
Japanese reviewers note that food not compliant with the cafe's allergen policy may not be brought into the shop (アレルギー対応でない食品の持ち込みは禁止).

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The whole menu is declared wheat-free, which implies any soy-based seasoning is a wheat-free type (tamari); this was not explicitly documented, so celiacs should confirm on the day.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Cafe is egg- and dairy-free throughout and all sweets are vegan, but some dishes may contain fish, so vegans should request a plant-based version.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Owner Chiaki has managed food allergies since childhood; the shop is built around allergen-free (卵・牛乳・小麦不使用) cooking.
Reviewers advise contacting the cafe in advance if you have specific allergy or ingredient concerns (アレルギーや食材が気になる方は事前に問い合わせを).

positives.text: 100% wheat-free kitchen with separated utensils and a ban on wheat flour entering the premises — very low celiac cross-contact risk.
Owner has lifelong personal experience of food allergies, so allergen handling is a core focus.
Kids' plate excludes the 27 major allergens, indicating serious allergen-management practice.

places.name: EWALU (エワル)
places.gf_detail: A rice-farmer-run bakery whose own claim is 「お米農家が営む完全グルテンフリー専門店」 (a completely gluten-free specialty shop run by a rice farmer); the shop states 「小麦粉、卵、乳製品、大豆など、アレルギーの原因となるものは一切使用していません」 (uses no wheat flour, egg, dairy, soy or other allergens at all). Everything — bread, bagels and cakes — is made from 100% self-grown rice flour, so there is no wheat on the premises. The complete-GF claim is clear, but there is no explicit dedicated-kitchen statement or third-party celiac report published, so keep at high and CONFIRM cross-contamination handling on visit.
places.vegan_detail: The shop avoids egg, dairy and soy in its core line, so plain rice-flour breads and some bagels are effectively vegan; however sweet bagels and cakes may use butter/cream cheese (e.g. blueberry cream-cheese bagel), so vegan status varies by item — ask per product.
places.hours_raw: 8:00–17:00 (bread sales from ~9:30–9:45; morning service 8:00–10:00). Closed Wednesdays.
places.neighborhood: 弥富市鯏浦町車東 (Ujiura, Yatomi City, Aichi) — near Kintetsu Yatomi Station, western Owari / greater Nagoya area
places.cuisine: Gluten-free rice-flour bakery (bread, bagels, cakes) with dog run
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] — A 100% rice-flour bakery with no wheat on site (own claim: 完全グルテンフリー専門店), so bread and bagels are safe for celiacs; because no explicit dedicated-kitchen/CC protocol or celiac audit is published, confirm cross-contact handling when you visit. Buying bread lets you use the on-site dog run; terrace seating is dog-friendly.

chef_bio.background: EWALU is run by a rice-farming family from the Nishi-Owari area of Aichi with over 50 years of agricultural history. They mill their own 100%-self-cultivated rice into flour and bake completely gluten-free bread, bagels and cakes, positioning the shop as 「お米農家が営む完全グルテンフリー専門店」. The premises in Yatomi include a dog run and dog-friendly terrace, and the shop also sells online via BASE. Individual owner names are not published in available Japanese sources.
chef_bio.philosophy: Let people enjoy rice — turning home-grown rice into gluten-free bread free of the common allergens (wheat, egg, dairy, soy).
chef_bio.specialty: 100% rice-flour 'mochi-mochi' loaf and rice-flour bagels.

anecdotes.text: Buying bread grants access to the on-site dog run, and the terrace seating is dog-friendly.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式BASEショップ・Instagram・あまつしまっぷ・米パンなび・食べログより、西尾張の米農家（50年超の歴史）が自家栽培米100%で小麦・卵・乳・大豆不使用の完全グルテンフリーパン／ベーグル／ケーキを製造、店名は『お米農家が営む完全グルテンフリー専門店』。弥富市にドッグラン併設であることを確認。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shop is a 100% rice-flour operation with no wheat flour on premises (完全グルテンフリー専門店), minimizing airborne/flour cross-contact; no explicit dedicated-kitchen protocol or celiac audit is published, so confirm CC handling on visit.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: A bakery — no soy sauce central to products; the shop states it uses no wheat, egg, dairy or soy at all in its core line.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Core line avoids egg/dairy/soy, but some sweet items add butter or cream cheese, so animal-ingredient contact varies by product.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No documented allergen-handling protocol found in available sources; recommend confirming directly.

positives.text: Uses 100% self-grown rice flour and declares no wheat, egg, dairy or soy — a genuinely wheat-free bakery.

places.name: F and (エフアンド)
places.gf_detail: A pasta bistro and cafe whose tagline is 「パスタとグルテンフリーのお菓子のお店」 — pasta plus gluten-free sweets. Wheat pasta can be swapped for gluten-free (rice) pasta, but the shop explicitly warns 「小麦が気になる方にはグルテンフリーパスタへの変更も可能です。（小麦麺と同じ鍋を使用しますので、アレルギーの保証は致しかねます）」 — the GF pasta is boiled in the same pot as wheat noodles, so no allergy guarantee. Their real GF strength is the baked goods: gluten-free cakes and 焼き菓子 made with 米粉 and そば粉 (note そば = buckwheat, a separate major allergen). For a celiac, treat the savoury/pasta side as cross-contaminated and rely on the dedicated gluten-free sweets, confirming cross-contact and buckwheat with staff.
places.vegan_detail: Menu centers on pasta, meat, seafood and dairy-rich dishes; a few vegetable plates (carrot rapé, etc.) may suit vegetarians but vegan options are not marketed. Ask per dish.
places.hours_raw: Lunch 11:30–14:30 (L.O.); Cafe & sweets from 11:30; Dinner 17:30–21:00; Bar 21:00–23:00. Closed Mondays and 2nd & 4th Sundays.
places.neighborhood: 守山区弁天が丘 (Bentengaoka, Moriyama-ku), Nagoya — near Ōmori-Kinjōgakuinmae Stn
places.cuisine: Pasta bistro / cafe with gluten-free sweets
places.notes: [GF] Pasta bistro whose GF appeal is its dedicated gluten-free sweets (米粉 and そば粉 baked goods, also sold nationwide via BASE). The GF pasta swap shares a boiling pot with wheat noodles — the shop itself says it can't guarantee for allergies — so celiacs should stick to the GF baked goods and confirm cross-contact + buckwheat content. A GF gratin (¥880) is on the menu. Reservations available; phone 052-886-0451.

chef_bio.background: エフアンド (F&..) opened in Moriyama-ku's Bentengaoka in December 2021 as 「パスタとグルテンフリーのお菓子のお店」 — a fresh-boiled-pasta and galette bistro that also makes gluten-free sweets. The owner offers proprietor-selected natural wines and runs the room lunch-through-bar. Gluten-free cakes and baked goods (using domestic rice flour and buckwheat flour) are a signature and are sold nationwide through the shop's BASE online store.
chef_bio.philosophy: Handmade fresh pasta and galettes alongside a genuine gluten-free confection line; natural wines and a relaxed cafe-to-bar format.
chef_bio.specialty: Fresh-boiled pasta, galettes, and gluten-free cakes/焼き菓子

anecdotes.text: The shop states gluten-free pasta substitution is possible but is boiled in the same pot as wheat noodles, so it cannot guarantee against allergies (小麦麺と同じ鍋を使用しますので、アレルギーの保証は致しかねます).

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイトが『パスタとグルテンフリーのお菓子のお店』と明記し、グルテンフリーパスタは小麦麺と同じ鍋使用でアレルギー保証不可と注意書き。日刊KELLYが2021年12月オープンを紹介。焼き菓子は米粉・そば粉を使い、BASEで通販。

gf_cross_contamination.text: GF (rice) pasta is boiled in the same pot as wheat noodles; the shop states it cannot guarantee for allergies. GF sweets use both 米粉 and そば粉 (buckwheat), so shared confection equipment plus a separate buckwheat allergen apply.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy, meat and seafood are pervasive on the menu; vegan handling not addressed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The shop is upfront about the shared-pot limitation, which suggests some allergy awareness — ask staff to steer you to the dedicated gluten-free baked goods rather than the pasta swap.

positives.text: Maintains a dedicated gluten-free cake and baked-goods line (米粉/そば粉), also sold nationwide via BASE.

places.name: Haruno-hana (はるのはな)
places.gf_detail: A 100% rice-flour bakery and cafe with no wheat on the premises — the venue states 「小麦・卵・乳不使用、米粉を100%使用したグルテンフリーのパン」 and 「メニューはもちろん全てが"グルテンフリー" 小麦・卵・乳不使用のランチを２種類ご用意しています」. Aichi's first rice-flour bagel specialist; owner Eriko Kondo is a registered dietitian (管理栄養士). Every item — bagels, bread, and both lunch sets — is wheat/egg/dairy-free, so cross-contact risk is inherently very low. As with any bakery, confirm current allergen handling directly if you have a severe reaction, but this is a genuinely dedicated GF facility.
places.vegan_detail: All food is egg- and dairy-free, which makes much of the plant-based. Lunch-A (日替わりランチ, vegetable deli plate with 8–10 seasonal sides) is largely meat-free and vegan-friendly; the spice curry (Lunch-B) and some bagels may contain animal-derived ingredients. Ask which items are fully vegan on the day.
places.hours_raw: Cafe & bread sales 11:00–15:00 (lunch L.O. 14:00; bread until sold out). Cafe days and bread-sales days are generally scheduled separately — check the Instagram operating calendar (@bagel_harunohana) for open dates.
places.neighborhood: 千種区萱場 (Kayaba, Chikusa-ku, Nagoya)
places.cuisine: Gluten-free rice-flour bakery & cafe (bagels, bread, lunch)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A fully dedicated wheat/egg/dairy-free rice-flour bakery and cafe — one of the safest celiac options in Nagoya. Nothing on the premises contains wheat, so bagels, bread and lunch plates are all safe. Opening days are irregular (cafe and bread days differ); confirm via the shop's Instagram calendar before making the ~15 min walk from Nagoya Dome-mae Yada Station.

chef_bio.background: Haruno-hana opened on 4 April 2025 in Kayaba, Chikusa-ku, on the site of the former katsudon shop 'Arata' (かつ丼あらた) along Dekimachi-dori. It is described as Aichi's first specialist rice-flour bagel shop, opened after two years of online sales and pop-ups. Owner Eriko Kondo is a registered dietitian who develops each product with nutritional balance and individual allergy responses in mind. The shop uses domestic rice flour blended with white-millet flour and potato starch, and emphasises pesticide-free or reduced-pesticide ingredients bought directly from local farmers.
chef_bio.philosophy: That everyone can enjoy the same food together regardless of allergies — all products are wheat-, egg- and dairy-free so allergic and non-allergic guests share one table.
chef_bio.specialty: 100% rice-flour bagels (savory, sweet and hard-crust styles) and gluten-free lunch plates.

anecdotes.text: Billed as Aichi Prefecture's first shop specialising in rice-flour bagels when it opened in April 2025, after two years of mail-order and pop-up trading.

Owner Eriko Kondo, a registered dietitian, formulates products considering nutritional balance and individual allergy responses, using domestic rice flour plus white-millet flour and potato starch, and pesticide-reduced produce from local farmers.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト（haruno-hana.com）は全商品が小麦・卵・乳不使用の米粉100%で、ランチも両セットともグルテンフリーと記載。地域メディア（chiku-san・goguynet・bagel-atsume）が2025年4月4日、千種区萱場（旧かつ丼あらた跡）に愛知県初の米粉ベーグル専門店として開業したことを記録。komeko-paletteのGF名古屋ガイドが店主・近藤恵理子を管理栄養士とし、国産米粉に白キビ粉・馬鈴薯でんぷんを配合、地元農家の減農薬食材を使うと紹介。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 100% rice-flour bakery with no wheat used anywhere; the venue states 「小麦・卵・乳不使用、米粉を100%使用したグルテンフリーのパン」, so cross-contact from wheat is not a factor on the premises.
Cafe menu is entirely wheat/egg/dairy-free: 「メニューはもちろん全てが"グルテンフリー" 小麦・卵・乳不使用のランチ」.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Lunch plates are declared wheat-free, implying no standard (wheat-based) soy sauce is used; confirm the seasoning on the day if highly sensitive.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All items are egg- and dairy-free by policy, reducing vegan cross-contact concerns; meat may appear in some lunch/bagel items.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Owner is a registered dietitian who develops products with individual allergy responses in mind.

positives.text: Dedicated wheat/egg/dairy-free facility — nothing containing wheat is made or sold on site.
Aichi's first rice-flour bagel specialist, run by a registered dietitian using domestic rice flour.

places.name: Komehana (米花)
places.gf_detail: A rice-flour (米粉) bread specialty bakery and cafe. The shop states 「パンは米粉を80％以上使用して作っています」 and separately offers 米粉100％のグルテンフリー商品 free of wheat, egg and dairy. The critical caveat for celiacs: the everyday 80%-rice-flour breads still contain wheat gluten, and both wheat-containing and 100% rice-flour items are baked in the same small kitchen, so only the explicitly 100% rice-flour (gluten-free) line is wheat-free and even that carries shared-facility cross-contact risk. The komeko-palette guide notes the shop does not publish contamination-prevention protocols, so a celiac should confirm the 100% GF line and how it is separated before buying.
places.vegan_detail: The 100% rice-flour gluten-free line is made without egg or dairy, so some items are effectively vegan; the standard breads are not necessarily vegan. Confirm per item.
places.hours_raw: Tue/Wed/Fri 11:00–19:00; some Saturdays (2nd/4th/5th) 11:00–17:00; closed Mon/Thu/Sun. Pre-order (by noon the day before) recommended.
places.neighborhood: 東区東桜 (Higashisakura, Higashi-ku), Nagoya — near Takaoka Stn
places.notes: [GF] Rice-flour bakery with a genuine 100% rice-flour, wheat/egg/dairy-free line — but everyday breads are only 80%+ rice flour (still contain wheat) and everything shares one kitchen. Not suitable for a strict wheat allergy without confirming the 100% GF line and separation. Small operation, few open days, pre-order the day before by noon. Phone 052-957-3151.

chef_bio.background: 米花 is a small owner-run rice-flour bread specialty shop in Higashi-ku's Higashisakura (東桜), a short walk from Takaoka Station. It bills itself as a 米粉パン専門店 / グルテンフリー / カフェ and is run as a husband-and-wife operation, the wife baking and the husband handling the shopfront. All breads use 80% or more rice flour, and the shop additionally bakes a fully gluten-free 100% rice-flour line as well as breads made without wheat, egg or dairy. Bread is handmade in small batches, with pre-orders taken by email/contact form/phone the day before.
chef_bio.philosophy: Moist, chewy rice-flour bread, with a fully gluten-free line and breads free of wheat, egg and dairy for those with allergies.
chef_bio.specialty: 米粉パン (rice-flour bread), bagels, focaccia

anecdotes.text: The shop makes all its bread with 80%+ rice flour and offers a fully gluten-free 100% rice-flour line, plus breads that use no wheat, egg or dairy.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト（wix）が米粉80％以上・米粉100％グルテンフリー商品を明記。nagoya-meshi.com が高岳のベーカリーカフェとして紹介し、卵・牛乳・バター不使用のパンの存在も確認。食べログが米粉100％パンとベーグル取り扱いを確認。コンタミ対策の記載は乏しい点に留意（セリアック注意）。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat-containing 80%-rice-flour breads and the 100% rice-flour GF line are baked in the same kitchen; the komeko-palette guide notes no published contamination-prevention protocol.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The 100% rice-flour line is made without egg or dairy, but shares equipment with standard breads.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Pre-order system by phone/email the day before allows requesting the 100% gluten-free items; confirm separation directly.

positives.text: Maintains an explicit 米粉100% gluten-free line free of wheat, egg and dairy, additive-free.

places.name: Komeko no Mise Palette (こめこの店Palette)
places.gf_detail: A dedicated gluten-free rice-flour bakery/cafe near Ozone/Morishita. The venue explicitly states 「製造施設内への小麦の持ち込みは完全禁止、米粉パン専用の設備・器具のみを使用し、コンタミネーション（混入）リスクはゼロ」 — wheat is completely banned from the production facility, only rice-bread-only equipment is used, and cross-contamination risk is zero. Bagels and breads are additive-free (no preservatives/colorants/emulsifiers), made only from domestic (国産) rice flour, natural yeast, natural salt, kibi sugar and rice oil. This is a genuinely dedicated, celiac-oriented facility.
places.vegan_detail: The bagels and breads are additive-free and made from simple plant-based ingredients (rice flour, natural yeast, salt, kibi sugar, rice oil), so many are vegan-friendly. However, lunch items such as rice-flour gyoza and shumai typically contain meat, and some bagels (e.g. cheese/azuki-with-additions) vary — ask which items are fully plant-based on the day.
places.hours_raw: 8:00–19:00 general; morning bagel set 9:00–10:30 (Tabelog: 8:00–10:30); lunch 11:00–14:00/15:00; some evening service 17:00–20:00 (L.O. 19:00). Operating days are irregular — phone ahead (080-9560-4339) to confirm before visiting.
places.neighborhood: 北区東大杉町 (Higashi-Osugi-cho, Kita-ku, Nagoya — near Ozone/Morishita)
places.cuisine: Gluten-free rice-flour bakery & cafe (bagels, gyoza, shumai, lunch)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A dedicated rice-flour-only facility that bans wheat entirely from production and states cross-contamination risk is zero — one of Nagoya's safest celiac bakeries. About 16 bagel varieties plus rice-flour gyoza/shumai (handmade wrappers) and lunch sets. Opening days are irregular; call 080-9560-4339 (PayPay accepted, no credit cards) and reserve if you want a table. ~4 min walk from Morishita Station near Ozone.

chef_bio.background: Komeko no Mise Palette opened on 30 November 2022 in a residential pocket of Kita-ku near Ozone/Morishita Station. It is a gluten-free specialist built around domestic rice flour, offering a wide bagel range (around 16 kinds, including red-bean/azuki and cinnamon-raisin) alongside handmade rice-flour gyoza and shumai whose wrappers are made from scratch for a chewy rice texture. The shop's guiding idea is food children and the wheat-sensitive can eat safely, so the entire production facility is kept wheat-free.
chef_bio.philosophy: Gentle, safe food everyone — including children and people who must avoid wheat — can eat with confidence; a wheat-free, rice-only production line.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour bagels (around 16 kinds) and handmade rice-flour gyoza/shumai; morning bagel sets and rice-flour lunches.

anecdotes.text: Bagel morning set (¥500) served from opening until 10:30 — choose a bagel plus a drink such as coffee.
Rice-flour gyoza and shumai are made with hand-rolled wrappers, giving a distinctively chewy (mochi-mochi) texture unique to rice flour.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: The shop's own About page (komeko-palette.com) and Tokai TV coverage confirm a dedicated wheat-free production facility with 国産 (domestic) rice flour, wild-yeast/cane-sugar bagels, and handmade rice-flour gyoza/shumai (red-bean and cinnamon-raisin bagels named). Tabelog (23082110) confirms opening 30 Nov 2022, address 北区東大杉町4-39-2, Morishita Station, PayPay payment.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Venue states 「製造施設内への小麦の持ち込みは完全禁止、米粉パン専用の設備・器具のみを使用し、コンタミネーション（混入）リスクはゼロ」 — wheat completely banned from the facility, rice-bread-only equipment, zero contamination risk.
MAFF Tokai regional 米粉店 mapping and Tabelog list it as a rice-flour/gluten-free specialist.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: As a wheat-free facility, savory items (gyoza/shumai) avoid wheat-based components; confirm the dipping sauce/seasoning is tamari-based or wheat-free if highly sensitive.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bagels/breads use only plant ingredients (rice flour, natural yeast, salt, kibi sugar, rice oil); meat appears in gyoza/shumai lunch items.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Whole-facility wheat ban reflects an allergy-first operation aimed at children and the wheat-sensitive.

positives.text: Explicit zero-contamination claim: wheat banned from the facility, rice-only equipment.
Additive-free bagels from 100% domestic rice flour, natural yeast, natural salt, kibi sugar and rice oil.

places.name: Lilac (ライラック)
places.gf_detail: Lilac is an all-gluten-free takeout patisserie that uses zero wheat flour in every product. The shop's own claim: 「小麦粉を一切使用しないオールグルテンフリー」, and it states 「小麦粉を使わず、米粉やホワイトソルガムキビ粉、タピオカ粉、葛粉、玄米粉など8種類以上の粉類を使用しています」 (no wheat flour; more than 8 kinds of flour — rice, white sorghum, tapioca, kudzu, brown rice, etc.). East Tokai TV (2025) and Nagoya press confirm all products are wheat-free. No explicit celiac cross-contamination or shared-facility protocol is published, so highly-sensitive celiacs should confirm directly; because the entire kitchen is wheat-free, airborne-flour risk is inherently low.
places.vegan_detail: This is a Western-style patisserie built on butter, fresh cream, cheese and eggs, so it is not vegan-friendly overall. A few egg-free items exist (e.g. 卵不使用サブレ egg-free sablé cookies) but these still contain dairy. Vegans should ask about specific items.
places.hours_raw: 10:00–19:00, closed Wednesdays (plus occasional irregular closures)
places.neighborhood: 千種区東山通 (Motoyama), Chikusa-ku, Nagoya
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] Every item is gluten-free — the shop uses no wheat flour at all, blending 8+ alternative flours (rice, white sorghum, tapioca, kudzu, brown rice) per recipe. Takeout only; signature 本山チーズタルト (Motoyama cheese tart). Because the whole kitchen is wheat-free, cross-contact risk is inherently low, but no formal celiac protocol is published, so highly sensitive customers should confirm shared-equipment details in person.

chef_bio.background: Owner-pâtissier Hiroshi Oki opened Lilac in Motoyama, Chikusa-ku on 30 January 2017 after training at established confectioneries in Nagoya and Gifu. He built the shop around fully gluten-free sweets, using no wheat flour and instead blending more than eight kinds of alternative flours per recipe. The concept extends to body-friendly ingredients — minimal white sugar, no artificial colorants or preservatives — while keeping premium butter and cream for texture.
chef_bio.philosophy: Prove that wheat-free sweets can match conventional patisserie in texture and taste by custom-blending multiple gluten-free flours for each item, while keeping ingredients gentle on the body.
chef_bio.specialty: ふわとろチーズタルト (melt-in-the-mouth cheese tart)

anecdotes.text: Signature 本山チーズタルト (Motoyama Cheese Tart) is the shop's number-one item, described as having a soft, molten center despite using no wheat.
Tokai TV NEWS featured Lilac in 2025 as a gluten-free specialist serving customers with wheat allergies as well as health-conscious families.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: venue site lilacs.jp states 小麦粉を一切使用しないオールグルテンフリー and blends several flours per item; 東海テレビNEWS (2025) and jouhou.nagoya confirm all-wheat-free, opened 2017-01, Ferio Motoyama 1F, and the 本山チーズタルト as the number-one seller.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The entire shop uses no wheat flour whatsoever, so cross-contact with wheat is inherently minimal; however, no formal celiac cross-contamination protocol is published.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Butter, fresh cream, cheese and eggs are used throughout; not a vegan-oriented kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Press reports the shop serves customers with wheat allergies and emphasizes body-friendly ingredients (minimal colorants/preservatives), but published allergen-handling detail is limited.

positives.text: 100% wheat-free kitchen using 8+ gluten-free flours (rice, white sorghum, tapioca, kudzu, brown rice).
Opened 2017 as a dedicated gluten-free sweets specialist; every product is gluten-free.

places.name: Michinori-tei (みちのり亭)
places.gf_detail: A fully dedicated gluten-free washoku restaurant opened 22 Nov 2024 by the Michinori Bento team (founder 'naco' has a severe wheat allergy), and it is DISTINCT from the takeaway bento shop. The venue's own claim is 「小麦はもちろん、大麦、ライ麦、オーツ麦など、グルテンを含む穀物を一切使用しない」 (uses absolutely no gluten-containing grains — not just wheat but barley, rye and oats). Corroborating JP coverage reports 「調理器具も完全に分けていて、小麦粉の持ち込みは一切禁止」 — cooking equipment is fully separated and no wheat flour is allowed on premises; breading is rice flour (米粉) and seasoning uses wheat-free tamari, with soy/miso/dashi verified wheat-free. Only caveat: they note some third-party condiment factories may also handle wheat, so extremely sensitive celiacs are advised to confirm the specific item on arrival.
places.vegan_detail: The menu is centered on Nagoya meat and seafood specialties (wagyu sukiyaki, miso katsu, chicken karaage, grilled mackerel, shrimp fry), so it is not a vegan destination. A few plant-based items exist — e.g. yuzu sherbet and some rice/onigiri and side dishes — but fillings and dashi should be checked, as much of the savoury food contains fish, egg or meat.
places.hours_raw: Lunch 11:00–15:00 (L.O. 14:00); dinner 18:00–22:00 (L.O. 21:00). Open daily (official site: no fixed closing days).
places.neighborhood: 中村区椿町 (Tsubaki-cho, Nakamura-ku) — Nagoya Station West exit / Taiko-dori side
places.cuisine: Gluten-free Japanese washoku and Nagoya-meshi set meals (teishoku/gozen)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A rare fully gluten-free sit-down washoku restaurant a 3-minute walk from Nagoya Station's west exit — reproduces Nagoya specialties (miso katsu, tebasaki, ebi fry, karaage) with 米粉 rice-flour breading and wheat-free tamari. Kitchen equipment is fully separated and no wheat flour is permitted on site, and the owner is a wheat-allergy sufferer, making it one of the safest full-meal options in central Nagoya for celiacs. Confirm the day of visit for open hours (schedule can be irregular despite the '定休日なし' listing) and flag any single condiment concern with staff.

chef_bio.background: Michinori-tei is the sit-down restaurant offshoot of the gluten-free takeaway brand 'Michinori Bento' (みちのり弁当), which the owner 'naco' launched in January 2023. naco herself has a severe wheat allergy, which is the founding motivation behind the entire project. The restaurant opened on 22 November 2024 in Tsubaki-cho on the west side of Nagoya Station, operated by parent company Tematojikan Co., Ltd. (株式会社テマトジカン). It was created as a place where people who cannot eat wheat — and their friends and family — can gather 'without hesitation' and share a meal together.
chef_bio.philosophy: To turn Nagoya into a 'gluten-free city' (名古屋をグルテンフリーの街へ) by faithfully recreating beloved local dishes without any gluten grain, so that people with wheat allergy or celiac disease can dine out with peace of mind.
chef_bio.specialty: Gluten-free renditions of Nagoya-meshi — miso katsu, tebasaki, ebi fry, karaage — using rice-flour batter and wheat-free seasoning, served as teishoku set meals.

anecdotes.text: The founder has a severe wheat allergy, and because soy sauce and miso themselves often contain wheat the kitchen carefully selects wheat-free seasonings and rebuilds each recipe with rice flour in place of wheat.
The venue grew out of the Michinori Bento brand founded in January 2023 and opened as a full restaurant on 22 November 2024, as a step toward making Nagoya a gluten-free-friendly city.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Japanese sources (the venue's gf-michinori.jp announcement, the Tematojikan PR Times press release, and JP celiac/food blogs jouhou.nagoya, onimaga.jp and nhkomorebi.com) consistently describe naco as a wheat-allergy sufferer who built Michinori Bento (Jan 2023) then Michinori-tei (Nov 2024) to give Nagoya a safe gluten-free dining home; the PR release stresses that soy sauce/miso often contain wheat and are carefully substituted.

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「調理器具も完全に分けていて、小麦粉の持ち込みは一切禁止」 — cooking equipment is completely separated and bringing wheat flour onto the premises is entirely prohibited.
「小麦はもちろん、大麦、ライ麦、オーツ麦など、グルテンを含む穀物を一切使用しない環境を徹底しています」 — the establishment rigorously maintains an environment free of every gluten grain (wheat, barley, rye, oats).
Caveat noted by coverage: 「一部調味料の製造工場では小麦を扱っている場合があります。重度の小麦アレルギーの方は、来店前にお店へ直接ご確認いただくことをおすすめします」 — some condiment factories may also process wheat, so severe sufferers should confirm directly.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Uses wheat-free tamari soy sauce (グルテンフリー醤油) for marinating and seasoning instead of standard wheat-containing shoyu; seasonings (醤油, 味噌, だし) are verified for wheat content.

vegan_cross_contact.text: No vegan-specific cross-contact protocol is published; the kitchen handles fish, egg and meat, so vegan diners should ask about dashi and fillings.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The owner 'naco' has a severe wheat allergy and personally set up the separated-kitchen, no-wheat-flour operation, so staff understand allergen safety firsthand.
The venue's site states 「厳選された食材と独自の品質管理で、小麦アレルギーの方にも安心してお召し上がりいただけ」 — carefully selected ingredients and proprietary quality management make it safe for those with wheat allergy.

positives.text: Entirely gluten-free menu — every dish, including fried Nagoya specialties, is made without any gluten grain using rice-flour breading.
Full-service sit-down washoku restaurant (28 seats) a 3-minute walk from Nagoya Station west exit — a genuine celiac-safe full-meal option, not just takeaway.

places.name: A double prime (a″／エーダブルプライム)
places.gf_detail: Fully dedicated rice-flour (国産米粉) confectionery — no wheat flour is used or baked anywhere on the premises. The shop states plainly 「こちらで売られている商品すべてがグルテンフリー」(everything sold here is gluten-free) and describes itself as 「米粉を用いた焼き菓子を販売しているグルテンフリー対応」. Run by registered-dietitian sisters, so allergen awareness is high; because every product is rice-flour based there is no wheat cross-contact risk in-house, though standard shared-oven/utensil caveats for a small kitchen apply. Barley/rye are not part of the baking, but confirm any bought-in decorations if extremely sensitive.
places.vegan_detail: Not a vegan shop — many items use dairy and egg (custard-cream choux, cheese terrine, gateau chocolat, butter sablé/diamant). A few plainer bakes may be plant-based, but this is not confirmed; vegan customers should ask which items are egg/dairy-free that day.
places.hours_raw: Wed–Sat 11:00–17:00 (food L.O. 16:30); closed Sun/Mon/Tue. (nagoya-meshi lists 11:30–17:30.) Opened Nov 2023.
places.neighborhood: 名古屋市昭和区北山本町 (Kitayamamotomachi, Showa-ku) — 3 min from Arahata Station
places.cuisine: Rice-flour baked goods & gluten-free confectionery / café
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A rice-flour-only sweets shop and tiny 4-seat café run by registered-dietitian sisters (opened Nov 2023). Every product is gluten-free — scones, sablé, diamant cookies, gateau chocolat, cheese terrine, seasonal cakes. No wheat is used on-site, so it is one of Nagoya's safest celiac sweet stops. No phone; PayPay/QR payment (no credit cards). Only 4 eat-in seats, so it's mostly takeout.

chef_bio.background: a″ is run by a pair of sisters: the elder sister is a registered dietitian (管理栄養士) who develops and bakes all the confectionery, while the younger sister handles packaging, branding and the shop's design. They opened the shop in Kitayamamotomachi, Showa-ku in November 2023, about a 3-minute walk from Arahata Station on the Tsurumai Line. The dietitian background shapes the whole menu around domestic rice flour (国産米粉) instead of wheat. The white-and-pale-blue interior and cute packaging reflect the design sister's hand.
chef_bio.philosophy: 「頑張りすぎずちょっと身体に良いおやつ」— everyday treats that are a little kinder to the body without trying too hard; gluten-free by design rather than as a special request.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour scones, sablé and diamant cookies, gateau chocolat and seasonal cheese terrines — all gluten-free.

anecdotes.text: The elder sister is a registered dietitian who makes every product, and the younger sister designs the packaging and store — a dietitian-plus-designer sister team.
Opened November 2023 as a rice-flour confectionery café/bakery with just four eat-in seats near Arahata subway station.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: nagoya-meshi.com describes a sister-run gluten-free 米粉 baked-goods shop where 『こちらで売られている商品すべてがグルテンフリー』, one sister a 管理栄養士. Tabelog confirms address, Nov-2023 opening, PayPay-only, 4 seats.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All products are rice-flour based and 『こちらで売られている商品すべてがグルテンフリー』; no wheat flour is used on the premises, so there is no in-house wheat cross-contact source.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Not applicable — this is a sweets/baked-goods shop; no soy-sauce-based savory dishes.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy and egg are used in many items (custard, cheese terrine, butter cookies); not a vegan kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The baker is a registered dietitian (管理栄養士), and the shop is built entirely around gluten-free rice-flour baking.

positives.text: 100% rice-flour, fully gluten-free product line — one of the safest celiac sweet stops in Nagoya.
Also sold at Social Tower Market pop-ups as 『米粉のスコーン・焼き菓子』.

places.name: Gluten Free BITTE (グルテンフリーBITTE)
places.gf_detail: Dedicated gluten-free rice-flour bakery in Owariasahi. The shop describes itself in its own words as 「小麦不使用・完全グルテンフリーのお店」 (a wheat-free, completely gluten-free shop) with 「コンタミネーションを徹底管理した」 (cross-contamination thoroughly controlled). No wheat products are baked on the premises and the owner, who has gluten intolerance (グルテン不耐症), founded the shop after her own symptoms cleared on rice-flour bread. Products carry per-item labelling for wheat, egg and dairy; because it is a home-scale operation confirm your celiac needs when reserving.
places.vegan_detail: Not a vegan shop. Every item is labelled for 小麦・卵・乳製品 (wheat, egg, dairy) use, so some rice-flour breads appear to be egg- and dairy-free, but the chiffon cakes use eggs. Vegan status is not explicitly advertised — confirm ingredient-by-ingredient when reserving.
places.hours_raw: Reservation-only, irregular monthly operation (that month's operating days and menu announced via official LINE / Instagram). Order via official LINE or Instagram DM.
places.neighborhood: 尾張旭市 (Owariasahi), Aichi — Nagoya area
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] Owariasahi rice-flour bakery run reservation-only by an owner with gluten intolerance; the whole facility is wheat-free (「小麦不使用・完全グルテンフリー」, contamination controlled), so it is celiac-oriented rather than a wheat bakery with a GF line. There is no cafe seating — you reserve breads and chiffon cakes via LINE/Instagram for pickup. Order ahead; each item is labelled for wheat/egg/dairy so you can screen for your needs.

chef_bio.background: The owner spent decades working in store design and visual display for domestic and international brands (「店主さんが元々店舗デザインのお仕事を何十年もされてきた方」). During that busy career her diet leaned heavily on bread and noodles. About ten years ago she developed mysterious rashes across her face and, after seeing several hospitals, was diagnosed with gluten intolerance (グルテン不耐症). Unable to find rice-flour bread she liked, she decided 「こうなったら自分で作るしかない」 (there was nothing for it but to make it myself) and, after years of trials, opened BITTE to share it.
chef_bio.philosophy: Bread had been her greatest daily pleasure (「パンが生活の一番の楽しみなのに」); wanting others with the same condition to enjoy bread again, she resolved 「私と同じような方がいたら、このパンを届けたい」 (if there are people like me, I want to deliver this bread to them). The shop name BITTE is German for a warm 'please/come again', reflecting time she spent living in Germany.
chef_bio.specialty: Gluten-free rice-flour bread (about 19 varieties incl. 米粉食パン) and chiffon cakes

anecdotes.text: Her family came to prefer her rice-flour bread over wheat bread, and her own rashes stopped once she switched.
The name BITTE (German for 'please/you're welcome, come again') was chosen from her experience living in Germany, moved by the warmth of the word from bakery staff.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: bitte2023.com (official site + /about origin story), komeko-palette.com curated guide listing, and a note.com case study by the shop's web designer describing the launch and per-item wheat/egg/dairy labelling.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shop states it is 「小麦不使用・完全グルテンフリーのお店」 with 「コンタミネーションを徹底管理した」 — wheat-free with contamination strictly managed; no wheat baked on site.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Bakery (breads and chiffon cakes) — soy sauce not a factor in the product line.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Eggs and dairy are used in some items (e.g. chiffon cakes); each product is labelled for 小麦・卵・乳製品 so vegans can screen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Owner-run by a person with gluten intolerance; reservation-only so needs can be discussed when ordering via LINE/Instagram.

positives.text: Entire facility is wheat-free/GF (no wheat baked on premises), founded and run by a celiac-adjacent (gluten-intolerant) owner.
Per-item allergen labelling for wheat, egg and dairy.

places.name: Kashiya Fujinomiya (菓子屋 藤ノ宮)
places.gf_detail: A 1938-founded confectionery (フジタセイカ株式会社) that in 2022 converted its entire factory to wheat-free production. The company states outright: 「2022年夏の動物ヨーチ製造終了に伴い、弊社では完全に小麦を使わないグルテンフリー食品製造を行うことにいたしました」 (upon ending 動物ヨーチ — its one wheat biscuit — in summer 2022, it fully switched to wheat-free GF manufacturing), and 「小麦を使わないおいしいお菓子とパンを作っています」. Because the only wheat product line was discontinued and the whole premises now produces exclusively wheat-free goods (100% 米粉 bread and 白あん sweets), there is no on-site wheat handling and cross-contact risk is very low. Curated JP celiac guide empacede rates it グルテンフリー ★★★★★; note the shop uses legacy shared confectionery equipment that predates the 2022 wheat wind-down, so highly sensitive celiacs may still wish to confirm.
places.vegan_detail: Most items are not vegan: the signature 栗せん crackers use egg and white-bean paste, and the 白あんけいく / 白あんてりいぬ cakes contain egg. The plain 米粉100%食パン (rice flour, sugar, salt, yeast, vegetable oil) appears egg- and dairy-free, so a plant-based option exists — confirm the specific loaf, as flavored breads (cheese, etc.) are not vegan.
places.hours_raw: Mon, Fri, Sat, Sun 11:00–19:00 / closed Tue, Wed, Thu
places.neighborhood: 名古屋市中村区千原町 (Nakamura-ku, Nagoya)
places.cuisine: Gluten-free confectionery and rice-flour bakery (Japanese-Western blend sweets)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] Antenna shop of Fujita Seika, a 1938 Nagoya senbei maker that in summer 2022 ended its only wheat product and turned the whole factory wheat-free. Everything sold is gluten-free (100% rice-flour bread and white-bean sweets), so the shop is celiac-appropriate with no wheat on premises; sweets contain egg so it is not a vegan destination. Open only Mon/Fri/Sat/Sun 11–19, closed Tue–Thu; ~3 min walk from Meitetsu Sakō (栄生) station, parking available, cards/e-money/QR accepted.

chef_bio.background: Fujita Seika Co., Ltd. (フジタセイカ株式会社) was founded on 8 December 1938 (昭和13年) when first-generation 藤田四郎 began making confectionery in Nakamura-ku, Nagoya. From 1978 (昭和53年) the second-generation and current president 藤田吉彦 began producing the shop's signature 栗せん — a wheat-free rice cracker made with white-bean paste and egg. Alongside 栗せん the factory long produced 動物ヨーチ (small animal biscuits, a wheat product); rising wheat prices and aging equipment led the company to end 動物ヨーチ in summer 2022 and pivot the entire operation to gluten-free manufacturing. The retail shop 菓子屋 藤ノ宮 opened on 23 September 2022 as the factory's direct-sales outlet.
chef_bio.philosophy: 「小麦を使わないおいしいお菓子とパンを作っています」 — to make genuinely delicious sweets and bread entirely without wheat, carrying a legacy senbei craft into a fully gluten-free future.
chef_bio.specialty: 栗せん (wheat-free chestnut senbei with white-bean paste) and 100% rice-flour bread & 和洋折衷 sweets.

anecdotes.text: The 栗せん recipe has been made for over 40 years and was always wheat-free, using white-bean paste and egg rather than flour — 「４０年以上作り続けていた栗せんべい」.
The full conversion to gluten-free came when the company discontinued its wheat 動物ヨーチ line in summer 2022: 「2022年夏の動物ヨーチ製造終了に伴い、弊社では完全に小麦を使わないグルテンフリー食品製造を行うことにいたしました」.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 菓子屋藤ノ宮公式サイト・会社概要ページに創業（昭和13年、藤田四郎）、栗せんの歴史（昭和53年〜、藤田吉彦）、2022年夏の動物ヨーチ製造終了と完全グルテンフリー化、直営店の2022年9月23日開店が明記。フジタセイカ本体サイト、食べログ、地元ブログ（oinagoya.com）で住所・商品を確認。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Company states the entire factory switched to fully wheat-free production in summer 2022: 「完全に小麦を使わないグルテンフリー食品製造を行うことにいたしました」 — no wheat product line remains on premises, so cross-contact risk is very low.

The factory used shared confectionery/senbei equipment before the 2022 wheat wind-down; no separate certification or explicit allergen-testing protocol is published, so extremely sensitive celiacs may confirm the equipment cleaning history.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Sweets/bakery only; no soy-sauce-based dishes served. Sweets use white-bean paste (白あん) and rice flour, not soy sauce.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Egg is used in the 栗せん and 白あん cakes and is handled in the same kitchen, so shared equipment may contact egg; the plain rice-flour bread appears egg/dairy-free but is produced in the same facility.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The shop is expressly built around wheat-free customers (celiac / wheat allergy) and its whole raison d'être is gluten-free confectionery, indicating high staff awareness of wheat avoidance.

positives.text: Curated Japanese celiac guide empacede rates it グルテンフリー ★★★★★, describing it as carrying no wheat products with no cross-contamination risk.
100% rice-flour bread and a legacy wheat-free senbei; the entire premises is dedicated to gluten-free output.

places.name: Kagome (香米豆ーkagomeー)
places.gf_detail: A 100% gluten-free rice-flour (米粉) bakery that handles no wheat at all. The shop states 「小麦・乳製品・卵（・白砂糖）を使用しないグルテンフリーのパン屋です。当店の商品はすべて小麦・乳製品・卵不使用です」 (a gluten-free bakery using no wheat, dairy, or egg — all our products use none of these), and the JP celiac guide glutenfree.empacede.co.jp lists it ★★★★★ with 「グルテンフリー（小麦の取り扱いなし）」 — no wheat is brought onto the premises, so there is no in-house cross-contact source. Because the entire facility is wheat-free (loaves, bagels, focaccia all rice/oat-based), this is a genuinely dedicated environment. The only residual caution is shared oat products (naturally GF oats but confirm if you are oat-sensitive) and general shared-equipment nuance for extreme sensitivity.
places.vegan_detail: Every product is also dairy-free and egg-free (卵・乳製品不使用), and sweetening is done only with plant sources — きび砂糖 (cane sugar), 甜菜糖 (beet sugar) and メープルシロップ (maple syrup). No animal products are used across the range, so the bread, bagels and focaccia are effectively fully vegan. Honey is not listed; confirm individual seasonal baked sweets if strictly avoiding it.
places.hours_raw: 7:30–13:30 (or until sold out); open Fri–Sun on the 2nd and 4th weekends of each month only. Morning eat-in set from 8:00.
places.neighborhood: 豊橋市杉山町 (Sugiyama-cho, Toyohashi City, Aichi / Nagoya area)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A fully wheat-free, dairy-free and egg-free rice-flour bakery — safe for celiacs since no wheat is handled on site (「小麦の取り扱いなし」). Everything from the shokupan loaves to bagels and focaccia is GF; nothing to avoid on the shelf. Note the very limited schedule: open only Fri–Sun on the 2nd and 4th weekends of the month, mornings only, and popular items sell out shortly after the 7:30 open, so go early and/or reserve via Instagram or text.

chef_bio.origin: 豊橋市, 愛知県 (Toyohashi, Aichi, Japan)
chef_bio.background: Kagome is a small husband-and-wife bakery in a residential corner of Sugiyama-cho, Toyohashi. The wife bakes the breads while the husband runs the shopfront and its small attached gallery, which hosts rotating art exhibitions. The bakery was built specifically around allergen-free baking — using no wheat, dairy or egg — to serve people with allergies and those seeking gluten-free food.
chef_bio.philosophy: To make gluten-free, allergen-friendly bread (no wheat/dairy/egg/refined white sugar) that is still genuinely delicious — famous locally for a 'surprisingly mochi-like' (驚くほどもっちり) rice-flour crumb — so that people with allergies can enjoy bakery bread safely.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour shokupan (米粉食パン) leavened with Shirakami-kodama yeast (白神こだま酵母), plus rice-flour bagels, focaccia and oatmeal breads.

anecdotes.text: The mochi-textured rice-flour bread is so popular it becomes scarce shortly after opening (オープン直後に品薄になってしまうほど人気).
A morning eat-in set from 8:00 pairs warm rice-flour bread with a drink, potage soup and a dessert.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト bakerykagome.net によれば「小麦・乳製品・卵（・白砂糖）を使用しないグルテンフリーのパン屋」で、白神こだま酵母を使った米粉食パンが中心、店内にギャラリー併設。号外NET 豊橋・田原の記事は夫婦経営で妻がパンを焼き夫が店を切り盛りする点と、もっちり食感の人気ぶりを伝える。グルテンフリーガイド（empacede）は小麦の取り扱いなしと評価。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「グルテンフリー（小麦の取り扱いなし）」 — the shop handles no wheat on the premises, so there is no in-house wheat cross-contact source.
「当店の商品はすべて小麦・乳製品・卵不使用です」 — every product is made without wheat, dairy or egg.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Not applicable — bakery; no soy-sauce-based dishes served.

vegan_cross_contact.text: No dairy or egg is used anywhere in the facility, so vegan cross-contact from within the kitchen is effectively nil; sweeteners are plant-based (kibi/beet sugar, maple syrup).

staff_allergy_handling.text: The entire shop is purpose-built as an allergen-free (wheat/dairy/egg) bakery, so allergen avoidance is the default operating model rather than a special request.

positives.text: Rated ★★★★★ on the JP gluten-free guide with the note 「小麦の取り扱いなし」 (no wheat handled).
All products are simultaneously gluten-free, dairy-free and egg-free — fully suitable for celiac and vegan diners.

places.name: Bakeshop SolSol (グルテンフリー米粉パン専門店 Bakeshop SolSol)
places.gf_detail: A dedicated gluten-free rice-flour bakery with no wheat on the premises. The shop states 「小麦パンと厨房を分けるために、専門店として独立開業しました」(we opened independently as a specialty shop specifically to keep our kitchen separate from wheat bread) and 「小麦粉アレルギーがある方でも安心して召し上がっていただけます」(safe for those with wheat-flour allergies to eat with peace of mind). The Japanese celiac guide glutenfree.empacede.co.jp rates it ★★★★★ and confirms 「全ての商品に小麦を使っていません」(none of the products use wheat). Because it is 100% wheat-free with a purpose-built separate kitchen, cross-contact risk is minimal, though seasoned buyers should still confirm any bought-in toppings.
places.vegan_detail: Not a vegan bakery. Many items are dairy/egg-rich (cream bread, chocolate breads, custard, waffles), while some plain rice-flour loaves may be plant-based. No vegan labelling is published, so ask per item.
places.hours_raw: Weekdays 11:00–17:00 / Weekends & holidays 11:00–18:00, closed Mon & Tue. May close early once the day's bread sells out.
places.neighborhood: 碧南市三宅町 (Miyakecho, Hekinan City), Aichi / Nagoya area
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] 100% wheat-free rice-flour bakery — the owner opened it as a standalone shop expressly to separate the kitchen from any wheat baking, so the whole product line is celiac-oriented. Its 花酵母のカンパーニュ (flower-yeast campagne) swept the 2024 2nd Delicious Rice Flour Bread Contest (Grand Prix + Gluten-Free Award). Go early: they bake a fixed batch and close when sold out. Closed Mon & Tue.

chef_bio.background: Katayama Yukihiro founded Bakeshop SolSol in Hekinan City after his nephew was diagnosed with a wheat allergy and could no longer eat ordinary wheat bread. Rather than baking gluten-free bread alongside wheat, he deliberately opened as a standalone gluten-free specialty shop so the kitchen would never share equipment or airborne flour with wheat products. Drawing on more than 15 years of bread-making, he works exclusively with rice flour (including Koshihikari) and his own SolSol rice-flour blends. In 2024 his 花酵母のカンパーニュ (flower-yeast campagne) took the Grand Prix, the Gluten-Free Award and the consumer-voted 'My Rice Flour Bread' award — a triple crown — at the 2nd Delicious Rice Flour Bread Contest in Yamagata.
chef_bio.philosophy: Make bread that people who cannot eat wheat can enjoy without compromise (小麦が食べられない人でも我慢せずに食べられるパン).
chef_bio.specialty: Flower-yeast rice-flour campagne (made with rare red-carnation flower yeast) and rice-flour breads/sweets, all wheat-free.

anecdotes.text: Opened as an independent specialty shop specifically to keep the kitchen separate from wheat bread; the origin was his nephew's wheat allergy.
花酵母のカンパーニュ won the Grand Prix (plus Gluten-Free Award and consumer 'My Rice Flour Bread' award) at the 2nd Delicious Rice Flour Bread Contest (2024), chosen from 111 entries as Japan's #1.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: solsol-gf.com (homepage, store, lineup) states it is a グルテンフリー米粉パン専門店 that separated its kitchen from wheat and that 花酵母のカンパーニュ won グランプリ at the 2nd contest; 中日新聞 (chunichi.co.jp) confirms the 全国3冠 (Grand Prix + Gluten-Free Award + My Rice Flour Bread) and that the origin was owner Katayama's nephew's wheat allergy; empacede rates it with no wheat handling.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Dedicated gluten-free premises — opened as a standalone shop expressly to keep the kitchen separate from wheat bread (「小麦パンと厨房を分けるために、専門店として独立開業しました」).
「全ての商品に小麦を使っていません」— none of the products use wheat (JP celiac guide, ★★★★★).

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Bakery — soy sauce is not a core ingredient; wheat is excluded from the shop entirely.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Not a vegan operation; dairy and egg are used in many items. No vegan cross-contact protocol published.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Founded around a family member's wheat allergy; the whole business model is wheat-free allergy safety (「小麦粉アレルギーがある方でも安心して召し上がっていただけます」).

positives.text: 100% wheat-free rice-flour bakery with a purpose-built separate kitchen.
★★★★★ on the Japanese gluten-free guide; award-winning gluten-free campagne.

places.name: Vegedeco Salad Lab (ベジデコサラダラボ)
places.gf_detail: A gluten-free takeout studio run by 株式会社Mitsuki Style specialising in decoration-cake-style vegetable salads (ベジデコサラダ®) and low-sugar sweets. The shop states it replaces wheat flour entirely: 「小麦粉の代わりに大豆粉や米粉を使用」 (soy flour / rice flour instead of wheat), with tofu-based creams and 甘麹 (fermented rice koji) for sweetness rather than sugar. Every item on the menu is made without wheat, so the whole product line is gluten-free; however no explicit celiac-grade cross-contact protocol (shared bowls/utensils) is published, so a strict celiac should confirm handling. Note the 大豆粉 (soy flour) used is naturally gluten-free — not a wheat gluten trap.
places.vegan_detail: Several items are fully plant-based — the ヴィーガンチーズケーキ is egg- and dairy-free, and the salad cakes use tofu cream and 甘麹 sweetening rather than dairy or refined sugar. But not everything is vegan: the さつまいもタルト contains eggs and the honey (ハニー) soufflé variant uses honey. Ask per item.
places.hours_raw: Fri–Sun 11:00–18:00 (closed Mon–Thu). Takeout only.
places.neighborhood: 名古屋市昭和区花見通 (Hanamidōri / Kawana, Showa-ku, Nagoya)
places.notes: [GF] Fully wheat-free takeout studio — the entire salad-cake and sweets line is made with 大豆粉/米粉 instead of wheat flour, so celiacs can eat across the menu. Open only Fri–Sun 11:00–18:00, 5 min from Kawana subway station. No published dedicated-celiac cross-contact protocol, so confirm handling if highly sensitive; call 052-753-7775 to reserve cakes.

chef_bio.background: 森安美月 (Mitsuki Moriyasu) is a self-described Food Style Designer and representative of 株式会社Mitsuki Style, which created the VegedecoSalad® concept — non-sugar, gluten-free vegetable arrangements built to look like decoration cakes. The company also runs gluten-free menu consulting, and opened the Vegedeco Salad Lab takeout shop in Showa-ku, Nagoya (opened 15 July 2018). The concept originated from a French restaurant, La Porte Marseille, in Sakae (now closed).
chef_bio.philosophy: Food that makes both mind and body healthy — vegetables enjoyed beautifully, using genuine seasonings, functional ingredients, no refined sugar and no wheat.
chef_bio.specialty: ベジデコサラダ® (decoration-cake-style vegetable salads) and gluten-free, low-sugar sweets

anecdotes.text: Featured internationally: CNN 'FOOD SNAP JAPAN' (2017) reaching 200+ countries, and the BBC Travel Show (2018) named Vegedeco Salad Lab one of Nagoya's top three attractions alongside Nagoya Castle and the Science Museum.
Also covered by German TV 'Galileo' (2016), Hawaii's KHON2 Living808 (2018), plus Teen Vogue, Marie Claire and National Geographic Traveler.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト・食べログ・名古屋情報通によれば、株式会社Mitsuki Styleの森安美月が運営するグルテンフリー専門のテイクアウト店。小麦粉不使用で大豆粉・米粉を使い、CNN・BBC・独Galileo・ハワイKHON2など海外メディアに多数掲載。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shop replaces wheat flour entirely with 大豆粉 (soy flour) and 米粉 (rice flour); the full product line is wheat-free, but no explicit celiac cross-contact protocol is published.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Sweets/salad-cake shop — soy sauce is not a primary ingredient; sweetening is via 甘麹 (fermented rice koji), not sugar or wheat-based seasoning.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Mixed line — some items use eggs (さつまいもタルト) and honey (soufflé), so vegan and non-vegan items are produced in the same kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No specific allergy-handling statement published; company does gluten-free menu consulting, suggesting allergen awareness. Confirm by phone (052-753-7775).

positives.text: Entire menu is wheat-free using soy/rice flour; internationally recognised gluten-free specialist (CNN, BBC).

places.name: Ve Tree (ヴィーツリー)
places.gf_detail: Dedicated vegan & gluten-free sweets shop where the entire product line is wheat-free — the shop's own tagline is 「乳卵小麦不使用」 (no dairy, egg or wheat used) and it states 「白砂糖不使用」 too. Cakes and焼き菓子 are built on 米粉 (rice flour) and 大豆粉 rather than wheat, and the shop describes everything as ヴィーガン＆グルテンフリー. Because no wheat is used anywhere on the premises, cross-contact risk is very low; a small caveat is that the same kitchen also runs cooking classes, though those are also wheat-free. Some items are additionally nut-free (※一部ナッツフリー商品).
places.vegan_detail: 100% plant-based sweets specialist — no dairy, eggs, or animal products; uses rice flour, soy flour and unrefined sweeteners in place of butter, eggs and white sugar. A 2025 addition is a vegan 米粉バーガー (rice-flour burger).
places.hours_raw: Saturdays only, 10:00–17:00 (retail storefront). Whole cakes by 1-week-advance reservation, pickup Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri/Sat.
places.neighborhood: 名古屋市北区鳩岡 (Hatooka, Kita-ku, along 国道41号 / Route 41, near Kurokawa)
places.cuisine: Vegan & gluten-free cakes, baked sweets and cookies (rice-flour / soy-flour)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] Fully wheat-free vegan sweets shop — every cake and cookie is safe for celiacs since no wheat is used on premises. Retail is open Saturdays only (10:00–17:00); whole cakes must be reserved ~1 week ahead. Great for a safe dessert stop or gift box, but not a meal venue (sweets, plus a newer vegan rice-flour burger).

chef_bio.background: Owner-representative 野口亜希子 founded the parent business VegeBio (ベジビーオ) as a healthy cooking studio on 2014-07-01, near Kurokawa station in Nagoya's Kita-ku. The name VegeBio combines 'Vegetable' and 'Biology,' reflecting her philosophy of building energetic, healthy daily life through vegetables and fruit. In 2017 the business expanded into retail and online sales, and after repeated customer requests to buy the cakes and baked goods from her classes, she opened the Ve Tree storefront (2022-01-11) as a dedicated vegan & gluten-free cake shop. The name 'Ve Tree' joins 'Ve' (vegan) with 'Tree,' symbolizing buds forming and blossoms appearing — growth.
chef_bio.philosophy: That truly delicious food should also satisfy the body's needs and be sustainable; sweets are positioned as 'reward treats' (ご褒美) that enrich life and serve as an entry point for customers to reconsider their broader eating habits.
chef_bio.specialty: Vegan & gluten-free cakes and baked sweets made with rice flour and soy flour, free of dairy, egg, wheat and white sugar.

anecdotes.text: The storefront was opened specifically in response to customers of her cooking classes asking to buy the cakes and baked goods themselves.
Media-noted as a rarity in Nagoya: a sweets shop where every single item is both vegan and gluten-free.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 会社概要・ショップ情報ページより：代表者は野口亜希子、2014年にヘルシー料理教室『ベジビーオ』として創業（北区鳩岡）、2022年に乳卵小麦・白砂糖不使用のヴィーガン＆グルテンフリーケーキ専門店『Ve Tree』をオープン。すべての商品が乳卵小麦不使用。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No wheat is used anywhere in the shop — the entire product range is 乳卵小麦不使用 (no dairy/egg/wheat), so cross-contact with wheat flour is essentially absent.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Sweets shop — no soy-sauce dishes; wheat-based seasonings are not part of the product line.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fully vegan facility — no dairy, eggs or animal products used, so no animal-product cross-contact.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Owner is a cooking instructor specializing in allergen-free (乳卵小麦不使用) baking; some items are additionally labeled nut-free (※一部ナッツフリー商品).

positives.text: Every product is both vegan and gluten-free — no wheat, dairy, egg or white sugar used at all.
Uses 米粉 (rice flour) and 大豆粉 (soy flour) as the base for cakes and baked goods.
